Output
"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\\n2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\\n3: \\n4: package terraform\\n5: \\n6: import (\\n7: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n8: \\t\\\"log\\\"\\n9: \\n10: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\\\"\\n11: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\\\"\\n12: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\\\"\\n13: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\\\"\\n14: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang/langrefs\\\"\\n15: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\\\"\\n16: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\\\"\\n17: )\\n18: \\n19: // ConcreteResourceNodeFunc is a callback type used to convert an\\n20: // abstract resource to a concrete one of some type.\\n21: type ConcreteResourceNodeFunc func(*NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex\\n22: \\n23: // GraphNodeConfigResource is implemented by any nodes that represent a resource.\\n24: // The type of operation cannot be assumed, only that this node represents\\n25: // the given resource.\\n26: type GraphNodeConfigResource interface {\\n27: \\tResourceAddr() addrs.ConfigResource\\n28: }\\n29: \\n30: // ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc is a callback type used to convert an\\n31: // abstract resource instance to a concrete one of some type.\\n32: type ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc func(*NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex\\n33: \\n34: // GraphNodeResourceInstance is implemented by any nodes that represent\\n35: // a resource instance. A single resource may have multiple instances if,\\n36: // for example, the \\\"count\\\" or \\\"for_each\\\" argument is used for it in\\n37: // configuration.\\n38: type GraphNodeResourceInstance interface {\\n39: \\tResourceInstanceAddr() addrs.AbsResourceInstance\\n40: \\n41: \\t// StateDependencies returns any inter-resource dependencies that are\\n42: \\t// stored in the state.\\n43: \\tStateDependencies() []addrs.ConfigResource\\n44: }\\n45: \\n46: // NodeAbstractResource represents a resource that has no associated\\n47: // operations. It registers all the interfaces for a resource that common\\n48: // across multiple operation types.\\n49: type NodeAbstractResource struct {\\n50: \\tAddr addrs.ConfigResource\\n51: \\n52: \\t// The fields below will be automatically set using the Attach\\n53: \\t// interfaces if you're running those transforms, but also be explicitly\\n54: \\t// set if you already have that information.\\n55: \\n56: \\tSchema        *configschema.Block // Schema for processing the configuration body\\n57: \\tSchemaVersion uint64              // Schema version of \\\"Schema\\\", as decided by the provider\\n58: \\n59: \\t// Config and RemovedConfig are mutally-exclusive, because a\\n60: \\t// resource can't be both declared and removed at the same time.\\n61: \\tConfig        *configs.Resource // Config is the resource in the config, if any\\n62: \\tRemovedConfig *configs.Removed  // RemovedConfig is the \\\"removed\\\" block for this resource, if any\\n63: \\n64: \\t// ProviderMetas is the provider_meta configs for the module this resource belongs to\\n65: \\tProviderMetas map[addrs.Provider]*configs.ProviderMeta\\n66: \\n67: \\tProvisionerSchemas map[string]*configschema.Block\\n68: \\n69: \\t// Set from GraphNodeTargetable\\n70: \\tTargets []addrs.Targetable\\n71: \\n72: \\t// Set from AttachDataResourceDependsOn\\n73: \\tdependsOn []addrs.ConfigResource\\n74: \\n75: \\t// The address of the provider this resource will use\\n76: \\tResolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig\\n77: \\t// storedProviderConfig is the provider address retrieved from the\\n78: \\t// state. This is defined here for access within the ProvidedBy method, but\\n79: \\t// will be set from the embedding instance type when the state is attached.\\n80: \\tstoredProviderConfig addrs.AbsProviderConfig\\n81: \\n82: \\t// This resource may expand into instances which need to be imported.\\n83: \\timportTargets []*ImportTarget\\n84: \\n85: \\t// generateConfigPath tells this node which file to write generated config\\n86: \\t// into. If empty, then config should not be generated.\\n87: \\tgenerateConfigPath string\\n88: \\n89: \\tforceCreateBeforeDestroy bool\\n90: }\\n91: \\n92: var (\\n93: \\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable               = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\\n94: \\t_ GraphNodeReferencer                  = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\\n95: \\t_ GraphNodeImportReferencer            = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\\n96: \\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer            = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\\n97: \\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer         = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\\n98: \\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource              = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\\n99: \\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig        = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\\n100: \\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceSchema        = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\\n101: \\t_ GraphNodeAttachProvisionerSchema     = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\\n102: \\t_ GraphNodeAttachProviderMetaConfigs   = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\\n103: \\t_ GraphNodeTargetable                  = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\\n104: \\t_ graphNodeAttachDataResourceDependsOn = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\\n105: \\t_ dag.GraphNodeDotter                  = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\\n106: \\t_ GraphNodeDestroyerCBD                = (*NodeAbstractResource)(nil)\\n107: )\\n108: \\n109: // NewNodeAbstractResource creates an abstract resource graph node for\\n110: // the given absolute resource address.\\n111: func NewNodeAbstractResource(addr addrs.ConfigResource) *NodeAbstractResource {\\n112: \\treturn &NodeAbstractResource{\\n113: \\t\\tAddr: addr,\\n114: \\t}\\n115: }\\n116: \\n117: var (\\n118: \\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance            = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\\n119: \\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable             = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\\n120: \\t_ GraphNodeReferencer                = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\\n121: \\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer          = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\\n122: \\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer       = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\\n123: \\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource            = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\\n124: \\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance          = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\\n125: \\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceState       = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\\n126: \\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig      = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\\n127: \\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceSchema      = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\\n128: \\t_ GraphNodeAttachProvisionerSchema   = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\\n129: \\t_ GraphNodeAttachProviderMetaConfigs = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\\n130: \\t_ GraphNodeTargetable                = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\\n131: \\t_ GraphNodeOverridable               = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\\n132: \\t_ dag.GraphNodeDotter                = (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance)(nil)\\n133: )\\n134: \\n135: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) Name() string {\\n136: \\treturn n.ResourceAddr().String()\\n137: }\\n138: \\n139: // GraphNodeModulePath\\n140: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) ModulePath() addrs.Module {\\n141: \\treturn n.Addr.Module\\n142: }\\n143: \\n144: // GraphNodeReferenceable\\n145: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable {\\n146: \\treturn []addrs.Referenceable{n.Addr.Resource}\\n147: }\\n148: \\n149: // CreateBeforeDestroy returns this node's CreateBeforeDestroy status.\\n150: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) CreateBeforeDestroy() bool {\\n151: \\tif n.forceCreateBeforeDestroy {\\n152: \\t\\treturn n.forceCreateBeforeDestroy\\n153: \\t}\\n154: \\n155: \\tif n.Config != nil && n.Config.Managed != nil {\\n156: \\t\\treturn n.Config.Managed.CreateBeforeDestroy\\n157: \\t}\\n158: \\n159: \\treturn false\\n160: }\\n161: \\n162: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) ModifyCreateBeforeDestroy(v bool) error {\\n163: \\tn.forceCreateBeforeDestroy = v\\n164: \\treturn nil\\n165: }\\n166: \\n167: // GraphNodeReferencer\\n168: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) References() []*addrs.Reference {\\n169: \\tvar result []*addrs.Reference\\n170: \\t// If we have a config then we prefer to use that.\\n171: \\tif c := n.Config; c != nil {\\n172: \\t\\tresult = append(result, n.DependsOn()...)\\n173: \\n174: \\t\\tif n.Schema == nil {\\n175: \\t\\t\\t// Should never happen, but we'll log if it does so that we can\\n176: \\t\\t\\t// see this easily when debugging.\\n177: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[WARN] no schema is attached to %s, so config references cannot be detected\\\", n.Name())\\n178: \\t\\t}\\n179: \\n180: \\t\\trefs, _ := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, c.Count)\\n181: \\t\\tresult = append(result, refs...)\\n182: \\t\\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, c.ForEach)\\n183: \\t\\tresult = append(result, refs...)\\n184: \\n185: \\t\\tfor _, expr := range c.TriggersReplacement {\\n186: \\t\\t\\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, expr)\\n187: \\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, refs...)\\n188: \\t\\t}\\n189: \\n190: \\t\\t// ReferencesInBlock() requires a schema\\n191: \\t\\tif n.Schema != nil {\\n192: \\t\\t\\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInBlock(addrs.ParseRef, c.Config, n.Schema)\\n193: \\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, refs...)\\n194: \\t\\t}\\n195: \\n196: \\t\\tif c.Managed != nil {\\n197: \\t\\t\\tif c.Managed.Connection != nil {\\n198: \\t\\t\\t\\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInBlock(addrs.ParseRef, c.Managed.Connection.Config, connectionBlockSupersetSchema)\\n199: \\t\\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, refs...)\\n200: \\t\\t\\t}\\n201: \\n202: \\t\\t\\tfor _, p := range c.Managed.Provisioners {\\n203: \\t\\t\\t\\tif p.When != configs.ProvisionerWhenCreate {\\n204: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n205: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n206: \\t\\t\\t\\tif p.Connection != nil {\\n207: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInBlock(addrs.ParseRef, p.Connection.Config, connectionBlockSupersetSchema)\\n208: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, refs...)\\n209: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n210: \\n211: \\t\\t\\t\\tschema := n.ProvisionerSchemas[p.Type]\\n212: \\t\\t\\t\\tif schema == nil {\\n213: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[WARN] no schema for provisioner %q is attached to %s, so provisioner block references cannot be detected\\\", p.Type, n.Name())\\n214: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n215: \\t\\t\\t\\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInBlock(addrs.ParseRef, p.Config, schema)\\n216: \\t\\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, refs...)\\n217: \\t\\t\\t}\\n218: \\t\\t}\\n219: \\n220: \\t\\tfor _, check := range c.Preconditions {\\n221: \\t\\t\\trefs, _ := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, check.Condition)\\n222: \\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, refs...)\\n223: \\t\\t\\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, check.ErrorMessage)\\n224: \\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, refs...)\\n225: \\t\\t}\\n226: \\t\\tfor _, check := range c.Postconditions {\\n227: \\t\\t\\trefs, _ := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, check.Condition)\\n228: \\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, refs...)\\n229: \\t\\t\\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, check.ErrorMessage)\\n230: \\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, refs...)\\n231: \\t\\t}\\n232: \\t}\\n233: \\n234: \\treturn result\\n235: }\\n236: \\n237: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) ImportReferences() []*addrs.Reference {\\n238: \\tvar result []*addrs.Reference\\n239: \\tfor _, importTarget := range n.importTargets {\\n240: \\t\\t// legacy import won't have any config\\n241: \\t\\tif importTarget.Config == nil {\\n242: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n243: \\t\\t}\\n244: \\n245: \\t\\trefs, _ := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, importTarget.Config.ID)\\n246: \\t\\tresult = append(result, refs...)\\n247: \\t\\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, importTarget.Config.ForEach)\\n248: \\t\\tresult = append(result, refs...)\\n249: \\t}\\n250: \\treturn result\\n251: }\\n252: \\n253: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) DependsOn() []*addrs.Reference {\\n254: \\tvar result []*addrs.Reference\\n255: \\tif c := n.Config; c != nil {\\n256: \\n257: \\t\\tfor _, traversal := range c.DependsOn {\\n258: \\t\\t\\tref, diags := addrs.ParseRef(traversal)\\n259: \\t\\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n260: \\t\\t\\t\\t// We ignore this here, because this isn't a suitable place to return\\n261: \\t\\t\\t\\t// errors. This situation should be caught and rejected during\\n262: \\t\\t\\t\\t// validation.\\n263: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[ERROR] Can't parse %#v from depends_on as reference: %s\\\", traversal, diags.Err())\\n264: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n265: \\t\\t\\t}\\n266: \\n267: \\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, ref)\\n268: \\t\\t}\\n269: \\t}\\n270: \\treturn result\\n271: }\\n272: \\n273: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) SetProvider(p addrs.AbsProviderConfig) {\\n274: \\tn.ResolvedProvider = p\\n275: }\\n276: \\n277: // GraphNodeProviderConsumer\\n278: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) ProvidedBy() (addrs.ProviderConfig, bool) {\\n279: \\t// Once the provider is fully resolved, we can return the known value.\\n280: \\tif n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.Type != \\\"\\\" {\\n281: \\t\\treturn n.ResolvedProvider, true\\n282: \\t}\\n283: \\n284: \\t// If we have a config we prefer that above all else\\n285: \\tif n.Config != nil {\\n286: \\t\\trelAddr := n.Config.ProviderConfigAddr()\\n287: \\t\\treturn addrs.LocalProviderConfig{\\n288: \\t\\t\\tLocalName: relAddr.LocalName,\\n289: \\t\\t\\tAlias:     relAddr.Alias,\\n290: \\t\\t}, false\\n291: \\t}\\n292: \\n293: \\t// See if we have a valid provider config from the state.\\n294: \\tif n.storedProviderConfig.Provider.Type != \\\"\\\" {\\n295: \\t\\t// An address from the state must match exactly, since we must ensure\\n296: \\t\\t// we refresh/destroy a resource with the same provider configuration\\n297: \\t\\t// that created it.\\n298: \\t\\treturn n.storedProviderConfig, true\\n299: \\t}\\n300: \\n301: \\t// We might have an import target that is providing a specific provider,\\n302: \\t// this is okay as we know there is nothing else potentially providing a\\n303: \\t// provider configuration.\\n304: \\tif len(n.importTargets) > 0 {\\n305: \\t\\t// The import targets should either all be defined via config or none\\n306: \\t\\t// of them should be. They should also all have the same provider, so it\\n307: \\t\\t// shouldn't matter which we check here, as they'll all give the same.\\n308: \\t\\tif n.importTargets[0].Config != nil && n.importTargets[0].Config.ProviderConfigRef != nil {\\n309: \\t\\t\\treturn addrs.LocalProviderConfig{\\n310: \\t\\t\\t\\tLocalName: n.importTargets[0].Config.ProviderConfigRef.Name,\\n311: \\t\\t\\t\\tAlias:     n.importTargets[0].Config.ProviderConfigRef.Alias,\\n312: \\t\\t\\t}, false\\n313: \\t\\t}\\n314: \\t}\\n315: \\n316: \\t// No provider configuration found; return a default address\\n317: \\treturn addrs.AbsProviderConfig{\\n318: \\t\\tProvider: n.Provider(),\\n319: \\t\\tModule:   n.ModulePath(),\\n320: \\t}, false\\n321: }\\n322: \\n323: // GraphNodeProviderConsumer\\n324: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) Provider() addrs.Provider {\\n325: \\tif n.Config != nil {\\n326: \\t\\treturn n.Config.Provider\\n327: \\t}\\n328: \\tif n.storedProviderConfig.Provider.Type != \\\"\\\" {\\n329: \\t\\treturn n.storedProviderConfig.Provider\\n330: \\t}\\n331: \\n332: \\tif len(n.importTargets) > 0 {\\n333: \\t\\t// The import targets should either all be defined via config or none\\n334: \\t\\t// of them should be. They should also all have the same provider, so it\\n335: \\t\\t// shouldn't matter which we check here, as they'll all give the same.\\n336: \\t\\tif n.importTargets[0].Config != nil {\\n337: \\t\\t\\treturn n.importTargets[0].Config.Provider\\n338: \\t\\t}\\n339: \\t}\\n340: \\n341: \\treturn addrs.ImpliedProviderForUnqualifiedType(n.Addr.Resource.ImpliedProvider())\\n342: }\\n343: \\n344: // GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer\\n345: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) ProvisionedBy() []string {\\n346: \\t// If we have no configuration, then we have no provisioners\\n347: \\tif n.Config == nil || n.Config.Managed == nil {\\n348: \\t\\treturn nil\\n349: \\t}\\n350: \\n351: \\t// Build the list of provisioners we need based on the configuration.\\n352: \\t// It is okay to have duplicates here.\\n353: \\tresult := make([]string, len(n.Config.Managed.Provisioners))\\n354: \\tfor i, p := range n.Config.Managed.Provisioners {\\n355: \\t\\tresult[i] = p.Type\\n356: \\t}\\n357: \\n358: \\treturn result\\n359: }\\n360: \\n361: // GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer\\n362: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) AttachProvisionerSchema(name string, schema *configschema.Block) {\\n363: \\tif n.ProvisionerSchemas == nil {\\n364: \\t\\tn.ProvisionerSchemas = make(map[string]*configschema.Block)\\n365: \\t}\\n366: \\tn.ProvisionerSchemas[name] = schema\\n367: }\\n368: \\n369: // GraphNodeResource\\n370: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) ResourceAddr() addrs.ConfigResource {\\n371: \\treturn n.Addr\\n372: }\\n373: \\n374: // GraphNodeTargetable\\n375: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) SetTargets(targets []addrs.Targetable) {\\n376: \\tn.Targets = targets\\n377: }\\n378: \\n379: // graphNodeAttachDataResourceDependsOn\\n380: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) AttachDataResourceDependsOn(deps []addrs.ConfigResource) {\\n381: \\tn.dependsOn = deps\\n382: }\\n383: \\n384: // GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig\\n385: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) AttachResourceConfig(c *configs.Resource, rc *configs.Removed) {\\n386: \\tn.Config = c\\n387: \\tn.RemovedConfig = rc\\n388: }\\n389: \\n390: // GraphNodeAttachResourceSchema impl\\n391: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) AttachResourceSchema(schema *configschema.Block, version uint64) {\\n392: \\tn.Schema = schema\\n393: \\tn.SchemaVersion = version\\n394: }\\n395: \\n396: // GraphNodeAttachProviderMetaConfigs impl\\n397: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) AttachProviderMetaConfigs(c map[addrs.Provider]*configs.ProviderMeta) {\\n398: \\tn.ProviderMetas = c\\n399: }\\n400: \\n401: // GraphNodeDotter impl.\\n402: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) DotNode(name string, opts *dag.DotOpts) *dag.DotNode {\\n403: \\treturn &dag.DotNode{\\n404: \\t\\tName: name,\\n405: \\t\\tAttrs: map[string]string{\\n406: \\t\\t\\t\\\"label\\\": n.Name(),\\n407: \\t\\t\\t\\\"shape\\\": \\\"box\\\",\\n408: \\t\\t},\\n409: \\t}\\n410: }\\n411: \\n412: // recordResourceData records some metadata for the resource as a whole in\\n413: // various locations. This currently includes adding resource expansion info to\\n414: // the instance expander, and recording the provider used in the state.\\n415: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) recordResourceData(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResource) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n416: \\n417: \\t// We'll record our expansion decision in the shared \\\"expander\\\" object\\n418: \\t// so that later operations (i.e. DynamicExpand and expression evaluation)\\n419: \\t// can refer to it. Since this node represents the abstract module, we need\\n420: \\t// to expand the module here to create all resources.\\n421: \\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\\n422: \\n423: \\t// Allowing unknown values in count and for_each is a top-level plan option.\\n424: \\t//\\n425: \\t// If this is false then the codepaths that handle unknown values below\\n426: \\t// become unreachable, because the evaluate functions will reject unknown\\n427: \\t// values as an error.\\n428: \\tallowUnknown := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\\n429: \\n430: \\tswitch {\\n431: \\tcase n.Config != nil && n.Config.Count != nil:\\n432: \\t\\tcount, countDiags := evaluateCountExpression(n.Config.Count, ctx, allowUnknown)\\n433: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(countDiags)\\n434: \\t\\tif countDiags.HasErrors() {\\n435: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n436: \\t\\t}\\n437: \\n438: \\t\\tif count >= 0 {\\n439: \\t\\t\\texpander.SetResourceCount(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource, count)\\n440: \\t\\t} else {\\n441: \\t\\t\\t// -1 represents \\\"unknown\\\"\\n442: \\t\\t\\texpander.SetResourceCountUnknown(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource)\\n443: \\t\\t}\\n444: \\n445: \\tcase n.Config != nil && n.Config.ForEach != nil:\\n446: \\t\\tforEach, known, forEachDiags := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, allowUnknown)\\n447: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(forEachDiags)\\n448: \\t\\tif forEachDiags.HasErrors() {\\n449: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n450: \\t\\t}\\n451: \\n452: \\t\\t// This method takes care of all of the business logic of updating this\\n453: \\t\\t// while ensuring that any existing instances are preserved, etc.\\n454: \\t\\tif known {\\n455: \\t\\t\\texpander.SetResourceForEach(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource, forEach)\\n456: \\t\\t} else {\\n457: \\t\\t\\texpander.SetResourceForEachUnknown(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource)\\n458: \\t\\t}\\n459: \\n460: \\tdefault:\\n461: \\t\\texpander.SetResourceSingle(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource)\\n462: \\t}\\n463: \\n464: \\tif addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\\n465: \\t\\t// ephemeral resources are not included in the state\\n466: \\t\\treturn diags\\n467: \\t}\\n468: \\n469: \\tstate := ctx.State()\\n470: \\tstate.SetResourceProvider(addr, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n471: \\n472: \\treturn diags\\n473: }\\n474: \\n475: // readResourceInstanceState reads the current object for a specific instance in\\n476: // the state.\\n477: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) readResourceInstanceState(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n478: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n479: \\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n480: \\tif err != nil {\\n481: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n482: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n483: \\t}\\n484: \\n485: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] readResourceInstanceState: reading state for %s\\\", addr)\\n486: \\n487: \\tsrc := ctx.State().ResourceInstanceObject(addr, addrs.NotDeposed)\\n488: \\tif src == nil {\\n489: \\t\\t// Presumably we only have deposed objects, then.\\n490: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] readResourceInstanceState: no state present for %s\\\", addr)\\n491: \\t\\treturn nil, nil\\n492: \\t}\\n493: \\n494: \\tschema, currentVersion := (providerSchema).SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.Resource.ContainingResource())\\n495: \\tif schema == nil {\\n496: \\t\\t// Shouldn't happen since we should've failed long ago if no schema is present\\n497: \\t\\treturn nil, diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"no schema available for %s while reading state; this is a bug in Terraform and should be reported\\\", addr))\\n498: \\t}\\n499: \\tsrc, upgradeDiags := upgradeResourceState(addr, provider, src, schema, currentVersion)\\n500: \\tif n.Config != nil {\\n501: \\t\\tupgradeDiags = upgradeDiags.InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, addr.String())\\n502: \\t}\\n503: \\tdiags = diags.Append(upgradeDiags)\\n504: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n505: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n506: \\t}\\n507: \\n508: \\tobj, err := src.Decode(schema.ImpliedType())\\n509: \\tif err != nil {\\n510: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n511: \\t}\\n512: \\n513: \\treturn obj, diags\\n514: }\\n515: \\n516: // readResourceInstanceStateDeposed reads the deposed object for a specific\\n517: // instance in the state.\\n518: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) readResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, key states.DeposedKey) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n519: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n520: \\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n521: \\tif err != nil {\\n522: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n523: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n524: \\t}\\n525: \\n526: \\tif key == states.NotDeposed {\\n527: \\t\\treturn nil, diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"readResourceInstanceStateDeposed used with no instance key; this is a bug in Terraform and should be reported\\\"))\\n528: \\t}\\n529: \\n530: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] readResourceInstanceStateDeposed: reading state for %s deposed object %s\\\", addr, key)\\n531: \\n532: \\tsrc := ctx.State().ResourceInstanceObject(addr, key)\\n533: \\tif src == nil {\\n534: \\t\\t// Presumably we only have deposed objects, then.\\n535: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] readResourceInstanceStateDeposed: no state present for %s deposed object %s\\\", addr, key)\\n536: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n537: \\t}\\n538: \\n539: \\tschema, currentVersion := (providerSchema).SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.Resource.ContainingResource())\\n540: \\tif schema == nil {\\n541: \\t\\t// Shouldn't happen since we should've failed long ago if no schema is present\\n542: \\t\\treturn nil, diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"no schema available for %s while reading state; this is a bug in Terraform and should be reported\\\", addr))\\n543: \\n544: \\t}\\n545: \\n546: \\tsrc, upgradeDiags := upgradeResourceState(addr, provider, src, schema, currentVersion)\\n547: \\tif n.Config != nil {\\n548: \\t\\tupgradeDiags = upgradeDiags.InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, addr.String())\\n549: \\t}\\n550: \\tdiags = diags.Append(upgradeDiags)\\n551: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n552: \\t\\t// Note that we don't have any channel to return warnings here. We'll\\n553: \\t\\t// accept that for now since warnings during a schema upgrade would\\n554: \\t\\t// be pretty weird anyway, since this operation is supposed to seem\\n555: \\t\\t// invisible to the user.\\n556: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n557: \\t}\\n558: \\n559: \\tobj, err := src.Decode(schema.ImpliedType())\\n560: \\tif err != nil {\\n561: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n562: \\t}\\n563: \\n564: \\treturn obj, diags\\n565: }\\n566: \\n567: // graphNodesAreResourceInstancesInDifferentInstancesOfSameModule is an\\n568: // annoyingly-task-specific helper function that returns true if and only if\\n569: // the following conditions hold:\\n570: //   - Both of the given vertices represent specific resource instances, as\\n571: //     opposed to unexpanded resources or any other non-resource-related object.\\n572: //   - The module instance addresses for both of the resource instances belong\\n573: //     to the same static module.\\n574: //   - The module instance addresses for both of the resource instances are\\n575: //     not equal, indicating that they belong to different instances of the\\n576: //     same module.\\n577: //\\n578: // This result can be used as a way to compensate for the effects of\\n579: // conservative analysis passes in our graph builders which make their\\n580: // decisions based only on unexpanded addresses, often so that they can behave\\n581: // correctly for interactions between expanded and not-yet-expanded objects.\\n582: //\\n583: // Callers of this helper function will typically skip adding an edge between\\n584: // the two given nodes if this function returns true.\\n585: func graphNodesAreResourceInstancesInDifferentInstancesOfSameModule(a, b dag.Vertex) bool {\\n586: \\taRI, aOK := a.(GraphNodeResourceInstance)\\n587: \\tbRI, bOK := b.(GraphNodeResourceInstance)\\n588: \\tif !(aOK && bOK) {\\n589: \\t\\treturn false\\n590: \\t}\\n591: \\taModInst := aRI.ResourceInstanceAddr().Module\\n592: \\tbModInst := bRI.ResourceInstanceAddr().Module\\n593: \\taMod := aModInst.Module()\\n594: \\tbMod := bModInst.Module()\\n595: \\tif !aMod.Equal(bMod) {\\n596: \\t\\treturn false\\n597: \\t}\\n598: \\treturn !aModInst.Equal(bModInst)\\n599: }\\n600: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract.go\"}"
14. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "internal/terraform/node_resource_apply.go",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\\n2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\\n3: \\n4: package terraform\\n5: \\n6: import (\\n7: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\\\"\\n8: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\\\"\\n9: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\\\"\\n10: )\\n11: \\n12: // nodeExpandApplyableResource handles the first layer of resource\\n13: // expansion during apply. Even though the resource instances themselves are\\n14: // already expanded from the plan, we still need to expand the\\n15: // NodeApplyableResource nodes into their respective modules.\\n16: type nodeExpandApplyableResource struct {\\n17: \\t*NodeAbstractResource\\n18: \\n19: \\tPartialExpansions []addrs.PartialExpandedResource\\n20: }\\n21: \\n22: var (\\n23: \\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable        = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\\n24: \\t_ GraphNodeReferencer           = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\\n25: \\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource       = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\\n26: \\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\\n27: \\t_ graphNodeExpandsInstances     = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\\n28: \\t_ GraphNodeTargetable           = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\\n29: \\t_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable    = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\\n30: )\\n31: \\n32: func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) expandsInstances() {\\n33: }\\n34: \\n35: func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) References() []*addrs.Reference {\\n36: \\trefs := n.NodeAbstractResource.References()\\n37: \\n38: \\t// The expand node needs to connect to the individual resource instances it\\n39: \\t// references, but cannot refer to it's own instances without causing\\n40: \\t// cycles. It would be preferable to entirely disallow self references\\n41: \\t// without the `self` identifier, but those were allowed in provisioners\\n42: \\t// for compatibility with legacy configuration. We also can't always just\\n43: \\t// filter them out for all resource node types, because the only method we\\n44: \\t// have for catching certain invalid configurations are the cycles that\\n45: \\t// result from these inter-instance references.\\n46: \\treturn filterSelfRefs(n.Addr.Resource, refs)\\n47: }\\n48: \\n49: func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) Name() string {\\n50: \\treturn n.NodeAbstractResource.Name() + \\\" (expand)\\\"\\n51: }\\n52: \\n53: func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) DynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n54: \\tif n.Addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\\n55: \\t\\treturn n.dynamicExpandEphemeral(ctx)\\n56: \\t}\\n57: \\n58: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n59: \\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\\n60: \\tmoduleInstances := expander.ExpandModule(n.Addr.Module, false)\\n61: \\tfor _, module := range moduleInstances {\\n62: \\t\\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(ctx, module)\\n63: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(module)))\\n64: \\t}\\n65: \\n66: \\treturn nil, diags\\n67: }\\n68: \\n69: // We need to expand the ephemeral resources mostly the same as we do during\\n70: // planning. There a lot of options than happen during planning which aren't\\n71: // applicable to apply however, and we have to make sure we don't re-register\\n72: // checks which already recorded in the plan, so we create a pared down version\\n73: // of the plan expansion here.\\n74: func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) dynamicExpandEphemeral(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n75: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n76: \\tvar g Graph\\n77: \\n78: \\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\\n79: \\tmoduleInstances := expander.ExpandModule(n.Addr.Module, false)\\n80: \\n81: \\tfor _, module := range moduleInstances {\\n82: \\t\\tresAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(module)\\n83: \\t\\texpDiags := n.expandEphemeralResourceInstances(ctx, resAddr, &g)\\n84: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(expDiags)\\n85: \\t}\\n86: \\n87: \\treturn &g, diags\\n88: }\\n89: \\n90: func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) expandEphemeralResourceInstances(globalCtx EvalContext, resAddr addrs.AbsResource, g *Graph) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\n91: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n92: \\n93: \\t// The rest of our work here needs to know which module instance it's\\n94: \\t// working in, so that it can evaluate expressions in the appropriate scope.\\n95: \\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(globalCtx, resAddr.Module)\\n96: \\n97: \\t// writeResourceState is responsible for informing the expander of what\\n98: \\t// repetition mode this resource has, which allows expander.ExpandResource\\n99: \\t// to work below.\\n100: \\tmoreDiags := n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, resAddr)\\n101: \\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\\n102: \\tif moreDiags.HasErrors() {\\n103: \\t\\treturn diags\\n104: \\t}\\n105: \\n106: \\texpander := moduleCtx.InstanceExpander()\\n107: \\tinstanceAddrs := expander.ExpandResource(resAddr)\\n108: \\n109: \\tinstG, instDiags := n.ephemeralResourceInstanceSubgraph(resAddr, instanceAddrs)\\n110: \\tif instDiags.HasErrors() {\\n111: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(instDiags)\\n112: \\t\\treturn diags\\n113: \\t}\\n114: \\tg.Subsume(&instG.AcyclicGraph.Graph)\\n115: \\treturn diags\\n116: }\\n117: \\n118: func (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) ephemeralResourceInstanceSubgraph(addr addrs.AbsResource, instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n119: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n120: \\n121: \\tconcreteEphemeral := func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\\n122: \\t\\ta.Config = n.Config\\n123: \\t\\ta.ResolvedProvider = n.ResolvedProvider\\n124: \\t\\ta.Schema = n.Schema\\n125: \\t\\ta.ProvisionerSchemas = n.ProvisionerSchemas\\n126: \\t\\ta.ProviderMetas = n.ProviderMetas\\n127: \\t\\ta.dependsOn = n.dependsOn\\n128: \\n129: \\t\\t// we still need the Plannable resource instance\\n130: \\t\\treturn &NodeApplyableResourceInstance{\\n131: \\t\\t\\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\\n132: \\t\\t}\\n133: \\t}\\n134: \\n135: \\t// Start creating the steps\\n136: \\tsteps := []GraphTransformer{\\n137: \\t\\t// Expand the count or for_each (if present)\\n138: \\t\\t&ResourceCountTransformer{\\n139: \\t\\t\\tConcrete:      concreteEphemeral,\\n140: \\t\\t\\tSchema:        n.Schema,\\n141: \\t\\t\\tAddr:          n.ResourceAddr(),\\n142: \\t\\t\\tInstanceAddrs: instanceAddrs,\\n143: \\t\\t},\\n144: \\n145: \\t\\t// Targeting\\n146: \\t\\t&TargetsTransformer{Targets: n.Targets},\\n147: \\n148: \\t\\t// Connect references so ordering is correct\\n149: \\t\\t&ReferenceTransformer{},\\n150: \\n151: \\t\\t// Make sure there is a single root\\n152: \\t\\t&RootTransformer{},\\n153: \\t}\\n154: \\n155: \\t// Build the graph\\n156: \\tb := &BasicGraphBuilder{\\n157: \\t\\tSteps: steps,\\n158: \\t\\tName:  \\\"nodeExpandApplyEphemeralResource\\\",\\n159: \\t}\\n160: \\tgraph, graphDiags := b.Build(addr.Module)\\n161: \\tdiags = diags.Append(graphDiags)\\n162: \\n163: \\treturn graph, diags\\n164: }\\n165: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_resource_apply.go\"}"
15. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "internal/terraform/node_resource_apply_instance.go",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\\n2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\\n3: \\n4: package terraform\\n5: \\n6: import (\\n7: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n8: \\t\\\"log\\\"\\n9: \\n10: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\\\"\\n11: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\\\"\\n12: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\\\"\\n13: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\\\"\\n14: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/objchange\\\"\\n15: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\\\"\\n16: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\\\"\\n17: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\\\"\\n18: )\\n19: \\n20: // NodeApplyableResourceInstance represents a resource instance that is\\n21: // \\\"applyable\\\": it is ready to be applied and is represented by a diff.\\n22: //\\n23: // This node is for a specific instance of a resource. It will usually be\\n24: // accompanied in the graph by a NodeApplyableResource representing its\\n25: // containing resource, and should depend on that node to ensure that the\\n26: // state is properly prepared to receive changes to instances.\\n27: type NodeApplyableResourceInstance struct {\\n28: \\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\\n29: \\n30: \\tgraphNodeDeposer // implementation of GraphNodeDeposerConfig\\n31: \\n32: \\t// forceReplace are resource instance addresses where the user wants to\\n33: \\t// force generating a replace action. This set isn't pre-filtered, so\\n34: \\t// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource\\n35: \\t// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.\\n36: \\tforceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\\n37: }\\n38: \\n39: var (\\n40: \\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource     = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\\n41: \\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance   = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\\n42: \\t_ GraphNodeCreator            = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\\n43: \\t_ GraphNodeReferencer         = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\\n44: \\t_ GraphNodeDeposer            = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\\n45: \\t_ GraphNodeExecutable         = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\\n46: \\t_ GraphNodeAttachDependencies = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\\n47: )\\n48: \\n49: // GraphNodeCreator\\n50: func (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) CreateAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\\n51: \\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\\n52: \\treturn &addr\\n53: }\\n54: \\n55: // GraphNodeReferencer, overriding NodeAbstractResourceInstance\\n56: func (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) References() []*addrs.Reference {\\n57: \\t// Start with the usual resource instance implementation\\n58: \\tret := n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.References()\\n59: \\n60: \\t// Applying a resource must also depend on the destruction of any of its\\n61: \\t// dependencies, since this may for example affect the outcome of\\n62: \\t// evaluating an entire list of resources with \\\"count\\\" set (by reducing\\n63: \\t// the count).\\n64: \\t//\\n65: \\t// However, we can't do this in create_before_destroy mode because that\\n66: \\t// would create a dependency cycle. We make a compromise here of requiring\\n67: \\t// changes to be updated across two applies in this case, since the first\\n68: \\t// plan will use the old values.\\n69: \\tif !n.CreateBeforeDestroy() {\\n70: \\t\\tfor _, ref := range ret {\\n71: \\t\\t\\tswitch tr := ref.Subject.(type) {\\n72: \\t\\t\\tcase addrs.ResourceInstance:\\n73: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewRef := *ref // shallow copy so we can mutate\\n74: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewRef.Subject = tr.Phase(addrs.ResourceInstancePhaseDestroy)\\n75: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewRef.Remaining = nil // can't access attributes of something being destroyed\\n76: \\t\\t\\t\\tret = append(ret, &newRef)\\n77: \\t\\t\\tcase addrs.Resource:\\n78: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewRef := *ref // shallow copy so we can mutate\\n79: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewRef.Subject = tr.Phase(addrs.ResourceInstancePhaseDestroy)\\n80: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewRef.Remaining = nil // can't access attributes of something being destroyed\\n81: \\t\\t\\t\\tret = append(ret, &newRef)\\n82: \\t\\t\\t}\\n83: \\t\\t}\\n84: \\t}\\n85: \\n86: \\treturn ret\\n87: }\\n88: \\n89: // GraphNodeAttachDependencies\\n90: func (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) AttachDependencies(deps []addrs.ConfigResource) {\\n91: \\tn.Dependencies = deps\\n92: }\\n93: \\n94: // GraphNodeExecutable\\n95: func (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n96: \\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\\n97: \\n98: \\tif n.Config == nil {\\n99: \\t\\t// If there is no config, and there is no change, then we have nothing\\n100: \\t\\t// to do and the change was left in the plan for informational\\n101: \\t\\t// purposes only.\\n102: \\t\\tchanges := ctx.Changes()\\n103: \\t\\tcsrc := changes.GetResourceInstanceChange(n.ResourceInstanceAddr(), addrs.NotDeposed)\\n104: \\t\\tif csrc == nil || csrc.Action == plans.NoOp {\\n105: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[DEBUG] NodeApplyableResourceInstance: No config or planned change recorded for %s\\\", n.Addr)\\n106: \\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n107: \\t\\t}\\n108: \\n109: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n110: \\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n111: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Resource node has no configuration attached\\\",\\n112: \\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n113: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"The graph node for %s has no configuration attached to it. This suggests a bug in Terraform's apply graph builder; please report it!\\\",\\n114: \\t\\t\\t\\taddr,\\n115: \\t\\t\\t),\\n116: \\t\\t))\\n117: \\t\\treturn diags\\n118: \\t}\\n119: \\n120: \\t// Eval info is different depending on what kind of resource this is\\n121: \\tswitch n.Config.Mode {\\n122: \\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\\n123: \\t\\treturn n.managedResourceExecute(ctx)\\n124: \\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\\n125: \\t\\treturn n.dataResourceExecute(ctx)\\n126: \\tcase addrs.EphemeralResourceMode:\\n127: \\t\\treturn n.ephemeralResourceExecute(ctx)\\n128: \\tdefault:\\n129: \\t\\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\\\"unsupported resource mode %s\\\", n.Config.Mode))\\n130: \\t}\\n131: }\\n132: \\n133: func (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) ephemeralResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\n134: \\t_, diags := ephemeralResourceOpen(ctx, ephemeralResourceInput{\\n135: \\t\\taddr:           n.Addr,\\n136: \\t\\tconfig:         n.Config,\\n137: \\t\\tproviderConfig: n.ResolvedProvider,\\n138: \\t})\\n139: \\n140: \\treturn diags\\n141: }\\n142: \\n143: func (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) dataResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n144: \\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n145: \\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n146: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n147: \\t\\treturn diags\\n148: \\t}\\n149: \\n150: \\tchange, err := n.readDiff(ctx, providerSchema)\\n151: \\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n152: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n153: \\t\\treturn diags\\n154: \\t}\\n155: \\t// Stop early if we don't actually have a diff\\n156: \\tif change == nil {\\n157: \\t\\treturn diags\\n158: \\t}\\n159: \\tif change.Action != plans.Read && change.Action != plans.NoOp {\\n160: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"nonsensical planned action %#v for %s; this is a bug in Terraform\\\", change.Action, n.Addr))\\n161: \\t}\\n162: \\n163: \\t// In this particular call to applyDataSource we include our planned\\n164: \\t// change, which signals that we expect this read to complete fully\\n165: \\t// with no unknown values; it'll produce an error if not.\\n166: \\tstate, repeatData, applyDiags := n.applyDataSource(ctx, change)\\n167: \\tdiags = diags.Append(applyDiags)\\n168: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n169: \\t\\treturn diags\\n170: \\t}\\n171: \\n172: \\tif state != nil {\\n173: \\t\\t// If n.applyDataSource returned a nil state object with no accompanying\\n174: \\t\\t// errors then it determined that the given change doesn't require\\n175: \\t\\t// actually reading the data (e.g. because it was already read during\\n176: \\t\\t// the plan phase) and so we're only running through here to get the\\n177: \\t\\t// extra details like precondition/postcondition checks.\\n178: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState))\\n179: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n180: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n181: \\t\\t}\\n182: \\t}\\n183: \\n184: \\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, nil, \\\"\\\"))\\n185: \\n186: \\tdiags = diags.Append(updateStateHook(ctx))\\n187: \\n188: \\t// Post-conditions might block further progress. We intentionally do this\\n189: \\t// _after_ writing the state/diff because we want to check against\\n190: \\t// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations\\n191: \\t// until the user makes the condition succeed.\\n192: \\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\\n193: \\t\\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\\n194: \\t\\tn.Config.Postconditions,\\n195: \\t\\tctx, n.ResourceInstanceAddr(),\\n196: \\t\\trepeatData,\\n197: \\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n198: \\t)\\n199: \\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\\n200: \\n201: \\treturn diags\\n202: }\\n203: \\n204: func (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) managedResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n205: \\t// Declare a bunch of variables that are used for state during\\n206: \\t// evaluation. Most of this are written to by-address below.\\n207: \\tvar state *states.ResourceInstanceObject\\n208: \\tvar createBeforeDestroyEnabled bool\\n209: \\tvar deposedKey states.DeposedKey\\n210: \\n211: \\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource\\n212: \\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n213: \\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n214: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n215: \\t\\treturn diags\\n216: \\t}\\n217: \\n218: \\t// Get the saved diff for apply\\n219: \\tdiffApply, err := n.readDiff(ctx, providerSchema)\\n220: \\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n221: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n222: \\t\\treturn diags\\n223: \\t}\\n224: \\n225: \\t// We don't want to do any destroys\\n226: \\t// (these are handled by NodeDestroyResourceInstance instead)\\n227: \\tif diffApply == nil || diffApply.Action == plans.Delete {\\n228: \\t\\treturn diags\\n229: \\t}\\n230: \\tif diffApply.Action == plans.Read {\\n231: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"nonsensical planned action %#v for %s; this is a bug in Terraform\\\", diffApply.Action, n.Addr))\\n232: \\t}\\n233: \\n234: \\tdestroy := (diffApply.Action == plans.Delete || diffApply.Action.IsReplace())\\n235: \\t// Get the stored action for CBD if we have a plan already\\n236: \\tcreateBeforeDestroyEnabled = diffApply.Change.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete\\n237: \\n238: \\tif destroy && n.CreateBeforeDestroy() {\\n239: \\t\\tcreateBeforeDestroyEnabled = true\\n240: \\t}\\n241: \\n242: \\tif createBeforeDestroyEnabled {\\n243: \\t\\tstate := ctx.State()\\n244: \\t\\tif n.PreallocatedDeposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\\n245: \\t\\t\\tdeposedKey = state.DeposeResourceInstanceObject(n.Addr)\\n246: \\t\\t} else {\\n247: \\t\\t\\tdeposedKey = n.PreallocatedDeposedKey\\n248: \\t\\t\\tstate.DeposeResourceInstanceObjectForceKey(n.Addr, deposedKey)\\n249: \\t\\t}\\n250: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] managedResourceExecute: prior object for %s now deposed with key %s\\\", n.Addr, deposedKey)\\n251: \\t}\\n252: \\n253: \\tstate, readDiags := n.readResourceInstanceState(ctx, n.ResourceInstanceAddr())\\n254: \\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\\n255: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n256: \\t\\treturn diags\\n257: \\t}\\n258: \\n259: \\t// Get the saved diff\\n260: \\tdiff, err := n.readDiff(ctx, providerSchema)\\n261: \\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n262: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n263: \\t\\treturn diags\\n264: \\t}\\n265: \\n266: \\t// Make a new diff, in case we've learned new values in the state\\n267: \\t// during apply which we can now incorporate.\\n268: \\tdiffApply, _, deferred, repeatData, planDiags := n.plan(ctx, diff, state, false, n.forceReplace)\\n269: \\tdiags = diags.Append(planDiags)\\n270: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n271: \\t\\treturn diags\\n272: \\t}\\n273: \\n274: \\tif deferred != nil {\\n275: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n276: \\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n277: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Resource deferred during apply, but not during plan\\\",\\n278: \\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n279: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Terraform has encountered a bug where a provider would mark the resource %q as deferred during apply, but not during plan. This is most likely a bug in the provider. Please file an issue with the provider.\\\", n.Addr,\\n280: \\t\\t\\t),\\n281: \\t\\t))\\n282: \\t\\treturn diags\\n283: \\t}\\n284: \\n285: \\t// Compare the diffs\\n286: \\tdiags = diags.Append(n.checkPlannedChange(ctx, diff, diffApply, providerSchema))\\n287: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n288: \\t\\treturn diags\\n289: \\t}\\n290: \\n291: \\tdiffApply = reducePlan(addr, diffApply, false)\\n292: \\t// reducePlan may have simplified our planned change\\n293: \\t// into a NoOp if it only requires destroying, since destroying\\n294: \\t// is handled by NodeDestroyResourceInstance. If so, we'll\\n295: \\t// still run through most of the logic here because we do still\\n296: \\t// need to deal with other book-keeping such as marking the\\n297: \\t// change as \\\"complete\\\", and running the author's postconditions.\\n298: \\n299: \\tdiags = diags.Append(n.preApplyHook(ctx, diffApply))\\n300: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n301: \\t\\treturn diags\\n302: \\t}\\n303: \\n304: \\t// If there is no change, there was nothing to apply, and we don't need to\\n305: \\t// re-write the state, but we do need to re-evaluate postconditions.\\n306: \\tif diffApply.Action == plans.NoOp {\\n307: \\t\\treturn diags.Append(n.managedResourcePostconditions(ctx, repeatData))\\n308: \\t}\\n309: \\n310: \\tstate, applyDiags := n.apply(ctx, state, diffApply, n.Config, repeatData, n.CreateBeforeDestroy())\\n311: \\n312: \\tdiags = diags.Append(applyDiags)\\n313: \\n314: \\t// We clear the change out here so that future nodes don't see a change\\n315: \\t// that is already complete.\\n316: \\terr = n.writeChange(ctx, nil, \\\"\\\")\\n317: \\tif err != nil {\\n318: \\t\\treturn diags.Append(err)\\n319: \\t}\\n320: \\n321: \\tstate = maybeTainted(addr.Absolute(ctx.Path()), state, diffApply, diags.Err())\\n322: \\n323: \\tif state != nil {\\n324: \\t\\t// dependencies are always updated to match the configuration during apply\\n325: \\t\\tstate.Dependencies = n.Dependencies\\n326: \\t}\\n327: \\terr = n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState)\\n328: \\tif err != nil {\\n329: \\t\\treturn diags.Append(err)\\n330: \\t}\\n331: \\n332: \\t// Run Provisioners\\n333: \\tcreateNew := (diffApply.Action == plans.Create || diffApply.Action.IsReplace())\\n334: \\tapplyProvisionersDiags := n.evalApplyProvisioners(ctx, state, createNew, configs.ProvisionerWhenCreate)\\n335: \\t// the provisioner errors count as port of the apply error, so we can bundle the diags\\n336: \\tdiags = diags.Append(applyProvisionersDiags)\\n337: \\n338: \\tstate = maybeTainted(addr.Absolute(ctx.Path()), state, diffApply, diags.Err())\\n339: \\n340: \\terr = n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState)\\n341: \\tif err != nil {\\n342: \\t\\treturn diags.Append(err)\\n343: \\t}\\n344: \\n345: \\tif createBeforeDestroyEnabled && diags.HasErrors() {\\n346: \\t\\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\\n347: \\t\\t\\t// This should never happen, and so it always indicates a bug.\\n348: \\t\\t\\t// We should evaluate this node only if we've previously deposed\\n349: \\t\\t\\t// an object as part of the same operation.\\n350: \\t\\t\\tif diffApply != nil {\\n351: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n352: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n353: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Attempt to restore non-existent deposed object\\\",\\n354: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n355: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Terraform has encountered a bug where it would need to restore a deposed object for %s without knowing a deposed object key for that object. This occurred during a %s action. This is a bug in Terraform; please report it!\\\",\\n356: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\taddr, diffApply.Action,\\n357: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n358: \\t\\t\\t\\t))\\n359: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n360: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n361: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n362: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Attempt to restore non-existent deposed object\\\",\\n363: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n364: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Terraform has encountered a bug where it would need to restore a deposed object for %s without knowing a deposed object key for that object. This is a bug in Terraform; please report it!\\\",\\n365: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\taddr,\\n366: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n367: \\t\\t\\t\\t))\\n368: \\t\\t\\t}\\n369: \\t\\t} else {\\n370: \\t\\t\\trestored := ctx.State().MaybeRestoreResourceInstanceDeposed(addr.Absolute(ctx.Path()), deposedKey)\\n371: \\t\\t\\tif restored {\\n372: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] managedResourceExecute: %s deposed object %s was restored as the current object\\\", addr, deposedKey)\\n373: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n374: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] managedResourceExecute: %s deposed object %s remains deposed\\\", addr, deposedKey)\\n375: \\t\\t\\t}\\n376: \\t\\t}\\n377: \\t}\\n378: \\n379: \\tdiags = diags.Append(n.postApplyHook(ctx, state, diags.Err()))\\n380: \\tdiags = diags.Append(updateStateHook(ctx))\\n381: \\n382: \\t// Post-conditions might block further progress. We intentionally do this\\n383: \\t// _after_ writing the state because we want to check against\\n384: \\t// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations\\n385: \\t// until the user makes the condition succeed.\\n386: \\treturn diags.Append(n.managedResourcePostconditions(ctx, repeatData))\\n387: }\\n388: \\n389: func (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) managedResourcePostconditions(ctx EvalContext, repeatData instances.RepetitionData) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n390: \\n391: \\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\\n392: \\t\\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\\n393: \\t\\tn.Config.Postconditions,\\n394: \\t\\tctx, n.ResourceInstanceAddr(), repeatData,\\n395: \\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n396: \\t)\\n397: \\treturn diags.Append(checkDiags)\\n398: }\\n399: \\n400: // checkPlannedChange produces errors if the _actual_ expected value is not\\n401: // compatible with what was recorded in the plan.\\n402: //\\n403: // Errors here are most often indicative of a bug in the provider, so our error\\n404: // messages will report with that in mind. It's also possible that there's a bug\\n405: // in Terraform's Core's own \\\"proposed new value\\\" code in EvalDiff.\\n406: func (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) checkPlannedChange(ctx EvalContext, plannedChange, actualChange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange, providerSchema providers.ProviderSchema) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\n407: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n408: \\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource\\n409: \\n410: \\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.ContainingResource())\\n411: \\tif schema == nil {\\n412: \\t\\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\\n413: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"provider does not support %q\\\", addr.Resource.Type))\\n414: \\t\\treturn diags\\n415: \\t}\\n416: \\n417: \\tabsAddr := addr.Absolute(ctx.Path())\\n418: \\n419: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] checkPlannedChange: Verifying that actual change (action %s) matches planned change (action %s)\\\", actualChange.Action, plannedChange.Action)\\n420: \\n421: \\tif plannedChange.Action != actualChange.Action {\\n422: \\t\\tswitch {\\n423: \\t\\tcase plannedChange.Action == plans.Update && actualChange.Action == plans.NoOp:\\n424: \\t\\t\\t// It's okay for an update to become a NoOp once we've filled in\\n425: \\t\\t\\t// all of the unknown values, since the final values might actually\\n426: \\t\\t\\t// match what was there before after all.\\n427: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[DEBUG] After incorporating new values learned so far during apply, %s change has become NoOp\\\", absAddr)\\n428: \\n429: \\t\\tcase (plannedChange.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete && actualChange.Action == plans.DeleteThenCreate) ||\\n430: \\t\\t\\t(plannedChange.Action == plans.DeleteThenCreate && actualChange.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete):\\n431: \\t\\t\\t// If the order of replacement changed, then that is a bug in terraform\\n432: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n433: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n434: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Terraform produced inconsistent final plan\\\",\\n435: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n436: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"When expanding the plan for %s to include new values learned so far during apply, the planned action changed from %s to %s.\\\\n\\\\nThis is a bug in Terraform and should be reported.\\\",\\n437: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tabsAddr, plannedChange.Action, actualChange.Action,\\n438: \\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n439: \\t\\t\\t))\\n440: \\t\\tdefault:\\n441: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n442: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n443: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider produced inconsistent final plan\\\",\\n444: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n445: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"When expanding the plan for %s to include new values learned so far during apply, provider %q changed the planned action from %s to %s.\\\\n\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\",\\n446: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tabsAddr, n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.String(),\\n447: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tplannedChange.Action, actualChange.Action,\\n448: \\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n449: \\t\\t\\t))\\n450: \\t\\t}\\n451: \\t}\\n452: \\n453: \\terrs := objchange.AssertObjectCompatible(schema, plannedChange.After, actualChange.After)\\n454: \\tfor _, err := range errs {\\n455: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n456: \\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n457: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider produced inconsistent final plan\\\",\\n458: \\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n459: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"When expanding the plan for %s to include new values learned so far during apply, provider %q produced an invalid new value for %s.\\\\n\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\",\\n460: \\t\\t\\t\\tabsAddr, n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.String(), tfdiags.FormatError(err),\\n461: \\t\\t\\t),\\n462: \\t\\t))\\n463: \\t}\\n464: \\treturn diags\\n465: }\\n466: \\n467: // maybeTainted takes the resource addr, new value, planned change, and possible\\n468: // error from an apply operation and return a new instance object marked as\\n469: // tainted if it appears that a create operation has failed.\\n470: func maybeTainted(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange, err error) *states.ResourceInstanceObject {\\n471: \\tif state == nil || change == nil || err == nil {\\n472: \\t\\treturn state\\n473: \\t}\\n474: \\tif state.Status == states.ObjectTainted {\\n475: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] maybeTainted: %s was already tainted, so nothing to do\\\", addr)\\n476: \\t\\treturn state\\n477: \\t}\\n478: \\tif change.Action == plans.Create {\\n479: \\t\\t// If there are errors during a _create_ then the object is\\n480: \\t\\t// in an undefined state, and so we'll mark it as tainted so\\n481: \\t\\t// we can try again on the next run.\\n482: \\t\\t//\\n483: \\t\\t// We don't do this for other change actions because errors\\n484: \\t\\t// during updates will often not change the remote object at all.\\n485: \\t\\t// If there _were_ changes prior to the error, it's the provider's\\n486: \\t\\t// responsibility to record the effect of those changes in the\\n487: \\t\\t// object value it returned.\\n488: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] maybeTainted: %s encountered an error during creation, so it is now marked as tainted\\\", addr)\\n489: \\t\\treturn state.AsTainted()\\n490: \\t}\\n491: \\treturn state\\n492: }\\n493: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_resource_apply_instance.go\"}"
16. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
