Output
"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\\n2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\\n3: \\n4: package terraform\\n5: \\n6: import (\\n7: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n8: \\t\\\"log\\\"\\n9: \\t\\\"path/filepath\\\"\\n10: \\t\\\"sort\\\"\\n11: \\n12: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2\\\"\\n13: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2/hclsyntax\\\"\\n14: \\t\\\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\\\"\\n15: \\n16: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\\\"\\n17: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\\\"\\n18: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\\\"\\n19: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/genconfig\\\"\\n20: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/deferring\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\\\"\\n27: )\\n28: \\n29: // NodePlannableResourceInstance represents a _single_ resource\\n30: // instance that is plannable. This means this represents a single\\n31: // count index, for example.\\n32: type NodePlannableResourceInstance struct {\\n33: \\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\\n34: \\tForceCreateBeforeDestroy bool\\n35: \\n36: \\t// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing individual instances\\n37: \\tskipRefresh bool\\n38: \\n39: \\t// skipPlanChanges indicates we should skip trying to plan change actions\\n40: \\t// for any instances.\\n41: \\tskipPlanChanges bool\\n42: \\n43: \\t// forceReplace are resource instance addresses where the user wants to\\n44: \\t// force generating a replace action. This set isn't pre-filtered, so\\n45: \\t// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource\\n46: \\t// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.\\n47: \\tforceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\\n48: \\n49: \\t// replaceTriggeredBy stores references from replace_triggered_by which\\n50: \\t// triggered this instance to be replaced.\\n51: \\treplaceTriggeredBy []*addrs.Reference\\n52: \\n53: \\t// importTarget, if populated, contains the information necessary to plan\\n54: \\t// an import of this resource.\\n55: \\timportTarget cty.Value\\n56: }\\n57: \\n58: var (\\n59: \\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance       = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)\\n60: \\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable        = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)\\n61: \\t_ GraphNodeReferencer           = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)\\n62: \\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource       = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)\\n63: \\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance     = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)\\n64: \\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)\\n65: \\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceState  = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)\\n66: \\t_ GraphNodeExecutable           = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)\\n67: )\\n68: \\n69: // GraphNodeEvalable\\n70: func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\n71: \\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\\n72: \\n73: \\t// Eval info is different depending on what kind of resource this is\\n74: \\tswitch addr.Resource.Resource.Mode {\\n75: \\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\\n76: \\t\\treturn n.managedResourceExecute(ctx)\\n77: \\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\\n78: \\t\\treturn n.dataResourceExecute(ctx)\\n79: \\tcase addrs.EphemeralResourceMode:\\n80: \\t\\treturn n.ephemeralResourceExecute(ctx)\\n81: \\tdefault:\\n82: \\t\\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\\\"unsupported resource mode %s\\\", n.Config.Mode))\\n83: \\t}\\n84: }\\n85: \\n86: func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) dataResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n87: \\tconfig := n.Config\\n88: \\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\\n89: \\n90: \\tvar change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\\n91: \\n92: \\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n93: \\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n94: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n95: \\t\\treturn diags\\n96: \\t}\\n97: \\n98: \\tdiags = diags.Append(validateSelfRef(addr.Resource, config.Config, providerSchema))\\n99: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n100: \\t\\treturn diags\\n101: \\t}\\n102: \\n103: \\tcheckRuleSeverity := tfdiags.Error\\n104: \\tif n.skipPlanChanges || n.preDestroyRefresh {\\n105: \\t\\tcheckRuleSeverity = tfdiags.Warning\\n106: \\t}\\n107: \\n108: \\tdeferrals := ctx.Deferrals()\\n109: \\tchange, state, deferred, repeatData, planDiags := n.planDataSource(ctx, checkRuleSeverity, n.skipPlanChanges, deferrals.ShouldDeferResourceInstanceChanges(addr, n.Dependencies))\\n110: \\tdiags = diags.Append(planDiags)\\n111: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n112: \\t\\treturn diags\\n113: \\t}\\n114: \\n115: \\t// A nil change here indicates that Terraform is deciding NOT to make a\\n116: \\t// change at all. In which case even if we wanted to try and defer it\\n117: \\t// (because of a dependency) we can't as there is no change to defer.\\n118: \\t//\\n119: \\t// The most common case for this is when the data source is being refreshed\\n120: \\t// but depends on unknown values or dependencies which means we just skip\\n121: \\t// refreshing the data source. We maintain that behaviour here.\\n122: \\tif change != nil && deferred != nil {\\n123: \\t\\t// Then this data source got deferred by the provider during planning.\\n124: \\t\\tdeferrals.ReportDataSourceInstanceDeferred(addr, deferred.Reason, change)\\n125: \\t} else {\\n126: \\t\\t// Not deferred; business as usual.\\n127: \\n128: \\t\\t// write the data source into both the refresh state and the\\n129: \\t\\t// working state\\n130: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, refreshState))\\n131: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n132: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n133: \\t\\t}\\n134: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState))\\n135: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n136: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n137: \\t\\t}\\n138: \\n139: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, change, \\\"\\\"))\\n140: \\n141: \\t\\t// Post-conditions might block further progress. We intentionally do this\\n142: \\t\\t// _after_ writing the state/diff because we want to check against\\n143: \\t\\t// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations\\n144: \\t\\t// until the user makes the condition succeed.\\n145: \\t\\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\\n146: \\t\\t\\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\\n147: \\t\\t\\tn.Config.Postconditions,\\n148: \\t\\t\\tctx, addr, repeatData,\\n149: \\t\\t\\tcheckRuleSeverity,\\n150: \\t\\t)\\n151: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\\n152: \\t}\\n153: \\n154: \\treturn diags\\n155: }\\n156: \\n157: func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) ephemeralResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n158: \\tdeferrals := ctx.Deferrals()\\n159: \\t// For deferred ephemeral resources, we don't need to do anything here.\\n160: \\tif deferrals.ShouldDeferResourceInstanceChanges(n.Addr, n.Dependencies) {\\n161: \\t\\tdeferrals.ReportEphemeralResourceInstanceDeferred(n.Addr, providers.DeferredReasonDeferredPrereq)\\n162: \\t\\treturn nil\\n163: \\t}\\n164: \\n165: \\tdeferred, diags := ephemeralResourceOpen(ctx, ephemeralResourceInput{\\n166: \\t\\taddr:           n.Addr,\\n167: \\t\\tconfig:         n.Config,\\n168: \\t\\tproviderConfig: n.ResolvedProvider,\\n169: \\t})\\n170: \\n171: \\tif deferred != nil {\\n172: \\t\\t// Then this ephemeral resource has been deferred while opening.\\n173: \\t\\tdeferrals.ReportEphemeralResourceInstanceDeferred(n.Addr, deferred.Reason)\\n174: \\t}\\n175: \\n176: \\treturn diags\\n177: }\\n178: \\n179: func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) managedResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n180: \\tconfig := n.Config\\n181: \\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\\n182: \\n183: \\tvar instanceRefreshState *states.ResourceInstanceObject\\n184: \\n185: \\tcheckRuleSeverity := tfdiags.Error\\n186: \\tif n.skipPlanChanges || n.preDestroyRefresh {\\n187: \\t\\tcheckRuleSeverity = tfdiags.Warning\\n188: \\t}\\n189: \\n190: \\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n191: \\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n192: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n193: \\t\\treturn diags\\n194: \\t}\\n195: \\n196: \\tif config != nil {\\n197: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(validateSelfRef(addr.Resource, config.Config, providerSchema))\\n198: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n199: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n200: \\t\\t}\\n201: \\t}\\n202: \\n203: \\timporting := n.importTarget != cty.NilVal && !n.preDestroyRefresh\\n204: \\n205: \\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\\n206: \\n207: \\t// If the resource is to be imported, we now ask the provider for an Import\\n208: \\t// and a Refresh, and save the resulting state to instanceRefreshState.\\n209: \\n210: \\tif importing {\\n211: \\t\\tif n.importTarget.IsKnown() {\\n212: \\t\\t\\tvar importDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n213: \\t\\t\\tinstanceRefreshState, deferred, importDiags = n.importState(ctx, addr, n.importTarget.AsString(), provider, providerSchema)\\n214: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(importDiags)\\n215: \\t\\t} else {\\n216: \\t\\t\\t// Otherwise, just mark the resource as deferred without trying to\\n217: \\t\\t\\t// import it.\\n218: \\t\\t\\tdeferred = &providers.Deferred{\\n219: \\t\\t\\t\\tReason: providers.DeferredReasonResourceConfigUnknown,\\n220: \\t\\t\\t}\\n221: \\t\\t\\tif n.Config == nil && len(n.generateConfigPath) > 0 {\\n222: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Then we're supposed to be generating configuration for this\\n223: \\t\\t\\t\\t// resource, but we can't because the configuration is unknown.\\n224: \\t\\t\\t\\t//\\n225: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Normally, the rest of this function would just be about\\n226: \\t\\t\\t\\t// planning the known configuration to make sure everything we\\n227: \\t\\t\\t\\t// do know about it is correct, but we can't even do that here.\\n228: \\t\\t\\t\\t//\\n229: \\t\\t\\t\\t// What we'll do is write out the address as being deferred with\\n230: \\t\\t\\t\\t// an entirely unknown value. Then we'll skip the rest of this\\n231: \\t\\t\\t\\t// function. (a) We're going to panic later when it complains\\n232: \\t\\t\\t\\t// about having no configuration, and (b) the rest of the\\n233: \\t\\t\\t\\t// function isn't doing anything as there is no configuration\\n234: \\t\\t\\t\\t// to validate.\\n235: \\n236: \\t\\t\\t\\timpliedType := providerSchema.ResourceTypes[addr.Resource.Resource.Type].Block.ImpliedType()\\n237: \\t\\t\\t\\tctx.Deferrals().ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(addr, providers.DeferredReasonResourceConfigUnknown, &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\\n238: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tAddr:         addr,\\n239: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tPrevRunAddr:  addr,\\n240: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\\n241: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tChange: plans.Change{\\n242: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tAction: plans.NoOp, // assume we'll get the config generation correct.\\n243: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tBefore: cty.NullVal(impliedType),\\n244: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tAfter:  cty.UnknownVal(impliedType),\\n245: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tImporting: &plans.Importing{\\n246: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tID: n.importTarget,\\n247: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n248: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n249: \\t\\t\\t\\t})\\n250: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n251: \\t\\t\\t}\\n252: \\t\\t}\\n253: \\t} else {\\n254: \\t\\tvar readDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n255: \\t\\tinstanceRefreshState, readDiags = n.readResourceInstanceState(ctx, addr)\\n256: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\\n257: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n258: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n259: \\t\\t}\\n260: \\t}\\n261: \\n262: \\tif deferred == nil {\\n263: \\t\\t// We'll save a snapshot of what we just read from the state into the\\n264: \\t\\t// prevRunState before we do anything else, since this will capture the\\n265: \\t\\t// result of any schema upgrading that readResourceInstanceState just did,\\n266: \\t\\t// but not include any out-of-band changes we might detect in in the\\n267: \\t\\t// refresh step below.\\n268: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, prevRunState))\\n269: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n270: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n271: \\t\\t}\\n272: \\t\\t// Also the refreshState, because that should still reflect schema upgrades\\n273: \\t\\t// even if it doesn't reflect upstream changes.\\n274: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, refreshState))\\n275: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n276: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n277: \\t\\t}\\n278: \\t}\\n279: \\n280: \\t// we may need to detect a change in CreateBeforeDestroy to ensure it's\\n281: \\t// stored when we are not refreshing\\n282: \\tupdatedCBD := false\\n283: \\tif n.Config != nil && n.Config.Managed != nil && instanceRefreshState != nil {\\n284: \\t\\tnewCBD := n.Config.Managed.CreateBeforeDestroy || n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy\\n285: \\t\\tupdatedCBD = instanceRefreshState.CreateBeforeDestroy != newCBD\\n286: \\t\\tinstanceRefreshState.CreateBeforeDestroy = newCBD\\n287: \\t}\\n288: \\n289: \\tvar refreshDeferred *providers.Deferred\\n290: \\t// This is the state of the resource before we refresh the value, we need to keep track\\n291: \\t// of this to report this as the before value if the refresh is deferred.\\n292: \\tpriorInstanceRefreshState := instanceRefreshState\\n293: \\n294: \\t// Refresh, maybe\\n295: \\t// The import process handles its own refresh\\n296: \\tif !n.skipRefresh && !importing {\\n297: \\t\\tvar refreshDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n298: \\t\\tinstanceRefreshState, refreshDeferred, refreshDiags = n.refresh(ctx, states.NotDeposed, instanceRefreshState, ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed())\\n299: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(refreshDiags)\\n300: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n301: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n302: \\t\\t}\\n303: \\n304: \\t\\tif instanceRefreshState != nil {\\n305: \\t\\t\\t// When refreshing we start by merging the stored dependencies and\\n306: \\t\\t\\t// the configured dependencies. The configured dependencies will be\\n307: \\t\\t\\t// stored to state once the changes are applied. If the plan\\n308: \\t\\t\\t// results in no changes, we will re-write these dependencies\\n309: \\t\\t\\t// below.\\n310: \\t\\t\\tinstanceRefreshState.Dependencies = mergeDeps(n.Dependencies, instanceRefreshState.Dependencies)\\n311: \\t\\t}\\n312: \\n313: \\t\\tif deferred == nil && refreshDeferred != nil {\\n314: \\t\\t\\tdeferred = refreshDeferred\\n315: \\t\\t}\\n316: \\n317: \\t\\tif deferred == nil {\\n318: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, refreshState))\\n319: \\t\\t}\\n320: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n321: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n322: \\t\\t}\\n323: \\t}\\n324: \\n325: \\tif n.skipRefresh && !importing && updatedCBD {\\n326: \\t\\t// CreateBeforeDestroy must be set correctly in the state which is used\\n327: \\t\\t// to create the apply graph, so if we did not refresh the state make\\n328: \\t\\t// sure we still update any changes to CreateBeforeDestroy.\\n329: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, refreshState))\\n330: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n331: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n332: \\t\\t}\\n333: \\t}\\n334: \\n335: \\t// Plan the instance, unless we're in the refresh-only mode\\n336: \\tif !n.skipPlanChanges {\\n337: \\n338: \\t\\t// add this instance to n.forceReplace if replacement is triggered by\\n339: \\t\\t// another change\\n340: \\t\\trepData := instances.RepetitionData{}\\n341: \\t\\tswitch k := addr.Resource.Key.(type) {\\n342: \\t\\tcase addrs.IntKey:\\n343: \\t\\t\\trepData.CountIndex = k.Value()\\n344: \\t\\tcase addrs.StringKey:\\n345: \\t\\t\\trepData.EachKey = k.Value()\\n346: \\t\\t\\trepData.EachValue = cty.DynamicVal\\n347: \\t\\t}\\n348: \\n349: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.replaceTriggered(ctx, repData))\\n350: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n351: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n352: \\t\\t}\\n353: \\n354: \\t\\tchange, instancePlanState, planDeferred, repeatData, planDiags := n.plan(\\n355: \\t\\t\\tctx, nil, instanceRefreshState, n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy, n.forceReplace,\\n356: \\t\\t)\\n357: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(planDiags)\\n358: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n359: \\t\\t\\t// If we are importing and generating a configuration, we need to\\n360: \\t\\t\\t// ensure the change is written out so the configuration can be\\n361: \\t\\t\\t// captured.\\n362: \\t\\t\\tif planDeferred == nil && len(n.generateConfigPath) > 0 {\\n363: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Update our return plan\\n364: \\t\\t\\t\\tchange := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\\n365: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tAddr:         n.Addr,\\n366: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\\n367: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\\n368: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tChange: plans.Change{\\n369: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// we only need a placeholder, so this will be a NoOp\\n370: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tAction:          plans.NoOp,\\n371: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tBefore:          instanceRefreshState.Value,\\n372: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tAfter:           instanceRefreshState.Value,\\n373: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tGeneratedConfig: n.generatedConfigHCL,\\n374: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n375: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n376: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, change, \\\"\\\"))\\n377: \\t\\t\\t}\\n378: \\n379: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n380: \\t\\t}\\n381: \\n382: \\t\\tif deferred == nil && planDeferred != nil {\\n383: \\t\\t\\tdeferred = planDeferred\\n384: \\t\\t}\\n385: \\n386: \\t\\tif importing {\\n387: \\t\\t\\tchange.Importing = &plans.Importing{ID: n.importTarget}\\n388: \\t\\t}\\n389: \\n390: \\t\\t// FIXME: here we udpate the change to reflect the reason for\\n391: \\t\\t// replacement, but we still overload forceReplace to get the correct\\n392: \\t\\t// change planned.\\n393: \\t\\tif len(n.replaceTriggeredBy) > 0 {\\n394: \\t\\t\\tchange.ActionReason = plans.ResourceInstanceReplaceByTriggers\\n395: \\t\\t}\\n396: \\n397: \\t\\tdeferrals := ctx.Deferrals()\\n398: \\t\\tif deferred != nil {\\n399: \\t\\t\\t// Then this resource has been deferred either during the import,\\n400: \\t\\t\\t// refresh or planning stage. We'll report the deferral and\\n401: \\t\\t\\t// store what we could produce in the deferral tracker.\\n402: \\t\\t\\tdeferrals.ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(addr, deferred.Reason, change)\\n403: \\t\\t} else if !deferrals.ShouldDeferResourceInstanceChanges(n.Addr, n.Dependencies) {\\n404: \\t\\t\\t// We intentionally write the change before the subsequent checks, because\\n405: \\t\\t\\t// all of the checks below this point are for problems caused by the\\n406: \\t\\t\\t// context surrounding the change, rather than the change itself, and\\n407: \\t\\t\\t// so it's helpful to still include the valid-in-isolation change as\\n408: \\t\\t\\t// part of the plan as additional context in our error output.\\n409: \\t\\t\\t//\\n410: \\t\\t\\t// FIXME: it is currently important that we write resource changes to\\n411: \\t\\t\\t// the plan (n.writeChange) before we write the corresponding state\\n412: \\t\\t\\t// (n.writeResourceInstanceState).\\n413: \\t\\t\\t//\\n414: \\t\\t\\t// This is because the planned resource state will normally have the\\n415: \\t\\t\\t// status of states.ObjectPlanned, which causes later logic to refer to\\n416: \\t\\t\\t// the contents of the plan to retrieve the resource data. Because\\n417: \\t\\t\\t// there is no shared lock between these two data structures, reversing\\n418: \\t\\t\\t// the order of these writes will cause a brief window of inconsistency\\n419: \\t\\t\\t// which can lead to a failed safety check.\\n420: \\t\\t\\t//\\n421: \\t\\t\\t// Future work should adjust these APIs such that it is impossible to\\n422: \\t\\t\\t// update these two data structures incorrectly through any objects\\n423: \\t\\t\\t// reachable via the terraform.EvalContext API.\\n424: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, change, \\\"\\\"))\\n425: \\t\\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n426: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n427: \\t\\t\\t}\\n428: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instancePlanState, workingState))\\n429: \\t\\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n430: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n431: \\t\\t\\t}\\n432: \\n433: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.checkPreventDestroy(change))\\n434: \\t\\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n435: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n436: \\t\\t\\t}\\n437: \\n438: \\t\\t\\t// If this plan resulted in a NoOp, then apply won't have a chance to make\\n439: \\t\\t\\t// any changes to the stored dependencies. Since this is a NoOp we know\\n440: \\t\\t\\t// that the stored dependencies will have no effect during apply, and we can\\n441: \\t\\t\\t// write them out now.\\n442: \\t\\t\\tif change.Action == plans.NoOp && !depsEqual(instanceRefreshState.Dependencies, n.Dependencies) {\\n443: \\t\\t\\t\\t// the refresh state will be the final state for this resource, so\\n444: \\t\\t\\t\\t// finalize the dependencies here if they need to be updated.\\n445: \\t\\t\\t\\tinstanceRefreshState.Dependencies = n.Dependencies\\n446: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, refreshState))\\n447: \\t\\t\\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n448: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n449: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n450: \\t\\t\\t}\\n451: \\n452: \\t\\t\\t// Post-conditions might block completion. We intentionally do this\\n453: \\t\\t\\t// _after_ writing the state/diff because we want to check against\\n454: \\t\\t\\t// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations\\n455: \\t\\t\\t// until the user makes the condition succeed.\\n456: \\t\\t\\t// (Note that some preconditions will end up being skipped during\\n457: \\t\\t\\t// planning, because their conditions depend on values not yet known.)\\n458: \\t\\t\\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\\n459: \\t\\t\\t\\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\\n460: \\t\\t\\t\\tn.Config.Postconditions,\\n461: \\t\\t\\t\\tctx, n.ResourceInstanceAddr(), repeatData,\\n462: \\t\\t\\t\\tcheckRuleSeverity,\\n463: \\t\\t\\t)\\n464: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\\n465: \\t\\t} else {\\n466: \\t\\t\\t// The deferrals tracker says that we must defer changes for\\n467: \\t\\t\\t// this resource instance, presumably due to a dependency on an\\n468: \\t\\t\\t// upstream object that was already deferred. Therefore we just\\n469: \\t\\t\\t// report our own deferral (capturing a placeholder value in the\\n470: \\t\\t\\t// deferral tracker) and don't add anything to the plan or\\n471: \\t\\t\\t// working state.\\n472: \\t\\t\\t// In this case, the expression evaluator should use the placeholder\\n473: \\t\\t\\t// value registered here as the value of this resource instance,\\n474: \\t\\t\\t// instead of using the plan.\\n475: \\t\\t\\tdeferrals.ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(n.Addr, providers.DeferredReasonDeferredPrereq, change)\\n476: \\t\\t}\\n477: \\t} else {\\n478: \\t\\t// In refresh-only mode we need to evaluate the for-each expression in\\n479: \\t\\t// order to supply the value to the pre- and post-condition check\\n480: \\t\\t// blocks. This has the unfortunate edge case of a refresh-only plan\\n481: \\t\\t// executing with a for-each map which has the same keys but different\\n482: \\t\\t// values, which could result in a post-condition check relying on that\\n483: \\t\\t// value being inaccurate. Unless we decide to store the value of the\\n484: \\t\\t// for-each expression in state, this is unavoidable.\\n485: \\t\\tforEach, _, _ := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, false)\\n486: \\t\\trepeatData := EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)\\n487: \\n488: \\t\\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\\n489: \\t\\t\\taddrs.ResourcePrecondition,\\n490: \\t\\t\\tn.Config.Preconditions,\\n491: \\t\\t\\tctx, addr, repeatData,\\n492: \\t\\t\\tcheckRuleSeverity,\\n493: \\t\\t)\\n494: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\\n495: \\n496: \\t\\t// Even if we don't plan changes, we do still need to at least update\\n497: \\t\\t// the working state to reflect the refresh result. If not, then e.g.\\n498: \\t\\t// any output values refering to this will not react to the drift.\\n499: \\t\\t// (Even if we didn't actually refresh above, this will still save\\n500: \\t\\t// the result of any schema upgrading we did in readResourceInstanceState.)\\n501: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, workingState))\\n502: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n503: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n504: \\t\\t}\\n505: \\n506: \\t\\t// Here we also evaluate post-conditions after updating the working\\n507: \\t\\t// state, because we want to check against the result of the refresh.\\n508: \\t\\t// Unlike in normal planning mode, these checks are still evaluated\\n509: \\t\\t// even if pre-conditions generated diagnostics, because we have no\\n510: \\t\\t// planned changes to block.\\n511: \\t\\tcheckDiags = evalCheckRules(\\n512: \\t\\t\\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\\n513: \\t\\t\\tn.Config.Postconditions,\\n514: \\t\\t\\tctx, addr, repeatData,\\n515: \\t\\t\\tcheckRuleSeverity,\\n516: \\t\\t)\\n517: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\\n518: \\n519: \\t\\t// In this case we skipped planning changes and therefore need to report the deferral\\n520: \\t\\t// here, if there was one.\\n521: \\t\\tif refreshDeferred != nil {\\n522: \\t\\t\\tctx.Deferrals().ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(addr, deferred.Reason, &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\\n523: \\t\\t\\t\\tAddr:         n.Addr,\\n524: \\t\\t\\t\\tPrevRunAddr:  n.Addr,\\n525: \\t\\t\\t\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\\n526: \\t\\t\\t\\tChange: plans.Change{\\n527: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tAction: plans.Read,\\n528: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tBefore: priorInstanceRefreshState.Value,\\n529: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tAfter:  instanceRefreshState.Value,\\n530: \\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n531: \\t\\t\\t})\\n532: \\t\\t}\\n533: \\t}\\n534: \\n535: \\treturn diags\\n536: }\\n537: \\n538: // replaceTriggered checks if this instance needs to be replace due to a change\\n539: // in a replace_triggered_by reference. If replacement is required, the\\n540: // instance address is added to forceReplace\\n541: func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) replaceTriggered(ctx EvalContext, repData instances.RepetitionData) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\n542: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n543: \\tif n.Config == nil {\\n544: \\t\\treturn diags\\n545: \\t}\\n546: \\n547: \\tfor _, expr := range n.Config.TriggersReplacement {\\n548: \\t\\tref, replace, evalDiags := ctx.EvaluateReplaceTriggeredBy(expr, repData)\\n549: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\\n550: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n551: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n552: \\t\\t}\\n553: \\n554: \\t\\tif replace {\\n555: \\t\\t\\t// FIXME: forceReplace accomplishes the same goal, however we may\\n556: \\t\\t\\t// want to communicate more information about which resource\\n557: \\t\\t\\t// triggered the replacement in the plan.\\n558: \\t\\t\\t// Rather than further complicating the plan method with more\\n559: \\t\\t\\t// options, we can refactor both of these features later.\\n560: \\t\\t\\tn.forceReplace = append(n.forceReplace, n.Addr)\\n561: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[DEBUG] ReplaceTriggeredBy forcing replacement of %s due to change in %s\\\", n.Addr, ref.DisplayString())\\n562: \\n563: \\t\\t\\tn.replaceTriggeredBy = append(n.replaceTriggeredBy, ref)\\n564: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n565: \\t\\t}\\n566: \\t}\\n567: \\n568: \\treturn diags\\n569: }\\n570: \\n571: func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) importState(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, importId string, provider providers.Interface, providerSchema providers.ProviderSchema) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n572: \\tdeferralAllowed := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\\n573: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n574: \\tabsAddr := addr.Resource.Absolute(ctx.Path())\\n575: \\thookResourceID := HookResourceIdentity{\\n576: \\t\\tAddr:         absAddr,\\n577: \\t\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider,\\n578: \\t}\\n579: \\n580: \\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\\n581: \\n582: \\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n583: \\t\\treturn h.PrePlanImport(hookResourceID, importId)\\n584: \\t}))\\n585: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n586: \\t\\treturn nil, deferred, diags\\n587: \\t}\\n588: \\n589: \\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.Addr.Resource.Resource)\\n590: \\tif schema == nil {\\n591: \\t\\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\\n592: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"provider does not support resource type for %q\\\", n.Addr))\\n593: \\t\\treturn nil, deferred, diags\\n594: \\t}\\n595: \\n596: \\tvar resp providers.ImportResourceStateResponse\\n597: \\tif n.override != nil {\\n598: \\t\\t// For overriding resources that are being imported, we cheat a little\\n599: \\t\\t// bit and look ahead at the configuration the user has provided and\\n600: \\t\\t// we'll use that as the basis for the resource we're going to make up\\n601: \\t\\t// that is due to be overridden.\\n602: \\n603: \\t\\t// Note, we know we have configuration as it's impossible to enable\\n604: \\t\\t// config generation during tests, and the validation that config exists\\n605: \\t\\t// if configuration generation is off has already happened.\\n606: \\t\\tif n.Config == nil {\\n607: \\t\\t\\t// But, just in case we change this at some point in the future,\\n608: \\t\\t\\t// let's add a specific error message here we can test for to\\n609: \\t\\t\\t// document the expectation somewhere. This shouldn't happen in\\n610: \\t\\t\\t// production, so we don't bother with a pretty error.\\n611: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"override blocks do not support config generation\\\"))\\n612: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, deferred, diags\\n613: \\t\\t}\\n614: \\n615: \\t\\tforEach, _, _ := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, false)\\n616: \\t\\tkeyData := EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)\\n617: \\t\\tconfigVal, _, configDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(n.Config.Config, schema, nil, keyData)\\n618: \\t\\tif configDiags.HasErrors() {\\n619: \\t\\t\\t// We have an overridden resource so we're definitely in a test and\\n620: \\t\\t\\t// the users config is not valid. So give up and just report the\\n621: \\t\\t\\t// problems in the users configuration. Normally, we'd import the\\n622: \\t\\t\\t// resource before giving up but for a test it doesn't matter, the\\n623: \\t\\t\\t// test fails in the same way and the state is just lost anyway.\\n624: \\t\\t\\t//\\n625: \\t\\t\\t// If there were only warnings from the config then we'll duplicate\\n626: \\t\\t\\t// them if we include them (as the config will be loaded again\\n627: \\t\\t\\t// later), so only add the configDiags into the main diags if we\\n628: \\t\\t\\t// found actual errors.\\n629: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\\n630: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, deferred, diags\\n631: \\t\\t}\\n632: \\t\\tconfigVal, _ = configVal.UnmarkDeep()\\n633: \\n634: \\t\\t// Let's pretend we're reading the value as a data source so we\\n635: \\t\\t// pre-compute values now as if the resource has already been created.\\n636: \\t\\toverride, overrideDiags := mocking.ComputedValuesForDataSource(configVal, mocking.MockedData{\\n637: \\t\\t\\tValue: n.override.Values,\\n638: \\t\\t\\tRange: n.override.ValuesRange,\\n639: \\t\\t}, schema)\\n640: \\t\\tresp = providers.ImportResourceStateResponse{\\n641: \\t\\t\\tImportedResources: []providers.ImportedResource{\\n642: \\t\\t\\t\\t{\\n643: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tTypeName: addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\\n644: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tState:    override,\\n645: \\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n646: \\t\\t\\t},\\n647: \\t\\t\\tDiagnostics: overrideDiags.InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, absAddr.String()),\\n648: \\t\\t}\\n649: \\t} else {\\n650: \\t\\tresp = provider.ImportResourceState(providers.ImportResourceStateRequest{\\n651: \\t\\t\\tTypeName: addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\\n652: \\t\\t\\tID:       importId,\\n653: \\t\\t\\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\\n654: \\t\\t\\t\\tDeferralAllowed: deferralAllowed,\\n655: \\t\\t\\t},\\n656: \\t\\t})\\n657: \\t}\\n658: \\t// If we don't support deferrals, but the provider reports a deferral and does not\\n659: \\t// emit any error level diagnostics, we should emit an error.\\n660: \\tif resp.Deferred != nil && !deferralAllowed && !resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {\\n661: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(deferring.UnexpectedProviderDeferralDiagnostic(n.Addr))\\n662: \\t}\\n663: \\tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics)\\n664: \\tdeferred = resp.Deferred\\n665: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n666: \\t\\treturn nil, deferred, diags\\n667: \\t}\\n668: \\n669: \\timported := resp.ImportedResources\\n670: \\n671: \\tif len(imported) > 1 {\\n672: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n673: \\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n674: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Multiple import states not supported\\\",\\n675: \\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"While attempting to import with ID %s, the provider \\\"+\\n676: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"returned multiple resource instance states. This \\\"+\\n677: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"is not currently supported.\\\",\\n678: \\t\\t\\t\\timportId,\\n679: \\t\\t\\t),\\n680: \\t\\t))\\n681: \\t}\\n682: \\n683: \\tif len(imported) == 0 {\\n684: \\n685: \\t\\t// Sanity check against the providers. If the provider defers the response, it may not have been able to return a state, so we'll only error if no deferral was returned.\\n686: \\t\\tif deferred == nil {\\n687: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n688: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n689: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Import returned no resources\\\",\\n690: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"While attempting to import with ID %s, the provider\\\"+\\n691: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"returned no instance states.\\\",\\n692: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\timportId,\\n693: \\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n694: \\t\\t\\t))\\n695: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, deferred, diags\\n696: \\t\\t}\\n697: \\n698: \\t\\t// If we were deferred, then let's make up a resource to represent the\\n699: \\t\\t// state we're going to import.\\n700: \\t\\tstate := providers.ImportedResource{\\n701: \\t\\t\\tTypeName: addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\\n702: \\t\\t\\tState:    cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType()),\\n703: \\t\\t}\\n704: \\n705: \\t\\t// We skip the read and further validation since we make up the state\\n706: \\t\\t// of the imported resource anyways.\\u0010\\n707: \\t\\treturn state.AsInstanceObject(), deferred, diags\\n708: \\t}\\n709: \\n710: \\tfor _, obj := range imported {\\n711: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] graphNodeImportState: import %s %q produced instance object of type %s\\\", absAddr.String(), importId, obj.TypeName)\\n712: \\t}\\n713: \\n714: \\timportedState := imported[0].AsInstanceObject()\\n715: \\n716: \\t// We can only call the hooks and validate the imported state if we have\\n717: \\t// actually done the import.\\n718: \\tif resp.Deferred == nil {\\n719: \\t\\t// call post-import hook\\n720: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n721: \\t\\t\\treturn h.PostPlanImport(hookResourceID, imported)\\n722: \\t\\t}))\\n723: \\t}\\n724: \\n725: \\tif imported[0].TypeName == \\\"\\\" {\\n726: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"import of %s didn't set type\\\", n.Addr.String()))\\n727: \\t\\treturn nil, deferred, diags\\n728: \\t}\\n729: \\n730: \\tif deferred == nil && importedState.Value.IsNull() {\\n731: \\t\\t// It's actually okay for a deferred import to have returned a null.\\n732: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n733: \\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n734: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Import returned null resource\\\",\\n735: \\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"While attempting to import with ID %s, the provider\\\"+\\n736: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"returned an instance with no state.\\\",\\n737: \\t\\t\\t\\timportId,\\n738: \\t\\t\\t),\\n739: \\t\\t))\\n740: \\n741: \\t}\\n742: \\n743: \\t// refresh\\n744: \\triNode := &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\\n745: \\t\\tAddr: n.Addr,\\n746: \\t\\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\\n747: \\t\\t\\tResolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\\n748: \\t\\t},\\n749: \\t\\toverride: n.override,\\n750: \\t}\\n751: \\tinstanceRefreshState, refreshDeferred, refreshDiags := riNode.refresh(ctx, states.NotDeposed, importedState, ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed())\\n752: \\tdiags = diags.Append(refreshDiags)\\n753: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n754: \\t\\treturn instanceRefreshState, deferred, diags\\n755: \\t}\\n756: \\n757: \\t// report the refresh was deferred, we don't need to error since the import step succeeded\\n758: \\tif deferred == nil && refreshDeferred != nil {\\n759: \\t\\tdeferred = refreshDeferred\\n760: \\t}\\n761: \\n762: \\t// verify the existence of the imported resource\\n763: \\tif refreshDeferred == nil && instanceRefreshState.Value.IsNull() {\\n764: \\t\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n765: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n766: \\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n767: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Cannot import non-existent remote object\\\",\\n768: \\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n769: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"While attempting to import an existing object to %q, \\\"+\\n770: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"the provider detected that no object exists with the given id. \\\"+\\n771: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Only pre-existing objects can be imported; check that the id \\\"+\\n772: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"is correct and that it is associated with the provider's \\\"+\\n773: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"configured region or endpoint, or use \\\\\\\"terraform apply\\\\\\\" to \\\"+\\n774: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"create a new remote object for this resource.\\\",\\n775: \\t\\t\\t\\tn.Addr,\\n776: \\t\\t\\t),\\n777: \\t\\t))\\n778: \\t\\treturn instanceRefreshState, deferred, diags\\n779: \\t}\\n780: \\n781: \\t// If we're importing and generating config, generate it now. We only\\n782: \\t// generate config if the import isn't being deferred. We should generate\\n783: \\t// the configuration in the plan that the import is actually happening in.\\n784: \\tif deferred == nil && len(n.generateConfigPath) > 0 {\\n785: \\t\\tif n.Config != nil {\\n786: \\t\\t\\treturn instanceRefreshState, nil, diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"tried to generate config for %s, but it already exists\\\", n.Addr))\\n787: \\t\\t}\\n788: \\n789: \\t\\t// Generate the HCL string first, then parse the HCL body from it.\\n790: \\t\\t// First we generate the contents of the resource block for use within\\n791: \\t\\t// the planning node. Then we wrap it in an enclosing resource block to\\n792: \\t\\t// pass into the plan for rendering.\\n793: \\t\\tgeneratedHCLAttributes, generatedDiags := n.generateHCLStringAttributes(n.Addr, instanceRefreshState, schema)\\n794: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(generatedDiags)\\n795: \\n796: \\t\\tn.generatedConfigHCL = genconfig.WrapResourceContents(n.Addr, generatedHCLAttributes)\\n797: \\n798: \\t\\t// parse the \\\"file\\\" as HCL to get the hcl.Body\\n799: \\t\\tsynthHCLFile, hclDiags := hclsyntax.ParseConfig([]byte(generatedHCLAttributes), filepath.Base(n.generateConfigPath), hcl.Pos{Byte: 0, Line: 1, Column: 1})\\n800: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(hclDiags)\\n801: \\t\\tif hclDiags.HasErrors() {\\n802: \\t\\t\\treturn instanceRefreshState, nil, diags\\n803: \\t\\t}\\n804: \\n805: \\t\\t// We have to do a kind of mini parsing of the content here to correctly\\n806: \\t\\t// mark attributes like 'provider' as hidden. We only care about the\\n807: \\t\\t// resulting content, so it's remain that gets passed into the resource\\n808: \\t\\t// as the config.\\n809: \\t\\t_, remain, resourceDiags := synthHCLFile.Body.PartialContent(configs.ResourceBlockSchema)\\n810: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(resourceDiags)\\n811: \\t\\tif resourceDiags.HasErrors() {\\n812: \\t\\t\\treturn instanceRefreshState, nil, diags\\n813: \\t\\t}\\n814: \\n815: \\t\\tn.Config = &configs.Resource{\\n816: \\t\\t\\tMode:     addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\\n817: \\t\\t\\tType:     n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\\n818: \\t\\t\\tName:     n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Name,\\n819: \\t\\t\\tConfig:   remain,\\n820: \\t\\t\\tManaged:  &configs.ManagedResource{},\\n821: \\t\\t\\tProvider: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider,\\n822: \\t\\t}\\n823: \\t}\\n824: \\n825: \\tif deferred == nil {\\n826: \\t\\t// Only write the state if the change isn't being deferred. We're also\\n827: \\t\\t// reporting the deferred status to the caller, so they should know\\n828: \\t\\t// not to read from the state.\\n829: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(riNode.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, refreshState))\\n830: \\t}\\n831: \\treturn instanceRefreshState, deferred, diags\\n832: }\\n833: \\n834: // generateHCLStringAttributes produces a string in HCL format for the given\\n835: // resource state and schema without the surrounding block.\\n836: func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) generateHCLStringAttributes(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, schema *configschema.Block) (string, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n837: \\tfilteredSchema := schema.Filter(\\n838: \\t\\tconfigschema.FilterOr(\\n839: \\t\\t\\tconfigschema.FilterReadOnlyAttribute,\\n840: \\t\\t\\tconfigschema.FilterDeprecatedAttribute,\\n841: \\n842: \\t\\t\\t// The legacy SDK adds an Optional+Computed \\\"id\\\" attribute to the\\n843: \\t\\t\\t// resource schema even if not defined in provider code.\\n844: \\t\\t\\t// During validation, however, the presence of an extraneous \\\"id\\\"\\n845: \\t\\t\\t// attribute in config will cause an error.\\n846: \\t\\t\\t// Remove this attribute so we do not generate an \\\"id\\\" attribute\\n847: \\t\\t\\t// where there is a risk that it is not in the real resource schema.\\n848: \\t\\t\\t//\\n849: \\t\\t\\t// TRADEOFF: Resources in which there actually is an\\n850: \\t\\t\\t// Optional+Computed \\\"id\\\" attribute in the schema will have that\\n851: \\t\\t\\t// attribute missing from generated config.\\n852: \\t\\t\\tconfigschema.FilterHelperSchemaIdAttribute,\\n853: \\t\\t),\\n854: \\t\\tconfigschema.FilterDeprecatedBlock,\\n855: \\t)\\n856: \\n857: \\tproviderAddr := addrs.LocalProviderConfig{\\n858: \\t\\tLocalName: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.Type,\\n859: \\t\\tAlias:     n.ResolvedProvider.Alias,\\n860: \\t}\\n861: \\n862: \\treturn genconfig.GenerateResourceContents(addr, filteredSchema, providerAddr, state.Value)\\n863: }\\n864: \\n865: // mergeDeps returns the union of 2 sets of dependencies\\n866: func mergeDeps(a, b []addrs.ConfigResource) []addrs.ConfigResource {\\n867: \\tswitch {\\n868: \\tcase len(a) == 0:\\n869: \\t\\treturn b\\n870: \\tcase len(b) == 0:\\n871: \\t\\treturn a\\n872: \\t}\\n873: \\n874: \\tset := make(map[string]addrs.ConfigResource)\\n875: \\n876: \\tfor _, dep := range a {\\n877: \\t\\tset[dep.String()] = dep\\n878: \\t}\\n879: \\n880: \\tfor _, dep := range b {\\n881: \\t\\tset[dep.String()] = dep\\n882: \\t}\\n883: \\n884: \\tnewDeps := make([]addrs.ConfigResource, 0, len(set))\\n885: \\tfor _, dep := range set {\\n886: \\t\\tnewDeps = append(newDeps, dep)\\n887: \\t}\\n888: \\n889: \\treturn newDeps\\n890: }\\n891: \\n892: func depsEqual(a, b []addrs.ConfigResource) bool {\\n893: \\tif len(a) != len(b) {\\n894: \\t\\treturn false\\n895: \\t}\\n896: \\n897: \\t// Because we need to sort the deps to compare equality, make shallow\\n898: \\t// copies to prevent concurrently modifying the array values on\\n899: \\t// dependencies shared between expanded instances.\\n900: \\tcopyA, copyB := make([]addrs.ConfigResource, len(a)), make([]addrs.ConfigResource, len(b))\\n901: \\tcopy(copyA, a)\\n902: \\tcopy(copyB, b)\\n903: \\ta, b = copyA, copyB\\n904: \\n905: \\tless := func(s []addrs.ConfigResource) func(i, j int) bool {\\n906: \\t\\treturn func(i, j int) bool {\\n907: \\t\\t\\treturn s[i].String() < s[j].String()\\n908: \\t\\t}\\n909: \\t}\\n910: \\n911: \\tsort.Slice(a, less(a))\\n912: \\tsort.Slice(b, less(b))\\n913: \\n914: \\tfor i := range a {\\n915: \\t\\tif !a[i].Equal(b[i]) {\\n916: \\t\\t\\treturn false\\n917: \\t\\t}\\n918: \\t}\\n919: \\treturn true\\n920: }\\n921: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_resource_plan_instance.go\"}"

Input
{
  "path": "internal/terraform/node_resource_destroy.go",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\\n2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\\n3: \\n4: package terraform\\n5: \\n6: import (\\n7: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n8: \\t\\\"log\\\"\\n9: \\n10: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\\\"\\n11: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\\\"\\n12: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\\\"\\n13: \\n14: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\\\"\\n15: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\\\"\\n16: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\\\"\\n17: )\\n18: \\n19: // NodeDestroyResourceInstance represents a resource instance that is to be\\n20: // destroyed.\\n21: type NodeDestroyResourceInstance struct {\\n22: \\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\\n23: }\\n24: \\n25: var (\\n26: \\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance      = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\\n27: \\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource      = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\\n28: \\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance    = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\\n29: \\t_ GraphNodeDestroyer           = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\\n30: \\t_ GraphNodeDestroyerCBD        = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\\n31: \\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable       = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\\n32: \\t_ GraphNodeReferencer          = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\\n33: \\t_ GraphNodeExecutable          = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\\n34: \\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer    = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\\n35: \\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\\n36: )\\n37: \\n38: func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) Name() string {\\n39: \\treturn n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String() + \\\" (destroy)\\\"\\n40: }\\n41: \\n42: func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) ProvidedBy() (addr addrs.ProviderConfig, exact bool) {\\n43: \\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.DataResourceMode {\\n44: \\t\\t// indicate that this node does not require a configured provider\\n45: \\t\\treturn nil, true\\n46: \\t}\\n47: \\treturn n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.ProvidedBy()\\n48: }\\n49: \\n50: // GraphNodeDestroyer\\n51: func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) DestroyAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\\n52: \\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\\n53: \\treturn &addr\\n54: }\\n55: \\n56: // GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\\n57: func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) CreateBeforeDestroy() bool {\\n58: \\t// State takes precedence during destroy.\\n59: \\t// If the resource was removed, there is no config to check.\\n60: \\t// If CBD was forced from descendant, it should be saved in the state\\n61: \\t// already.\\n62: \\tif s := n.instanceState; s != nil {\\n63: \\t\\tif s.Current != nil {\\n64: \\t\\t\\treturn s.Current.CreateBeforeDestroy\\n65: \\t\\t}\\n66: \\t}\\n67: \\n68: \\tif n.Config != nil && n.Config.Managed != nil {\\n69: \\t\\treturn n.Config.Managed.CreateBeforeDestroy\\n70: \\t}\\n71: \\n72: \\treturn false\\n73: }\\n74: \\n75: // GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\\n76: func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) ModifyCreateBeforeDestroy(v bool) error {\\n77: \\treturn nil\\n78: }\\n79: \\n80: // GraphNodeReferenceable, overriding NodeAbstractResource\\n81: func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable {\\n82: \\tnormalAddrs := n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.ReferenceableAddrs()\\n83: \\tdestroyAddrs := make([]addrs.Referenceable, len(normalAddrs))\\n84: \\n85: \\tphaseType := addrs.ResourceInstancePhaseDestroy\\n86: \\tif n.CreateBeforeDestroy() {\\n87: \\t\\tphaseType = addrs.ResourceInstancePhaseDestroyCBD\\n88: \\t}\\n89: \\n90: \\tfor i, normalAddr := range normalAddrs {\\n91: \\t\\tswitch ta := normalAddr.(type) {\\n92: \\t\\tcase addrs.Resource:\\n93: \\t\\t\\tdestroyAddrs[i] = ta.Phase(phaseType)\\n94: \\t\\tcase addrs.ResourceInstance:\\n95: \\t\\t\\tdestroyAddrs[i] = ta.Phase(phaseType)\\n96: \\t\\tdefault:\\n97: \\t\\t\\tdestroyAddrs[i] = normalAddr\\n98: \\t\\t}\\n99: \\t}\\n100: \\n101: \\treturn destroyAddrs\\n102: }\\n103: \\n104: // GraphNodeReferencer, overriding NodeAbstractResource\\n105: func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) References() []*addrs.Reference {\\n106: \\t// If we have a config, then we need to include destroy-time dependencies\\n107: \\tif c := n.Config; c != nil && c.Managed != nil {\\n108: \\t\\tvar result []*addrs.Reference\\n109: \\n110: \\t\\t// We include conn info and config for destroy time provisioners\\n111: \\t\\t// as dependencies that we have.\\n112: \\t\\tfor _, p := range c.Managed.Provisioners {\\n113: \\t\\t\\tschema := n.ProvisionerSchemas[p.Type]\\n114: \\n115: \\t\\t\\tif p.When == configs.ProvisionerWhenDestroy {\\n116: \\t\\t\\t\\tif p.Connection != nil {\\n117: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, ReferencesFromConfig(p.Connection.Config, connectionBlockSupersetSchema)...)\\n118: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n119: \\t\\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, ReferencesFromConfig(p.Config, schema)...)\\n120: \\t\\t\\t}\\n121: \\t\\t}\\n122: \\n123: \\t\\treturn result\\n124: \\t}\\n125: \\n126: \\treturn nil\\n127: }\\n128: \\n129: // GraphNodeExecutable\\n130: func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n131: \\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\\n132: \\n133: \\t// Eval info is different depending on what kind of resource this is\\n134: \\tswitch addr.Resource.Resource.Mode {\\n135: \\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\\n136: \\t\\treturn n.managedResourceExecute(ctx)\\n137: \\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\\n138: \\t\\treturn n.dataResourceExecute(ctx)\\n139: \\tdefault:\\n140: \\t\\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\\\"unsupported resource mode %s\\\", n.Config.Mode))\\n141: \\t}\\n142: }\\n143: \\n144: func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) managedResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n145: \\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\\n146: \\n147: \\t// Get our state\\n148: \\tis := n.instanceState\\n149: \\tif is == nil {\\n150: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[WARN] NodeDestroyResourceInstance for %s with no state\\\", addr)\\n151: \\t}\\n152: \\n153: \\t// These vars are updated through pointers at various stages below.\\n154: \\tvar changeApply *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\\n155: \\tvar state *states.ResourceInstanceObject\\n156: \\n157: \\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n158: \\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n159: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n160: \\t\\treturn diags\\n161: \\t}\\n162: \\n163: \\tchangeApply, err = n.readDiff(ctx, providerSchema)\\n164: \\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n165: \\tif changeApply == nil || diags.HasErrors() {\\n166: \\t\\treturn diags\\n167: \\t}\\n168: \\n169: \\tchangeApply = reducePlan(addr.Resource, changeApply, true)\\n170: \\t// reducePlan may have simplified our planned change\\n171: \\t// into a NoOp if it does not require destroying.\\n172: \\tif changeApply == nil || changeApply.Action == plans.NoOp {\\n173: \\t\\treturn diags\\n174: \\t}\\n175: \\n176: \\tstate, readDiags := n.readResourceInstanceState(ctx, addr)\\n177: \\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\\n178: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n179: \\t\\treturn diags\\n180: \\t}\\n181: \\n182: \\t// Exit early if the state object is null after reading the state\\n183: \\tif state == nil || state.Value.IsNull() {\\n184: \\t\\treturn diags\\n185: \\t}\\n186: \\n187: \\tdiags = diags.Append(n.preApplyHook(ctx, changeApply))\\n188: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n189: \\t\\treturn diags\\n190: \\t}\\n191: \\n192: \\t// Run destroy provisioners if not tainted\\n193: \\tif state.Status != states.ObjectTainted {\\n194: \\t\\tapplyProvisionersDiags := n.evalApplyProvisioners(ctx, state, false, configs.ProvisionerWhenDestroy)\\n195: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(applyProvisionersDiags)\\n196: \\t\\t// keep the diags separate from the main set until we handle the cleanup\\n197: \\n198: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n199: \\t\\t\\t// If we have a provisioning error, then we just call\\n200: \\t\\t\\t// the post-apply hook now.\\n201: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.postApplyHook(ctx, state, diags.Err()))\\n202: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n203: \\t\\t}\\n204: \\t}\\n205: \\n206: \\t// Managed resources need to be destroyed, while data sources\\n207: \\t// are only removed from state.\\n208: \\t// we pass a nil configuration to apply because we are destroying\\n209: \\ts, d := n.apply(ctx, state, changeApply, nil, instances.RepetitionData{}, false)\\n210: \\tstate, diags = s, diags.Append(d)\\n211: \\t// we don't return immediately here on error, so that the state can be\\n212: \\t// finalized\\n213: \\n214: \\terr = n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState)\\n215: \\tif err != nil {\\n216: \\t\\treturn diags.Append(err)\\n217: \\t}\\n218: \\n219: \\t// create the err value for postApplyHook\\n220: \\tdiags = diags.Append(n.postApplyHook(ctx, state, diags.Err()))\\n221: \\tdiags = diags.Append(updateStateHook(ctx))\\n222: \\treturn diags\\n223: }\\n224: \\n225: func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) dataResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n226: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] NodeDestroyResourceInstance: removing state object for %s\\\", n.Addr)\\n227: \\tctx.State().SetResourceInstanceCurrent(n.Addr, nil, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n228: \\treturn diags.Append(updateStateHook(ctx))\\n229: }\\n230: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_resource_destroy.go\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\\n2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\\n3: \\n4: package terraform\\n5: \\n6: import (\\n7: \\t\\\"log\\\"\\n8: \\n9: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\\\"\\n10: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\\\"\\n11: \\n12: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\\\"\\n13: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\\\"\\n14: )\\n15: \\n16: // NodeForgetResourceInstance represents a resource instance that is to be\\n17: // removed from state.\\n18: type NodeForgetResourceInstance struct {\\n19: \\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\\n20: }\\n21: \\n22: var (\\n23: \\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance      = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\\n24: \\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource      = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\\n25: \\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance    = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\\n26: \\t_ GraphNodeReferencer          = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\\n27: \\t_ GraphNodeExecutable          = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\\n28: \\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer    = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\\n29: \\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\\n30: \\t_ GraphNodeDestroyer           = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\\n31: )\\n32: \\n33: func (n *NodeForgetResourceInstance) DestroyAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\\n34: \\treturn &n.Addr\\n35: }\\n36: \\n37: func (n *NodeForgetResourceInstance) Name() string {\\n38: \\treturn n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String() + \\\" (forget)\\\"\\n39: }\\n40: \\n41: func (n *NodeForgetResourceInstance) ProvidedBy() (addr addrs.ProviderConfig, exact bool) {\\n42: \\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.DataResourceMode {\\n43: \\t\\t// Indicate that this node does not require a configured provider\\n44: \\t\\treturn nil, true\\n45: \\t}\\n46: \\treturn n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.ProvidedBy()\\n47: }\\n48: \\n49: // GraphNodeExecutable\\n50: func (n *NodeForgetResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n51: \\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\\n52: \\n53: \\tis := n.instanceState\\n54: \\tif is == nil {\\n55: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[WARN] NodeForgetResourceInstance for %s with no state\\\", addr)\\n56: \\t}\\n57: \\n58: \\tvar changeApply *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\\n59: \\tvar state *states.ResourceInstanceObject\\n60: \\n61: \\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n62: \\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n63: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n64: \\t\\treturn diags\\n65: \\t}\\n66: \\n67: \\tchangeApply, err = n.readDiff(ctx, providerSchema)\\n68: \\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n69: \\tif changeApply == nil || diags.HasErrors() {\\n70: \\t\\treturn diags\\n71: \\t}\\n72: \\n73: \\tstate, readDiags := n.readResourceInstanceState(ctx, addr)\\n74: \\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\\n75: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n76: \\t\\treturn diags\\n77: \\t}\\n78: \\n79: \\t// Exit early if state is already null\\n80: \\tif state == nil || state.Value.IsNull() {\\n81: \\t\\treturn diags\\n82: \\t}\\n83: \\n84: \\tctx.State().ForgetResourceInstanceCurrent(n.Addr)\\n85: \\n86: \\tdiags = diags.Append(updateStateHook(ctx))\\n87: \\treturn diags\\n88: }\\n89: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_resource_forget.go\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\\n2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\\n3: \\n4: package terraform\\n5: \\n6: import (\\n7: \\t\\\"log\\\"\\n8: \\n9: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\\\"\\n10: )\\n11: \\n12: // NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance represents a resource that is \\\"destroyable\\\":\\n13: // it is ready to be destroyed.\\n14: type NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance struct {\\n15: \\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\\n16: }\\n17: \\n18: var (\\n19: \\t_ GraphNodeExecutable = (*NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance)(nil)\\n20: )\\n21: \\n22: // GraphNodeExecutable\\n23: func (n *NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\n24: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance: removing state object for %s\\\", n.Addr)\\n25: \\tctx.State().SetResourceInstanceCurrent(n.Addr, nil, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n26: \\treturn nil\\n27: }\\n28: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_data_destroy.go\"}"

Input
{
  "path": "internal/terraform/transform_diff.go",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\\n2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\\n3: \\n4: package terraform\\n5: \\n6: import (\\n7: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n8: \\t\\\"log\\\"\\n9: \\n10: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\\\"\\n11: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\\\"\\n12: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\\\"\\n13: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\\\"\\n14: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\\\"\\n15: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\\\"\\n16: )\\n17: \\n18: // DiffTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds graph nodes representing\\n19: // each of the resource changes described in the given Changes object.\\n20: type DiffTransformer struct {\\n21: \\tConcrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\\n22: \\tState    *states.State\\n23: \\tChanges  *plans.ChangesSrc\\n24: \\tConfig   *configs.Config\\n25: }\\n26: \\n27: // return true if the given resource instance has either Preconditions or\\n28: // Postconditions defined in the configuration.\\n29: func (t *DiffTransformer) hasConfigConditions(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) bool {\\n30: \\t// unit tests may have no config\\n31: \\tif t.Config == nil {\\n32: \\t\\treturn false\\n33: \\t}\\n34: \\n35: \\tcfg := t.Config.DescendantForInstance(addr.Module)\\n36: \\tif cfg == nil {\\n37: \\t\\treturn false\\n38: \\t}\\n39: \\n40: \\tres := cfg.Module.ResourceByAddr(addr.ConfigResource().Resource)\\n41: \\tif res == nil {\\n42: \\t\\treturn false\\n43: \\t}\\n44: \\n45: \\treturn len(res.Preconditions) > 0 || len(res.Postconditions) > 0\\n46: }\\n47: \\n48: func (t *DiffTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\\n49: \\tif t.Changes == nil || len(t.Changes.Resources) == 0 {\\n50: \\t\\t// Nothing to do!\\n51: \\t\\treturn nil\\n52: \\t}\\n53: \\n54: \\t// Go through all the modules in the diff.\\n55: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer starting\\\")\\n56: \\n57: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n58: \\tstate := t.State\\n59: \\tchanges := t.Changes\\n60: \\n61: \\t// DiffTransformer creates resource _instance_ nodes. If there are any\\n62: \\t// whole-resource nodes already in the graph, we must ensure that they\\n63: \\t// get evaluated before any of the corresponding instances by creating\\n64: \\t// dependency edges, so we'll do some prep work here to ensure we'll only\\n65: \\t// create connections to nodes that existed before we started here.\\n66: \\tresourceNodes := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.ConfigResource, []GraphNodeConfigResource]()\\n67: \\tfor _, node := range g.Vertices() {\\n68: \\t\\trn, ok := node.(GraphNodeConfigResource)\\n69: \\t\\tif !ok {\\n70: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n71: \\t\\t}\\n72: \\t\\t// We ignore any instances that _also_ implement\\n73: \\t\\t// GraphNodeResourceInstance, since in the unlikely event that they\\n74: \\t\\t// do exist we'd probably end up creating cycles by connecting them.\\n75: \\t\\tif _, ok := node.(GraphNodeResourceInstance); ok {\\n76: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n77: \\t\\t}\\n78: \\n79: \\t\\trAddr := rn.ResourceAddr()\\n80: \\t\\tresourceNodes.Put(rAddr, append(resourceNodes.Get(rAddr), rn))\\n81: \\t}\\n82: \\n83: \\tfor _, rc := range changes.Resources {\\n84: \\t\\taddr := rc.Addr\\n85: \\t\\tdk := rc.DeposedKey\\n86: \\n87: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: found %s change for %s %s\\\", rc.Action, addr, dk)\\n88: \\n89: \\t\\t// Depending on the action we'll need some different combinations of\\n90: \\t\\t// nodes, because destroying uses a special node type separate from\\n91: \\t\\t// other actions.\\n92: \\t\\tvar update, delete, forget, createBeforeDestroy bool\\n93: \\t\\tswitch rc.Action {\\n94: \\t\\tcase plans.NoOp:\\n95: \\t\\t\\t// For a no-op change we don't take any action but we still\\n96: \\t\\t\\t// run any condition checks associated with the object, to\\n97: \\t\\t\\t// make sure that they still hold when considering the\\n98: \\t\\t\\t// results of other changes.\\n99: \\t\\t\\tupdate = t.hasConfigConditions(addr)\\n100: \\t\\tcase plans.Delete:\\n101: \\t\\t\\tdelete = true\\n102: \\t\\tcase plans.DeleteThenCreate, plans.CreateThenDelete:\\n103: \\t\\t\\tupdate = true\\n104: \\t\\t\\tdelete = true\\n105: \\t\\t\\tcreateBeforeDestroy = (rc.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete)\\n106: \\t\\tcase plans.Forget:\\n107: \\t\\t\\tforget = true\\n108: \\t\\tdefault:\\n109: \\t\\t\\tupdate = true\\n110: \\t\\t}\\n111: \\n112: \\t\\t// A deposed instance may only have a change of Delete, Forget, or NoOp.\\n113: \\t\\t// A NoOp can happen if the provider shows it no longer exists during\\n114: \\t\\t// the most recent ReadResource operation.\\n115: \\t\\tif dk != states.NotDeposed && !(rc.Action == plans.Delete || rc.Action == plans.Forget || rc.Action == plans.NoOp) {\\n116: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n117: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n118: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Invalid planned change for deposed object\\\",\\n119: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"The plan contains a non-remove change for %s deposed object %s. The only valid actions for a deposed object are to destroy it or remove it from state, so this is a bug in Terraform.\\\", addr, dk),\\n120: \\t\\t\\t))\\n121: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n122: \\t\\t}\\n123: \\n124: \\t\\t// If we're going to do a create_before_destroy Replace operation then\\n125: \\t\\t// we need to allocate a DeposedKey to use to retain the\\n126: \\t\\t// not-yet-destroyed prior object, so that the delete node can destroy\\n127: \\t\\t// _that_ rather than the newly-created node, which will be current\\n128: \\t\\t// by the time the delete node is visited.\\n129: \\t\\tif update && delete && createBeforeDestroy {\\n130: \\t\\t\\t// In this case, variable dk will be the _pre-assigned_ DeposedKey\\n131: \\t\\t\\t// that must be used if the update graph node deposes the current\\n132: \\t\\t\\t// instance, which will then align with the same key we pass\\n133: \\t\\t\\t// into the destroy node to ensure we destroy exactly the deposed\\n134: \\t\\t\\t// object we expect.\\n135: \\t\\t\\tif state != nil {\\n136: \\t\\t\\t\\tris := state.ResourceInstance(addr)\\n137: \\t\\t\\t\\tif ris == nil {\\n138: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Should never happen, since we don't plan to replace an\\n139: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// instance that doesn't exist yet.\\n140: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n141: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n142: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Invalid planned change\\\",\\n143: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"The plan contains a replace change for %s, which doesn't exist yet. This is a bug in Terraform.\\\", addr),\\n144: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t))\\n145: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n146: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n147: \\n148: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Allocating a deposed key separately from using it can be racy\\n149: \\t\\t\\t\\t// in general, but we assume here that nothing except the apply\\n150: \\t\\t\\t\\t// node we instantiate below will actually make new deposed objects\\n151: \\t\\t\\t\\t// in practice, and so the set of already-used keys will not change\\n152: \\t\\t\\t\\t// between now and then.\\n153: \\t\\t\\t\\tdk = ris.FindUnusedDeposedKey()\\n154: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n155: \\t\\t\\t\\t// If we have no state at all yet then we can use _any_\\n156: \\t\\t\\t\\t// DeposedKey.\\n157: \\t\\t\\t\\tdk = states.NewDeposedKey()\\n158: \\t\\t\\t}\\n159: \\t\\t}\\n160: \\n161: \\t\\tif update {\\n162: \\t\\t\\t// All actions except destroying the node type chosen by t.Concrete\\n163: \\t\\t\\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\\n164: \\t\\t\\tvar node dag.Vertex = abstract\\n165: \\t\\t\\tif f := t.Concrete; f != nil {\\n166: \\t\\t\\t\\tnode = f(abstract)\\n167: \\t\\t\\t}\\n168: \\n169: \\t\\t\\tif createBeforeDestroy {\\n170: \\t\\t\\t\\t// We'll attach our pre-allocated DeposedKey to the node if\\n171: \\t\\t\\t\\t// it supports that. NodeApplyableResourceInstance is the\\n172: \\t\\t\\t\\t// specific concrete node type we are looking for here really,\\n173: \\t\\t\\t\\t// since that's the only node type that might depose objects.\\n174: \\t\\t\\t\\tif dn, ok := node.(GraphNodeDeposer); ok {\\n175: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdn.SetPreallocatedDeposedKey(dk)\\n176: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n177: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented by %s, deposing prior object to %s\\\", addr, dag.VertexName(node), dk)\\n178: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n179: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented by %s\\\", addr, dag.VertexName(node))\\n180: \\t\\t\\t}\\n181: \\n182: \\t\\t\\tg.Add(node)\\n183: \\t\\t\\tfor _, rsrcNode := range resourceNodes.Get(addr.ConfigResource()) {\\n184: \\t\\t\\t\\tg.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(node, rsrcNode))\\n185: \\t\\t\\t}\\n186: \\t\\t}\\n187: \\n188: \\t\\tif delete {\\n189: \\t\\t\\t// Destroying always uses a destroy-specific node type, though\\n190: \\t\\t\\t// which one depends on whether we're destroying a current object\\n191: \\t\\t\\t// or a deposed object.\\n192: \\t\\t\\tvar node GraphNodeResourceInstance\\n193: \\t\\t\\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\\n194: \\t\\t\\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\\n195: \\t\\t\\t\\tnode = &NodeDestroyResourceInstance{\\n196: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\\n197: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n198: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n199: \\t\\t\\t\\tnode = &NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\\n200: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\\n201: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDeposedKey:                   dk,\\n202: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n203: \\t\\t\\t}\\n204: \\t\\t\\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\\n205: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented for destruction by %s\\\", addr, dag.VertexName(node))\\n206: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n207: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s deposed object %s will be represented for destruction by %s\\\", addr, dk, dag.VertexName(node))\\n208: \\t\\t\\t}\\n209: \\t\\t\\tg.Add(node)\\n210: \\t\\t}\\n211: \\n212: \\t\\tif forget {\\n213: \\t\\t\\tvar node GraphNodeResourceInstance\\n214: \\t\\t\\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\\n215: \\t\\t\\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\\n216: \\t\\t\\t\\tnode = &NodeForgetResourceInstance{\\n217: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\\n218: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n219: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n220: \\t\\t\\t\\tnode = &NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\\n221: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\\n222: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDeposedKey:                   dk,\\n223: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n224: \\t\\t\\t}\\n225: \\n226: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented for forgetting by %s\\\", addr, dag.VertexName(node))\\n227: \\t\\t\\tg.Add(node)\\n228: \\t\\t}\\n229: \\n230: \\t}\\n231: \\n232: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer complete\\\")\\n233: \\n234: \\treturn diags.Err()\\n235: }\\n236: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/transform_diff.go\"}"
21. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "internal/terraform/transform_state.go",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\\n2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\\n3: \\n4: package terraform\\n5: \\n6: import (\\n7: \\t\\\"log\\\"\\n8: \\n9: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\\\"\\n10: )\\n11: \\n12: // StateTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds the elements of\\n13: // the state to the graph.\\n14: //\\n15: // This transform is used for example by the DestroyPlanGraphBuilder to ensure\\n16: // that only resources that are in the state are represented in the graph.\\n17: type StateTransformer struct {\\n18: \\t// ConcreteCurrent and ConcreteDeposed are used to specialize the abstract\\n19: \\t// resource instance nodes that this transformer will create.\\n20: \\t//\\n21: \\t// If either of these is nil, the objects of that type will be skipped and\\n22: \\t// not added to the graph at all. It doesn't make sense to use this\\n23: \\t// transformer without setting at least one of these, since that would\\n24: \\t// skip everything and thus be a no-op.\\n25: \\tConcreteCurrent ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\\n26: \\tConcreteDeposed ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposedNodeFunc\\n27: \\n28: \\tState *states.State\\n29: }\\n30: \\n31: func (t *StateTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\\n32: \\tif t.State == nil {\\n33: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: state is nil, so nothing to do\\\")\\n34: \\t\\treturn nil\\n35: \\t}\\n36: \\n37: \\tswitch {\\n38: \\tcase t.ConcreteCurrent != nil && t.ConcreteDeposed != nil:\\n39: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: creating nodes for both current and deposed instance objects\\\")\\n40: \\tcase t.ConcreteCurrent != nil:\\n41: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: creating nodes for current instance objects only\\\")\\n42: \\tcase t.ConcreteDeposed != nil:\\n43: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: creating nodes for deposed instance objects only\\\")\\n44: \\tdefault:\\n45: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: pointless no-op call, creating no nodes at all\\\")\\n46: \\t}\\n47: \\n48: \\tfor _, ms := range t.State.Modules {\\n49: \\t\\tfor _, rs := range ms.Resources {\\n50: \\t\\t\\tresourceAddr := rs.Addr\\n51: \\n52: \\t\\t\\tfor key, is := range rs.Instances {\\n53: \\t\\t\\t\\taddr := resourceAddr.Instance(key)\\n54: \\n55: \\t\\t\\t\\tif obj := is.Current; obj != nil && t.ConcreteCurrent != nil {\\n56: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\\n57: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tnode := t.ConcreteCurrent(abstract)\\n58: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tg.Add(node)\\n59: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: added %T for %s current object\\\", node, addr)\\n60: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n61: \\n62: \\t\\t\\t\\tif t.ConcreteDeposed != nil {\\n63: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfor dk := range is.Deposed {\\n64: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\\n65: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tnode := t.ConcreteDeposed(abstract, dk)\\n66: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tg.Add(node)\\n67: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: added %T for %s deposed object %s\\\", node, addr, dk)\\n68: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n69: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n70: \\t\\t\\t}\\n71: \\t\\t}\\n72: \\t}\\n73: \\n74: \\treturn nil\\n75: }\\n76: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/transform_state.go\"}"
22. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "internal/terraform/transform_deferred.go",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\\n2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\\n3: \\n4: package terraform\\n5: \\n6: import (\\n7: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\\\"\\n8: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\\\"\\n9: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\\\"\\n10: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\\\"\\n11: )\\n12: \\n13: // DeferredTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds graph nodes representing\\n14: // each of the deferred changes to the graph.\\n15: //\\n16: // Deferred changes are not executed during the apply phase, but they are\\n17: // tracked in the graph to ensure that the correct ordering is maintained and\\n18: // the target flags are correctly applied.\\n19: type DeferredTransformer struct {\\n20: \\tDeferredChanges []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc\\n21: }\\n22: \\n23: func (t *DeferredTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\\n24: \\tif len(t.DeferredChanges) == 0 {\\n25: \\t\\treturn nil\\n26: \\t}\\n27: \\n28: \\t// As with the DiffTransformer, DeferredTransformer creates resource\\n29: \\t// instance nodes. If there are any whole-resource nodes already in the\\n30: \\t// graph, we must ensure they get evaluated before any of the corresponding\\n31: \\t// instances by creating dependency edges.\\n32: \\tresourceNodes := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.ConfigResource, []GraphNodeConfigResource]()\\n33: \\tfor _, node := range g.Vertices() {\\n34: \\t\\trn, ok := node.(GraphNodeConfigResource)\\n35: \\t\\tif !ok {\\n36: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n37: \\t\\t}\\n38: \\t\\t// We ignore any instances that _also_ implement\\n39: \\t\\t// GraphNodeResourceInstance, since in the unlikely event that they\\n40: \\t\\t// do exist we'd probably end up creating cycles by connecting them.\\n41: \\t\\tif _, ok := node.(GraphNodeResourceInstance); ok {\\n42: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n43: \\t\\t}\\n44: \\n45: \\t\\trAddr := rn.ResourceAddr()\\n46: \\t\\tresourceNodes.Put(rAddr, append(resourceNodes.Get(rAddr), rn))\\n47: \\t}\\n48: \\n49: \\tfor _, change := range t.DeferredChanges {\\n50: \\t\\tnode := &nodeApplyableDeferredInstance{\\n51: \\t\\t\\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(change.ChangeSrc.Addr),\\n52: \\t\\t\\tReason:                       change.DeferredReason,\\n53: \\t\\t\\tChangeSrc:                    change.ChangeSrc,\\n54: \\t\\t}\\n55: \\n56: \\t\\t// Create a special node for partial instances, that handles the\\n57: \\t\\t// addresses a little differently.\\n58: \\t\\tif change.DeferredReason == providers.DeferredReasonInstanceCountUnknown {\\n59: \\t\\t\\tper := change.ChangeSrc.Addr.PartialResource()\\n60: \\n61: \\t\\t\\t// This is a partial instance, so we need to create a partial node\\n62: \\t\\t\\t// instead of a full instance node.\\n63: \\t\\t\\tnode := &nodeApplyableDeferredPartialInstance{\\n64: \\t\\t\\t\\tnodeApplyableDeferredInstance: node,\\n65: \\t\\t\\t\\tPartialAddr:                   per,\\n66: \\t\\t\\t}\\n67: \\t\\t\\tg.Add(node)\\n68: \\n69: \\t\\t\\t// Now we want to find the expansion node that would be applied for\\n70: \\t\\t\\t// this resource, and tell it that it is performing a partial\\n71: \\t\\t\\t// expansion.\\n72: \\t\\t\\tfor _, v := range g.Vertices() {\\n73: \\t\\t\\t\\tif n, ok := v.(*nodeExpandApplyableResource); ok {\\n74: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif per.ConfigResource().Equal(n.Addr) {\\n75: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tn.PartialExpansions = append(n.PartialExpansions, per)\\n76: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n77: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n78: \\t\\t\\t}\\n79: \\n80: \\t\\t\\t// Also connect the deferred instance node to the underlying\\n81: \\t\\t\\t// resource node to make sure any expansion happens first.\\n82: \\t\\t\\tfor _, resourceNode := range resourceNodes.Get(node.Addr.ConfigResource()) {\\n83: \\t\\t\\t\\tg.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(node, resourceNode))\\n84: \\t\\t\\t}\\n85: \\n86: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n87: \\t\\t}\\n88: \\n89: \\t\\t// Otherwise, just add the normal deferred instance node.\\n90: \\t\\tg.Add(node)\\n91: \\n92: \\t\\t// Still connect the deferred instance node to the underlying resource\\n93: \\t\\t// node.\\n94: \\t\\tfor _, resourceNode := range resourceNodes.Get(node.Addr.ConfigResource()) {\\n95: \\t\\t\\tg.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(node, resourceNode))\\n96: \\t\\t}\\n97: \\t}\\n98: \\n99: \\treturn nil\\n100: }\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/transform_deferred.go\"}"
23. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "internal/terraform/transform_orphan_count.go",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\\n2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\\n3: \\n4: package terraform\\n5: \\n6: import (\\n7: \\t\\\"log\\\"\\n8: \\n9: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\\\"\\n10: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\\\"\\n11: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\\\"\\n12: )\\n13: \\n14: // OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds orphans\\n15: // for an expanded count to the graph.
