38. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "\n# Create a Python script to save the remaining files from the data we retrieved earlier\npython3 << 'PYSCRIPT'\nimport json\n\n# Files that were successfully retrieved (based on JSON responses)\nfiles_content = {\n    'node_resource_destroy.go': '''// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n)\n\n// NodeDestroyResourceInstance represents a resource instance that is to be\n// destroyed.\ntype NodeDestroyResourceInstance struct {\n\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance      = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource      = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance    = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeDestroyer           = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeDestroyerCBD        = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable       = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferencer          = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeExecutable          = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer    = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer = (*NodeDestroyResourceInstance)(nil)\n)\n\nfunc (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) Name() string {\n\treturn n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String() + \" (destroy)\"\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) ProvidedBy() (addr addrs.ProviderConfig, exact bool) {\n\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.DataResourceMode {\n\t\t// indicate that this node does not require a configured provider\n\t\treturn nil, true\n\t}\n\treturn n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.ProvidedBy()\n}\n\n// GraphNodeDestroyer\nfunc (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) DestroyAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\treturn &addr\n}\n\n// GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\nfunc (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) CreateBeforeDestroy() bool {\n\t// State takes precedence during destroy.\n\t// If the resource was removed, there is no config to check.\n\t// If CBD was forced from descendant, it should be saved in the state\n\t// already.\n\tif s := n.instanceState; s != nil {\n\t\tif s.Current != nil {\n\t\t\treturn s.Current.CreateBeforeDestroy\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif n.Config != nil && n.Config.Managed != nil {\n\t\treturn n.Config.Managed.CreateBeforeDestroy\n\t}\n\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\nfunc (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) ModifyCreateBeforeDestroy(v bool) error {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// GraphNodeReferenceable, overriding NodeAbstractResource\nfunc (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable {\n\tnormalAddrs := n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.ReferenceableAddrs()\n\tdestroyAddrs := make([]addrs.Referenceable, len(normalAddrs))\n\n\tphaseType := addrs.ResourceInstancePhaseDestroy\n\tif n.CreateBeforeDestroy() {\n\t\tphaseType = addrs.ResourceInstancePhaseDestroyCBD\n\t}\n\n\tfor i, normalAddr := range normalAddrs {\n\t\tswitch ta := normalAddr.(type) {\n\t\tcase addrs.Resource:\n\t\t\tdestroyAddrs[i] = ta.Phase(phaseType)\n\t\tcase addrs.ResourceInstance:\n\t\t\tdestroyAddrs[i] = ta.Phase(phaseType)\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tdestroyAddrs[i] = normalAddr\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn destroyAddrs\n}\n\n// GraphNodeReferencer, overriding NodeAbstractResource\nfunc (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n\t// If we have a config, then we need to include destroy-time dependencies\n\tif c := n.Config; c != nil && c.Managed != nil {\n\t\tvar result []*addrs.Reference\n\n\t\t// We include conn info and config for destroy time provisioners\n\t\t// as dependencies that we have.\n\t\tfor _, p := range c.Managed.Provisioners {\n\t\t\tschema := n.ProvisionerSchemas[p.Type]\n\n\t\t\tif p.When == configs.ProvisionerWhenDestroy {\n\t\t\t\tif p.Connection != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, ReferencesFromConfig(p.Connection.Config, connectionBlockSupersetSchema)...)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, ReferencesFromConfig(p.Config, schema)...)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn result\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// GraphNodeExecutable\nfunc (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\n\t// Eval info is different depending on what kind of resource this is\n\tswitch addr.Resource.Resource.Mode {\n\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\n\t\treturn n.managedResourceExecute(ctx)\n\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\n\t\treturn n.dataResourceExecute(ctx)\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported resource mode %s\", n.Config.Mode))\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) managedResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\n\t// Get our state\n\tis := n.instanceState\n\tif is == nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] NodeDestroyResourceInstance for %s with no state\", addr)\n\t}\n\n\t// These vars are updated through pointers at various stages below.\n\tvar changeApply *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\n\tvar state *states.ResourceInstanceObject\n\n\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tchangeApply, err = n.readDiff(ctx, providerSchema)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif changeApply == nil || diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tchangeApply = reducePlan(addr.Resource, changeApply, true)\n\t// reducePlan may have simplified our planned change\n\t// into a NoOp if it does not require destroying.\n\tif changeApply == nil || changeApply.Action == plans.NoOp {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tstate, readDiags := n.readResourceInstanceState(ctx, addr)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Exit early if the state object is null after reading the state\n\tif state == nil || state.Value.IsNull() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(n.preApplyHook(ctx, changeApply))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Run destroy provisioners if not tainted\n\tif state.Status != states.ObjectTainted {\n\t\tapplyProvisionersDiags := n.evalApplyProvisioners(ctx, state, false, configs.ProvisionerWhenDestroy)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(applyProvisionersDiags)\n\t\t// keep the diags separate from the main set until we handle the cleanup\n\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t// If we have a provisioning error, then we just call\n\t\t\t// the post-apply hook now.\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.postApplyHook(ctx, state, diags.Err()))\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Managed resources need to be destroyed, while data sources\n\t// are only removed from state.\n\t// we pass a nil configuration to apply because we are destroying\n\ts, d := n.apply(ctx, state, changeApply, nil, instances.RepetitionData{}, false)\n\tstate, diags = s, diags.Append(d)\n\t// we don't return immediately here on error, so that the state can be\n\t// finalized\n\n\terr = n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\t// create the err value for postApplyHook\n\tdiags = diags.Append(n.postApplyHook(ctx, state, diags.Err()))\n\tdiags = diags.Append(updateStateHook(ctx))\n\treturn diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) dataResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] NodeDestroyResourceInstance: removing state object for %s\", n.Addr)\n\tctx.State().SetResourceInstanceCurrent(n.Addr, nil, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\treturn diags.Append(updateStateHook(ctx))\n}\n''',\n\n    'node_resource_forget.go': '''// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n)\n\n// NodeForgetResourceInstance represents a resource instance that is to be\n// removed from state.\ntype NodeForgetResourceInstance struct {\n\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance      = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource      = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance    = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferencer          = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeExecutable          = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer    = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeDestroyer           = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n)\n\nfunc (n *NodeForgetResourceInstance) DestroyAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n\treturn &n.Addr\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeForgetResourceInstance) Name() string {\n\treturn n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String() + \" (forget)\"\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeForgetResourceInstance) ProvidedBy() (addr addrs.ProviderConfig, exact bool) {\n\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.DataResourceMode {\n\t\t// Indicate that this node does not require a configured provider\n\t\treturn nil, true\n\t}\n\treturn n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.ProvidedBy()\n}\n\n// GraphNodeExecutable\nfunc (n *NodeForgetResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\n\tis := n.instanceState\n\tif is == nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] NodeForgetResourceInstance for %s with no state\", addr)\n\t}\n\n\tvar changeApply *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\n\tvar state *states.ResourceInstanceObject\n\n\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tchangeApply, err = n.readDiff(ctx, providerSchema)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif changeApply == nil || diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tstate, readDiags := n.readResourceInstanceState(ctx, addr)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Exit early if state is already null\n\tif state == nil || state.Value.IsNull() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tctx.State().ForgetResourceInstanceCurrent(n.Addr)\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(updateStateHook(ctx))\n\treturn diags\n}\n''',\n\n    'node_data_destroy.go': '''// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance represents a resource that is \"destroyable\":\n// it is ready to be destroyed.\ntype NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance struct {\n\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeExecutable = (*NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance)(nil)\n)\n\n// GraphNodeExecutable\nfunc (n *NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] NodeDestroyableDataResourceInstance: removing state object for %s\", n.Addr)\n\tctx.State().SetResourceInstanceCurrent(n.Addr, nil, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\treturn nil\n}\n''',\n}\n\n# Save all files\nfor filename, content in files_content.items():\n    filepath = f'/workspace/internal/terraform/{filename}'\n    with open(filepath, 'w') as f:\n        f.write(content)\n    print(f\"Saved {filename}\")\n\nprint(\"Completed saving basic node files\")\nPYSCRIPT\n"
}
Output
"Saved node_resource_destroy.go\nSaved node_resource_forget.go\nSaved node_data_destroy.go\nCompleted saving basic node files"
39. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// DiffTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds graph nodes representing\n// each of the resource changes described in the given Changes object.\ntype DiffTransformer struct {\n\tConcrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n\tState    *states.State\n\tChanges  *plans.ChangesSrc\n\tConfig   *configs.Config\n}\n\n// return true if the given resource instance has either Preconditions or\n// Postconditions defined in the configuration.\nfunc (t *DiffTransformer) hasConfigConditions(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) bool {\n\t// unit tests may have no config\n\tif t.Config == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tcfg := t.Config.DescendantForInstance(addr.Module)\n\tif cfg == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tres := cfg.Module.ResourceByAddr(addr.ConfigResource().Resource)\n\tif res == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn len(res.Preconditions) > 0 || len(res.Postconditions) > 0\n}\n\nfunc (t *DiffTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n\tif t.Changes == nil || len(t.Changes.Resources) == 0 {\n\t\t// Nothing to do!\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Go through all the modules in the diff.\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer starting\")\n\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tstate := t.State\n\tchanges := t.Changes\n\n\t// DiffTransformer creates resource _instance_ nodes. If there are any\n\t// whole-resource nodes already in the graph, we must ensure that they\n\t// get evaluated before any of the corresponding instances by creating\n\t// dependency edges, so we'll do some prep work here to ensure we'll only\n\t// create connections to nodes that existed before we started here.\n\tresourceNodes := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.ConfigResource, []GraphNodeConfigResource]()\n\tfor _, node := range g.Vertices() {\n\t\trn, ok := node.(GraphNodeConfigResource)\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\t// We ignore any instances that _also_ implement\n\t\t// GraphNodeResourceInstance, since in the unlikely event that they\n\t\t// do exist we'd probably end up creating cycles by connecting them.\n\t\tif _, ok := node.(GraphNodeResourceInstance); ok {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\trAddr := rn.ResourceAddr()\n\t\tresourceNodes.Put(rAddr, append(resourceNodes.Get(rAddr), rn))\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, rc := range changes.Resources {\n\t\taddr := rc.Addr\n\t\tdk := rc.DeposedKey\n\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: found %s change for %s %s\", rc.Action, addr, dk)\n\n\t\t// Depending on the action we'll need some different combinations of\n\t\t// nodes, because destroying uses a special node type separate from\n\t\t// other actions.\n\t\tvar update, delete, forget, createBeforeDestroy bool\n\t\tswitch rc.Action {\n\t\tcase plans.NoOp:\n\t\t\t// For a no-op change we don't take any action but we still\n\t\t\t// run any condition checks associated with the object, to\n\t\t\t// make sure that they still hold when considering the\n\t\t\t// results of other changes.\n\t\t\tupdate = t.hasConfigConditions(addr)\n\t\tcase plans.Delete:\n\t\t\tdelete = true\n\t\tcase plans.DeleteThenCreate, plans.CreateThenDelete:\n\t\t\tupdate = true\n\t\t\tdelete = true\n\t\t\tcreateBeforeDestroy = (rc.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete)\n\t\tcase plans.Forget:\n\t\t\tforget = true\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tupdate = true\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// A deposed instance may only have a change of Delete, Forget, or NoOp.\n\t\t// A NoOp can happen if the provider shows it no longer exists during\n\t\t// the most recent ReadResource operation.\n\t\tif dk != states.NotDeposed && !(rc.Action == plans.Delete || rc.Action == plans.Forget || rc.Action == plans.NoOp) {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Invalid planned change for deposed object\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"The plan contains a non-remove change for %s deposed object %s. The only valid actions for a deposed object are to destroy it or remove it from state, so this is a bug in Terraform.\", addr, dk),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// If we're going to do a create_before_destroy Replace operation then\n\t\t// we need to allocate a DeposedKey to use to retain the\n\t\t// not-yet-destroyed prior object, so that the delete node can destroy\n\t\t// _that_ rather than the newly-created node, which will be current\n\t\t// by the time the delete node is visited.\n\t\tif update && delete && createBeforeDestroy {\n\t\t\t// In this case, variable dk will be the _pre-assigned_ DeposedKey\n\t\t\t// that must be used if the update graph node deposes the current\n\t\t\t// instance, which will then align with the same key we pass\n\t\t\t// into the destroy node to ensure we destroy exactly the deposed\n\t\t\t// object we expect.\n\t\t\tif state != nil {\n\t\t\t\tris := state.ResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\t\t\tif ris == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Should never happen, since we don't plan to replace an\n\t\t\t\t\t// instance that doesn't exist yet.\n\t\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Invalid planned change\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"The plan contains a replace change for %s, which doesn't exist yet. This is a bug in Terraform.\", addr),\n\t\t\t\t\t))\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Allocating a deposed key separately from using it can be racy\n\t\t\t\t// in general, but we assume here that nothing except the apply\n\t\t\t\t// node we instantiate below will actually make new deposed objects\n\t\t\t\t// in practice, and so the set of already-used keys will not change\n\t\t\t\t// between now and then.\n\t\t\t\tdk = ris.FindUnusedDeposedKey()\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// If we have no state at all yet then we can use _any_\n\t\t\t\t// DeposedKey.\n\t\t\t\tdk = states.NewDeposedKey()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif update {\n\t\t\t// All actions except destroying the node type chosen by t.Concrete\n\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\t\tvar node dag.Vertex = abstract\n\t\t\tif f := t.Concrete; f != nil {\n\t\t\t\tnode = f(abstract)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif createBeforeDestroy {\n\t\t\t\t// We'll attach our pre-allocated DeposedKey to the node if\n\t\t\t\t// it supports that. NodeApplyableResourceInstance is the\n\t\t\t\t// specific concrete node type we are looking for here really,\n\t\t\t\t// since that's the only node type that might depose objects.\n\t\t\t\tif dn, ok := node.(GraphNodeDeposer); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tdn.SetPreallocatedDeposedKey(dk)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented by %s, deposing prior object to %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node), dk)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented by %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node))\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n\t\t\tfor _, rsrcNode := range resourceNodes.Get(addr.ConfigResource()) {\n\t\t\t\tg.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(node, rsrcNode))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif delete {\n\t\t\t// Destroying always uses a destroy-specific node type, though\n\t\t\t// which one depends on whether we're destroying a current object\n\t\t\t// or a deposed object.\n\t\t\tvar node GraphNodeResourceInstance\n\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\t\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeDestroyResourceInstance{\n\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n\t\t\t\t\tDeposedKey:                   dk,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented for destruction by %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node))\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s deposed object %s will be represented for destruction by %s\", addr, dk, dag.VertexName(node))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif forget {\n\t\t\tvar node GraphNodeResourceInstance\n\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\t\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeForgetResourceInstance{\n\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n\t\t\t\t\tDeposedKey:                   dk,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented for forgetting by %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node))\n\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n\t\t}\n\n\t}\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer complete\")\n\n\treturn diags.Err()\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/transform_diff.go"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/internal/terraform/transform_diff.go"
40. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n)\n\n// StateTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds the elements of\n// the state to the graph.\n//\n// This transform is used for example by the DestroyPlanGraphBuilder to ensure\n// that only resources that are in the state are represented in the graph.\ntype StateTransformer struct {\n\t// ConcreteCurrent and ConcreteDeposed are used to specialize the abstract\n\t// resource instance nodes that this transformer will create.\n\t//\n\t// If either of these is nil, the objects of that type will be skipped and\n\t// not added to the graph at all. It doesn't make sense to use this\n\t// transformer without setting at least one of these, since that would\n\t// skip everything and thus be a no-op.\n\tConcreteCurrent ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n\tConcreteDeposed ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposedNodeFunc\n\n\tState *states.State\n}\n\nfunc (t *StateTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n\tif t.State == nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: state is nil, so nothing to do\")\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tswitch {\n\tcase t.ConcreteCurrent != nil && t.ConcreteDeposed != nil:\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: creating nodes for both current and deposed instance objects\")\n\tcase t.ConcreteCurrent != nil:\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: creating nodes for current instance objects only\")\n\tcase t.ConcreteDeposed != nil:\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: creating nodes for deposed instance objects only\")\n\tdefault:\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: pointless no-op call, creating no nodes at all\")\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, ms := range t.State.Modules {\n\t\tfor _, rs := range ms.Resources {\n\t\t\tresourceAddr := rs.Addr\n\n\t\t\tfor key, is := range rs.Instances {\n\t\t\t\taddr := resourceAddr.Instance(key)\n\n\t\t\t\tif obj := is.Current; obj != nil && t.ConcreteCurrent != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\t\t\t\tnode := t.ConcreteCurrent(abstract)\n\t\t\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: added %T for %s current object\", node, addr)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tif t.ConcreteDeposed != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tfor dk := range is.Deposed {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tnode := t.ConcreteDeposed(abstract, dk)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: added %T for %s deposed object %s\", node, addr, dk)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/transform_state.go"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/internal/terraform/transform_state.go"
41. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat > /workspace/internal/terraform/transform_deferred.go << 'EOFFILE'\n// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n)\n\n// DeferredTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds graph nodes representing\n// each of the deferred changes to the graph.\n//\n// Deferred changes are not executed during the apply phase, but they are\n// tracked in the graph to ensure that the correct ordering is maintained and\n// the target flags are correctly applied.\ntype DeferredTransformer struct {\n\tDeferredChanges []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc\n}\n\nfunc (t *DeferredTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n\tif len(t.DeferredChanges) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// As with the DiffTransformer, DeferredTransformer creates resource\n\t// instance nodes. If there are any whole-resource nodes already in the\n\t// graph, we must ensure they get evaluated before any of the corresponding\n\t// instances by creating dependency edges.\n\tresourceNodes := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.ConfigResource, []GraphNodeConfigResource]()\n\tfor _, node := range g.Vertices() {\n\t\trn, ok := node.(GraphNodeConfigResource)\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\t// We ignore any instances that _also_ implement\n\t\t// GraphNodeResourceInstance, since in the unlikely event that they\n\t\t// do exist we'd probably end up creating cycles by connecting them.\n\t\tif _, ok := node.(GraphNodeResourceInstance); ok {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\trAddr := rn.ResourceAddr()\n\t\tresourceNodes.Put(rAddr, append(resourceNodes.Get(rAddr), rn))\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, change := range t.DeferredChanges {\n\t\tnode := &nodeApplyableDeferredInstance{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(change.ChangeSrc.Addr),\n\t\t\tReason:                       change.DeferredReason,\n\t\t\tChangeSrc:                    change.ChangeSrc,\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Create a special node for partial instances, that handles the\n\t\t// addresses a little differently.\n\t\tif change.DeferredReason == providers.DeferredReasonInstanceCountUnknown {\n\t\t\tper := change.ChangeSrc.Addr.PartialResource()\n\n\t\t\t// This is a partial instance, so we need to create a partial node\n\t\t\t// instead of a full instance node.\n\t\t\tnode := &nodeApplyableDeferredPartialInstance{\n\t\t\t\tnodeApplyableDeferredInstance: node,\n\t\t\t\tPartialAddr:                   per,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n\n\t\t\t// Now we want to find the expansion node that would be applied for\n\t\t\t// this resource, and tell it that it is performing a partial\n\t\t\t// expansion.\n\t\t\tfor _, v := range g.Vertices() {\n\t\t\t\tif n, ok := v.(*nodeExpandApplyableResource); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tif per.ConfigResource().Equal(n.Addr) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn.PartialExpansions = append(n.PartialExpansions, per)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Also connect the deferred instance node to the underlying\n\t\t\t// resource node to make sure any expansion happens first.\n\t\t\tfor _, resourceNode := range resourceNodes.Get(node.Addr.ConfigResource()) {\n\t\t\t\tg.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(node, resourceNode))\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Otherwise, just add the normal deferred instance node.\n\t\tg.Add(node)\n\n\t\t// Still connect the deferred instance node to the underlying resource\n\t\t// node.\n\t\tfor _, resourceNode := range resourceNodes.Get(node.Addr.ConfigResource()) {\n\t\t\tg.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(node, resourceNode))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\nEOFFILE\n"
}
Output
""
42. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n)\n\n// OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds orphans\n// for an expanded count to the graph. The determination of this depends\n// on the count argument given.\n//\n// Orphans are found by comparing the count to what is found in the state.\n// This transform assumes that if an element in the state is within the count\n// bounds given, that it is not an orphan.\ntype OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer struct {\n\tConcrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n\n\tAddr          addrs.AbsResource           // Addr of the resource to look for orphans\n\tInstanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance // Addresses that currently exist in config\n\tState         *states.State               // Full global state\n}\n\nfunc (t *OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n\trs := t.State.Resource(t.Addr)\n\tif rs == nil {\n\t\treturn nil // Resource doesn't exist in state, so nothing to do!\n\t}\n\n\t// This is an O(n*m) analysis, which we accept for now because the\n\t// number of instances of a single resource ought to always be small in any\n\t// reasonable Terraform configuration.\nHave:\n\tfor key, inst := range rs.Instances {\n\t\t// Instances which have no current objects (only one or more\n\t\t// deposed objects) will be taken care of separately\n\t\tif inst.Current == nil {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tthisAddr := rs.Addr.Instance(key)\n\t\tfor _, wantAddr := range t.InstanceAddrs {\n\t\t\tif wantAddr.Equal(thisAddr) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue Have\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\t// If thisAddr is not in t.InstanceAddrs then we've found an \"orphan\"\n\n\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(thisAddr)\n\t\tvar node dag.Vertex = abstract\n\t\tif f := t.Concrete; f != nil {\n\t\t\tnode = f(abstract)\n\t\t}\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer: adding %s as %T\", thisAddr, node)\n\t\tg.Add(node)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/transform_orphan_count.go"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/internal/terraform/transform_orphan_count.go"

{
  "command": "python3 << 'PYSCRIPT'\nimport json\n\n# Extract and save node_resource_plan_instance.go\ntry:\n    with open('/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/6f353274-00a0-47ba-83a1-0d1cdbbca63c/tool-results/mcp-sourcegraph-sg_read_file-1772349802295.txt', 'r') as f:\n        data = json.load(f)\n        with open('/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_plan_instance.go', 'w') as out:\n            out.write(data['content'])\n    print(\"Saved node_resource_plan_instance.go\")\nexcept Exception as e:\n    print(f\"Error saving node_resource_plan_instance.go: {e}\")\n\n# The abstract_instance.go file should already be saved from earlier\nprint(\"File saves completed\")\nPYSCRIPT\n"
}
Output

45. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "\n# Let me try to identify which files contain the import and plan nodefiles by checking the first part of each\nfor file in /logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/6f353274-00a0-47ba-83a1-0d1cdbbca63c/tool-results/mcp-sourcegraph-*.txt; do\n  echo \"=== $file ===\" \n  python3 << PYCHECK\nimport json\nimport sys\ntry:\n    with open('$file', 'r') as f:\n        data = json.load(f)\n        if 'content' in data:\n            lines = data['content'].split('\\n')[:5]\n            for line in lines:\n                print(line[:100])\n        elif 'url' in data:\n            print(\"Has url:\", data['url'][:80])\nexcept Exception as e:\n    print(f\"Error: {e}\")\nPYCHECK\ndone | head -100\n"
}

\\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\\\"\\n11: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\\\"\\n12: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\\\"\\n13: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\\\"\\n14: )\\n15: \\n16: type graphNodeImportState struct {\\n17: \\tAddr             addrs.AbsResourceInstance // Addr is the resource address to import into\\n18: \\tID               string                    // ID is the ID to import as\\n19: \\tProviderAddr     addrs.AbsProviderConfig   // Provider address given by the user, or implied by the resource type\\n20: \\tResolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig   // provider node address after resolution\\n21: \\n22: \\tstates []providers.ImportedResource\\n23: }\\n24: \\n25: var (\\n26: \\t_ GraphNodeModulePath        = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\\n27: \\t_ GraphNodeExecutable        = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\\n28: \\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer  = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\\n29: \\t_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\\n30: )\\n31: \\n32: func (n *graphNodeImportState) Name() string {\\n33: \\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s (import id %q)\\\", n.Addr, n.ID)\\n34: }\\n35: \\n36: // GraphNodeProviderConsumer\\n37: func (n *graphNodeImportState) ProvidedBy() (addrs.ProviderConfig, bool) {\\n38: \\t// We assume that n.ProviderAddr has been properly populated here.\\n39: \\t// It's the responsibility of the code creating a graphNodeImportState\\n40: \\t// to populate this, possibly by calling DefaultProviderConfig() on the\\n41: \\t// resource address to infer an implied provider from the resource type\\n42: \\t// name.\\n43: \\treturn n.ProviderAddr, false\\n44: }\\n45: \\n46: // GraphNodeProviderConsumer\\n47: func (n *graphNodeImportState) Provider() addrs.Provider {\\n48: \\t// We assume that n.ProviderAddr has been properly populated here.\\n49: \\t// It's the responsibility of the code creating a graphNodeImportState\\n50: \\t// to populate this, possibly by calling DefaultProviderConfig() on the\\n51: \\t// resource address to infer an implied provider from the resource type\\n52: \\t// name.\\n53: \\treturn n.ProviderAddr.Provider\\n54: }\\n55: \\n56: // GraphNodeProviderConsumer\\n57: func (n *graphNodeImportState) SetProvider(addr addrs.AbsProviderConfig) {\\n58: \\tn.ResolvedProvider = addr\\n59: }\\n60: \\n61: // GraphNodeModuleInstance\\n62: func (n *graphNodeImportState) Path() addrs.ModuleInstance {\\n63: \\treturn n.Addr.Module\\n64: }\\n65: \\n66: // GraphNodeModulePath\\n67: func (n *graphNodeImportState) ModulePath() addrs.Module {\\n68: \\treturn n.Addr.Module.Module()\\n69: }\\n70: \\n71: // GraphNodeExecutable impl.\\n72: func (n *graphNodeImportState) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n73: \\t// Reset our states\\n74: \\tn.states = nil\\n75: \\n76: \\tprovider, _, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n77: \\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n78: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n79: \\t\\treturn diags\\n80: \\t}\\n81: \\n82: \\t// import state\\n83: \\tabsAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(ctx.Path())\\n84: \\thookResourceID := HookResourceIdentity{\\n85: \\t\\tAddr:         absAddr,\\n86: \\t\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider,\\n87: \\t}\\n88: \\n89: \\t// Call pre-import hook\\n90: \\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n91: \\t\\treturn h.PreImportState(hookResourceID, n.ID)\\n92: \\t}))\\n93: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n94: \\t\\treturn diags\\n95: \\t}\\n96: \\n97: \\tresp := provider.ImportResourceState(providers.ImportResourceStateRequest{\\n98: \\t\\tTypeName: n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\\n99: \\t\\tID:       n.ID,\\n100: \\t\\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_resource_import.go%3FL1-100\"}"

Output
"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\\n2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\\n3: \\n4: package terraform\\n5: \\n6: import (\\n7: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\\\"\\n8: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\\\"\\n9: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\\\"\\n10: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking\\\"\\n11: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\\\"\\n12: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\\\"\\n13: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\\\"\\n14: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\\\"\\n15: )\\n16: \\n17: // ApplyGraphBuilder implements GraphBuilder and is responsible for building\\n18: // a graph for applying a Terraform diff.\\n19: //\\n20: // Because the graph is built from the diff (vs. the config or state),\\n21: // this helps ensure that the apply-time graph doesn't modify any resources\\n22: // that aren't explicitly in the diff. There are other scenarios where the\\n23: // diff can be deviated, so this is just one layer of protection.\\n24: type ApplyGraphBuilder struct {\\n25: \\t// Config is the configuration tree that the diff was built from.\\n26: \\tConfig *configs.Config\\n27: \\n28: \\t// Changes describes the changes that we need apply.\\n29: \\tChanges *plans.ChangesSrc\\n30: \\n31: \\t// DeferredChanges describes the changes that were deferred during the plan\\n32: \\t// and should not be applied.\\n33: \\tDeferredChanges []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc\\n34: \\n35: \\t// State is the current state\\n36: \\tState *states.State\\n37: \\n38: \\t// RootVariableValues are the root module input variables captured as\\n39: \\t// part of the plan object, which we must reproduce in the apply step\\n40: \\t// to get a consistent result.\\n41: \\tRootVariableValues InputValues\\n42: \\n43: \\t// ExternalProviderConfigs are pre-initialized root module provider\\n44: \\t// configurations that the graph builder should assume will be available\\n45: \\t// immediately during the subsequent plan walk, without any explicit\\n46: \\t// initialization step.\\n47: \\tExternalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface\\n48: \\n49: \\t// Plugins is a library of the plug-in components (providers and\\n50: \\t// provisioners) available for use.\\n51: \\tPlugins *contextPlugins\\n52: \\n53: \\t// Targets are resources to target. This is only required to make sure\\n54: \\t// unnecessary outputs aren't included in the apply graph. The plan\\n55: \\t// builder successfully handles targeting resources. In the future,\\n56: \\t// outputs should go into the diff so that this is unnecessary.\\n57: \\tTargets []addrs.Targetable\\n58: \\n59: \\t// ForceReplace are the resource instance addresses that the user\\n60: \\t// requested to force replacement for when creating the plan, if any.\\n61: \\t// The apply step refers to these as part of verifying that the planned\\n62: \\t// actions remain consistent between plan and apply.\\n63: \\tForceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\\n64: \\n65: \\t// Plan Operation this graph will be used for.\\n66: \\tOperation walkOperation\\n67: \\n68: \\t// ExternalReferences allows the external caller to pass in references to\\n69: \\t// nodes that should not be pruned even if they are not referenced within\\n70: \\t// the actual graph.\\n71: \\tExternalReferences []*addrs.Reference\\n72: \\n73: \\t// Overrides provides the set of overrides supplied by the testing\\n74: \\t// framework.\\n75: \\tOverrides *mocking.Overrides\\n76: }\\n77: \\n78: // See GraphBuilder\\n79: func (b *ApplyGraphBuilder) Build(path addrs.ModuleInstance) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n80: \\treturn (&BasicGraphBuilder{\\n81: \\t\\tSteps: b.Steps(),\\n82: \\t\\tName:  \\\"ApplyGraphBuilder\\\",\\n83: \\t}).Build(path)\\n84: }\\n85: \\n86: // See GraphBuilder\\n87: func (b *ApplyGraphBuilder) Steps() []GraphTransformer {\\n88: \\t// Custom factory for creating providers.\\n89: \\tconcreteProvider := func(a *NodeAbstractProvider) dag.Vertex {\\n90: \\t\\treturn &NodeApplyableProvider{\\n91: \\t\\t\\tNodeAbstractProvider: a,\\n92: \\t\\t}\\n93: \\t}\\n94: \\n95: \\tconcreteResource := func(a *NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex {\\n96: \\t\\treturn &nodeExpandApplyableResource{\\n97: \\t\\t\\tNodeAbstractResource: a,\\n98: \\t\\t}\\n99: \\t}\\n100: \\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/graph_builder_apply.go%3FL1-100\"}"

{
  "endLine": 100,
  "path": "internal/terraform/graph_builder_plan.go",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3",

}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\\n2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\\n3: \\n4: package terraform\\n5: \\n6: import (\\n7: \\t\\\"log\\\"\\n8: \\n9: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\\\"\\n10: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\\\"\\n11: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\\\"\\n12: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking\\\"\\n13: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\\\"\\n14: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\\\"\\n15: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\\\"\\n16: )\\n17: \\n18: // PlanGraphBuilder is a GraphBuilder implementation that builds a graph for\\n19: // planning and for other \\\"plan-like\\\" operations which don't require an\\n20: // already-calculated plan as input.\\n21: //\\n22: // Unlike the apply graph builder, this graph builder:\\n23: //\\n24: //   - Makes its decisions primarily based on the given configuration, which\\n25: //     represents the desired state.\\n26: //\\n27: //   - Ignores certain lifecycle concerns like create_before_destroy, because\\n28: //     those are only important once we already know what action we're planning\\n29: //     to take against a particular resource instance.\\n30: type PlanGraphBuilder struct {\\n31: \\t// Config is the configuration tree to build a plan from.\\n32: \\tConfig *configs.Config\\n33: \\n34: \\t// State is the current state\\n35: \\tState *states.State\\n36: \\n37: \\t// RootVariableValues are the raw input values for root input variables\\n38: \\t// given by the caller, which we'll resolve into final values as part\\n39: \\t// of the plan walk.\\n40: \\tRootVariableValues InputValues\\n41: \\n42: \\t// ExternalProviderConfigs are pre-initialized root module provider\\n43: \\t// configurations that the graph builder should assume will be available\\n44: \\t// immediately during the subsequent plan walk, without any explicit\\n45: \\t// initialization step.\\n46: \\tExternalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface\\n47: \\n48: \\t// Plugins is a library of plug-in components (providers and\\n49: \\t// provisioners) available for use.\\n50: \\tPlugins *contextPlugins\\n51: \\n52: \\t// Targets are resources to target\\n53: \\tTargets []addrs.Targetable\\n54: \\n55: \\t// ForceReplace are resource instances where if we would normally have\\n56: \\t// generated a NoOp or Update action then we'll force generating a replace\\n57: \\t// action instead. Create and Delete actions are not affected.\\n58: \\tForceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\\n59: \\n60: \\t// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing managed resources\\n61: \\tskipRefresh bool\\n62: \\n63: \\t// preDestroyRefresh indicates that we are executing the refresh which\\n64: \\t// happens immediately before a destroy plan, which happens to use the\\n65: \\t// normal planing mode so skipPlanChanges cannot be set.\\n66: \\tpreDestroyRefresh bool\\n67: \\n68: \\t// skipPlanChanges indicates that we should skip the step of comparing\\n69: \\t// prior state with configuration and generating planned changes to\\n70: \\t// resource instances. (This is for the \\\"refresh only\\\" planning mode,\\n71: \\t// where we _only_ do the refresh step.)\\n72: \\tskipPlanChanges bool\\n73: \\n74: \\tConcreteProvider                ConcreteProviderNodeFunc\\n75: \\tConcreteResource                ConcreteResourceNodeFunc\\n76: \\tConcreteResourceInstance        ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\\n77: \\tConcreteResourceOrphan          ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\\n78: \\tConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposedNodeFunc\\n79: \\tConcreteModule                  ConcreteModuleNodeFunc\\n80: \\n81: \\t// Plan Operation this graph will be used for.\\n82: \\tOperation walkOperation\\n83: \\n84: \\t// ExternalReferences allows the external caller to pass in references to\\n85: \\t// nodes that should not be pruned even if they are not referenced within\\n86: \\t// the actual graph.\\n87: \\tExternalReferences []*addrs.Reference\\n88: \\n89: \\t// Overrides provides the set of overrides supplied by the testing\\n90: \\t// framework.\\n91: \\tOverrides *mocking.Overrides\\n92: \\n93: \\t// ImportTargets are the list of resources to import.\\n94: \\tImportTargets []*ImportTarget\\n95: \\n96: \\t// forgetResources lists the resources that are to be forgotten, i.e. removed\\n97: \\t// from state without destroying.\\n98: \\tforgetResources []addrs.ConfigResource\\n99: \\n100: \\t// forgetModules lists the modules that are to be forgotten, i.e. removed\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/graph_builder_plan.go%3FL1-100\"}"
50. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "\n# Create a python script to fetch full file contents from Sourcegraph in chunks\npython3 << 'PYSCRIPT'\n# We already have the partial content from the earlier retrieval in memory\n# Let's use bash curl to fetch the full files directly\nimport subprocess\nimport json\n\nfiles = [\n    ('node_resource_import.go', 'internal/terraform/node_resource_import.go'),\n    ('node_resource_plan.go', 'internal/terraform/node_resource_plan.go'),  \n    ('graph_builder_apply.go', 'internal/terraform/graph_builder_apply.go'),\n    ('graph_builder_plan.go', 'internal/terraform/graph_builder_plan.go'),\n]\n\n# These files were already retrieved earlier - let me check the saved responses\n# We have them in the log results from earlier API calls\nprint(\"Files remaining to save\")\nfor fname, _ in files:\n    print(f\"  - {fname}\")\nPYSCRIPT\n"
}
Output
"Files remaining to save\n  - node_resource_import.go\n  - node_resource_plan.go\n  - graph_builder_apply.go\n  - graph_builder_plan.go"
51. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// ApplyGraphBuilder implements GraphBuilder and is responsible for building\n// a graph for applying a Terraform diff.\n//\n// Because the graph is built from the diff (vs. the config or state),\n// this helps ensure that the apply-time graph doesn't modify any resources\n// that aren't explicitly in the diff. There are other scenarios where the\n// diff can be deviated, so this is just one layer of protection.\ntype ApplyGraphBuilder struct {\n\t// Config is the configuration tree that the diff was built from.\n\tConfig *configs.Config\n\n\t// Changes describes the changes that we need apply.\n\tChanges *plans.ChangesSrc\n\n\t// DeferredChanges describes the changes that were deferred during the plan\n\t// and should not be applied.\n\tDeferredChanges []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc\n\n\t// State is the current state\n\tState *states.State\n\n\t// RootVariableValues are the root module input variables captured as\n\t// part of the plan object, which we must reproduce in the apply step\n\t// to get a consistent result.\n\tRootVariableValues InputValues\n\n\t// ExternalProviderConfigs are pre-initialized root module provider\n\t// configurations that the graph builder should assume will be available\n\t// immediately during the subsequent plan walk, without any explicit\n\t// initialization step.\n\tExternalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface\n\n\t// Plugins is a library of the plug-in components (providers and\n\t// provisioners) available for use.\n\tPlugins *contextPlugins\n\n\t// Targets are resources to target. This is only required to make sure\n\t// unnecessary outputs aren't included in the apply graph. The plan\n\t// builder successfully handles targeting resources. In the future,\n\t// outputs should go into the diff so that this is unnecessary.\n\tTargets []addrs.Targetable\n\n\t// ForceReplace are the resource instance addresses that the user\n\t// requested to force replacement for when creating the plan, if any.\n\t// The apply step refers to these as part of verifying that the planned\n\t// actions remain consistent between plan and apply.\n\tForceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\n\t// Plan Operation this graph will be used for.\n\tOperation walkOperation\n\n\t// ExternalReferences allows the external caller to pass in references to\n\t// nodes that should not be pruned even if they are not referenced within\n\t// the actual graph.\n\tExternalReferences []*addrs.Reference\n\n\t// Overrides provides the set of overrides supplied by the testing\n\t// framework.\n\tOverrides *mocking.Overrides\n}\n\n// See GraphBuilder\nfunc (b *ApplyGraphBuilder) Build(path addrs.ModuleInstance) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\treturn (&BasicGraphBuilder{\n\t\tSteps: b.Steps(),\n\t\tName:  \"ApplyGraphBuilder\",\n\t}).Build(path)\n}\n\n// See GraphBuilder\nfunc (b *ApplyGraphBuilder) Steps() []GraphTransformer {\n\t// Custom factory for creating providers.\n\tconcreteProvider := func(a *NodeAbstractProvider) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn &NodeApplyableProvider{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractProvider: a,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tconcreteResource := func(a *NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn &nodeExpandApplyableResource{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: a,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tconcreteResourceInstance := func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn &NodeApplyableResourceInstance{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n\t\t\tforceReplace:                 b.ForceReplace,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tsteps := []GraphTransformer{\n\t\t// Creates all the resources represented in the config. During apply,\n\t\t// we use this just to ensure that the whole-resource metadata is\n\t\t// updated to reflect things such as whether the count argument is\n\t\t// set in config, or which provider configuration manages each resource.\n\t\t&ConfigTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcrete: concreteResource,\n\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Add dynamic values\n\t\t&RootVariableTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:       b.Config,\n\t\t\tRawValues:    b.RootVariableValues,\n\t\t\tDestroyApply: b.Operation == walkDestroy,\n\t\t},\n\t\t&ModuleVariableTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:       b.Config,\n\t\t\tDestroyApply: b.Operation == walkDestroy,\n\t\t},\n\t\t&variableValidationTransformer{},\n\t\t&LocalTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n\t\t&OutputTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:     b.Config,\n\t\t\tDestroying: b.Operation == walkDestroy,\n\t\t\tOverrides:  b.Overrides,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Creates all the resource instances represented in the diff, along\n\t\t// with dependency edges against the whole-resource nodes added by\n\t\t// ConfigTransformer above.\n\t\t&DiffTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcrete: concreteResourceInstance,\n\t\t\tState:    b.State,\n\t\t\tChanges:  b.Changes,\n\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Creates nodes for all the deferred changes.\n\t\t&DeferredTransformer{\n\t\t\tDeferredChanges: b.DeferredChanges,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Add nodes and edges for check block assertions. Check block data\n\t\t// sources were added earlier.\n\t\t&checkTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:    b.Config,\n\t\t\tOperation: b.Operation,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Attach the state\n\t\t&AttachStateTransformer{State: b.State},\n\n\t\t// Create orphan output nodes\n\t\t&OrphanOutputTransformer{Config: b.Config, State: b.State},\n\n\t\t// Attach the configuration to any resources\n\t\t&AttachResourceConfigTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n\n\t\t// add providers\n\t\ttransformProviders(concreteProvider, b.Config, b.ExternalProviderConfigs),\n\n\t\t// Remove modules no longer present in the config\n\t\t&RemovedModuleTransformer{Config: b.Config, State: b.State},\n\n\t\t// Must attach schemas before ReferenceTransformer so that we can\n\t\t// analyze the configuration to find references.\n\t\t&AttachSchemaTransformer{Plugins: b.Plugins, Config: b.Config},\n\n\t\t// Create expansion nodes for all of the module calls. This must\n\t\t// come after all other transformers that create nodes representing\n\t\t// objects that can belong to modules.\n\t\t&ModuleExpansionTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n\n\t\t// Plug in any external references.\n\t\t&ExternalReferenceTransformer{\n\t\t\tExternalReferences: b.ExternalReferences,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Connect references so ordering is correct\n\t\t&ReferenceTransformer{},\n\t\t&AttachDependenciesTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Nested data blocks should be loaded after every other resource has\n\t\t// done its thing.\n\t\t&checkStartTransformer{Config: b.Config, Operation: b.Operation},\n\n\t\t// Detect when create_before_destroy must be forced on for a particular\n\t\t// node due to dependency edges, to avoid graph cycles during apply.\n\t\t//\n\t\t// FIXME: this should not need to be recalculated during apply.\n\t\t// Currently however, the instance object which stores the planned\n\t\t// information is lost for newly created instances because it contains\n\t\t// no state value, and we end up recalculating CBD for all nodes.\n\t\t&ForcedCBDTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Destruction ordering\n\t\t&DestroyEdgeTransformer{\n\t\t\tChanges:   b.Changes,\n\t\t\tOperation: b.Operation,\n\t\t},\n\t\t&CBDEdgeTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig: b.Config,\n\t\t\tState:  b.State,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// In a destroy, we need to remove configuration nodes that are not used\n\t\t// at all, as they may not be able to evaluate. These include variables,\n\t\t// locals, and instance expanders.\n\t\t&pruneUnusedNodesTransformer{\n\t\t\tskip: b.Operation != walkDestroy,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Target\n\t\t&TargetsTransformer{Targets: b.Targets},\n\n\t\t// Close any ephemeral resource instances.\n\t\t&ephemeralResourceCloseTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Close opened plugin connections\n\t\t&CloseProviderTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// close the root module\n\t\t&CloseRootModuleTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Perform the transitive reduction to make our graph a bit\n\t\t// more understandable if possible (it usually is possible).\n\t\t&TransitiveReductionTransformer{},\n\t}\n\n\treturn steps\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/graph_builder_apply.go"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/internal/terraform/graph_builder_apply.go"
52. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// PlanGraphBuilder is a GraphBuilder implementation that builds a graph for\n// planning and for other \"plan-like\" operations which don't require an\n// already-calculated plan as input.\n//\n// Unlike the apply graph builder, this graph builder:\n//\n//   - Makes its decisions primarily based on the given configuration, which\n//     represents the desired state.\n//\n//   - Ignores certain lifecycle concerns like create_before_destroy, because\n//     those are only important once we already know what action we're planning\n//     to take against a particular resource instance.\ntype PlanGraphBuilder struct {\n\t// Config is the configuration tree to build a plan from.\n\tConfig *configs.Config\n\n\t// State is the current state\n\tState *states.State\n\n\t// RootVariableValues are the raw input values for root input variables\n\t// given by the caller, which we'll resolve into final values as part\n\t// of the plan walk.\n\tRootVariableValues InputValues\n\n\t// ExternalProviderConfigs are pre-initialized root module provider\n\t// configurations that the graph builder should assume will be available\n\t// immediately during the subsequent plan walk, without any explicit\n\t// initialization step.\n\tExternalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface\n\n\t// Plugins is a library of plug-in components (providers and\n\t// provisioners) available for use.\n\tPlugins *contextPlugins\n\n\t// Targets are resources to target\n\tTargets []addrs.Targetable\n\n\t// ForceReplace are resource instances where if we would normally have\n\t// generated a NoOp or Update action then we'll force generating a replace\n\t// action instead. Create and Delete actions are not affected.\n\tForceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\n\t// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing managed resources\n\tskipRefresh bool\n\n\t// preDestroyRefresh indicates that we are executing the refresh which\n\t// happens immediately before a destroy plan, which happens to use the\n\t// normal planing mode so skipPlanChanges cannot be set.\n\tpreDestroyRefresh bool\n\n\t// skipPlanChanges indicates that we should skip the step of comparing\n\t// prior state with configuration and generating planned changes to\n\t// resource instances. (This is for the \"refresh only\" planning mode,\n\t// where we _only_ do the refresh step.)\n\tskipPlanChanges bool\n\n\tConcreteProvider                ConcreteProviderNodeFunc\n\tConcreteResource                ConcreteResourceNodeFunc\n\tConcreteResourceInstance        ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n\tConcreteResourceOrphan          ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n\tConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposedNodeFunc\n\tConcreteModule                  ConcreteModuleNodeFunc\n\n\t// Plan Operation this graph will be used for.\n\tOperation walkOperation\n\n\t// ExternalReferences allows the external caller to pass in references to\n\t// nodes that should not be pruned even if they are not referenced within\n\t// the actual graph.\n\tExternalReferences []*addrs.Reference\n\n\t// Overrides provides the set of overrides supplied by the testing\n\t// framework.\n\tOverrides *mocking.Overrides\n\n\t// ImportTargets are the list of resources to import.\n\tImportTargets []*ImportTarget\n\n\t// forgetResources lists the resources that are to be forgotten, i.e. removed\n\t// from state without destroying.\n\tforgetResources []addrs.ConfigResource\n\n\t// forgetModules lists the modules that are to be forgotten, i.e. removed\n\t// from state without destroying.\n\tforgetModules []addrs.Module\n\n\t// GenerateConfig tells Terraform where to write and generated config for\n\t// any import targets that do not already have configuration.\n\t//\n\t// If empty, then config will not be generated.\n\tGenerateConfigPath string\n}\n\n// See GraphBuilder\nfunc (b *PlanGraphBuilder) Build(path addrs.ModuleInstance) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] building graph for %s\", b.Operation)\n\treturn (&BasicGraphBuilder{\n\t\tSteps: b.Steps(),\n\t\tName:  \"PlanGraphBuilder\",\n\t}).Build(path)\n}\n\n// See GraphBuilder\nfunc (b *PlanGraphBuilder) Steps() []GraphTransformer {\n\tswitch b.Operation {\n\tcase walkPlan:\n\t\tb.initPlan()\n\tcase walkPlanDestroy:\n\t\tb.initDestroy()\n\tcase walkValidate:\n\t\tb.initValidate()\n\tcase walkImport:\n\t\tb.initImport()\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(\"invalid plan operation: \" + b.Operation.String())\n\t}\n\n\tsteps := []GraphTransformer{\n\t\t// Creates all the resources represented in the config\n\t\t&ConfigTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcrete: b.ConcreteResource,\n\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n\t\t\tdestroy:  b.Operation == walkDestroy || b.Operation == walkPlanDestroy,\n\n\t\t\timportTargets: b.ImportTargets,\n\n\t\t\t// We only want to generate config during a plan operation.\n\t\t\tgenerateConfigPathForImportTargets: b.GenerateConfigPath,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Add dynamic values\n\t\t&RootVariableTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:       b.Config,\n\t\t\tRawValues:    b.RootVariableValues,\n\t\t\tPlanning:     true,\n\t\t\tDestroyApply: false, // always false for planning\n\t\t},\n\t\t&ModuleVariableTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:       b.Config,\n\t\t\tPlanning:     true,\n\t\t\tDestroyApply: false, // always false for planning\n\t\t},\n\t\t&variableValidationTransformer{\n\t\t\tvalidateWalk: b.Operation == walkValidate,\n\t\t},\n\t\t&LocalTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n\t\t&OutputTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:      b.Config,\n\t\t\tRefreshOnly: b.skipPlanChanges || b.preDestroyRefresh,\n\t\t\tDestroying:  b.Operation == walkPlanDestroy,\n\t\t\tOverrides:   b.Overrides,\n\n\t\t\t// NOTE: We currently treat anything built with the plan graph\n\t\t\t// builder as \"planning\" for our purposes here, because we share\n\t\t\t// the same graph node implementation between all of the walk\n\t\t\t// types and so the pre-planning walks still think they are\n\t\t\t// producing a plan even though we immediately discard it.\n\t\t\tPlanning: true,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Add nodes and edges for the check block assertions. Check block data\n\t\t// sources were added earlier.\n\t\t&checkTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:    b.Config,\n\t\t\tOperation: b.Operation,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Add orphan resources\n\t\t&OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcrete: b.ConcreteResourceOrphan,\n\t\t\tState:    b.State,\n\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n\t\t\tskip:     b.Operation == walkPlanDestroy,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// We also need nodes for any deposed instance objects present in the\n\t\t// state, so we can plan to destroy them. (During plan this will\n\t\t// intentionally skip creating nodes for _current_ objects, since\n\t\t// ConfigTransformer created nodes that will do that during\n\t\t// DynamicExpand.)\n\t\t&StateTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcreteCurrent: b.ConcreteResourceInstance,\n\t\t\tConcreteDeposed: b.ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed,\n\t\t\tState:           b.State,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Attach the state\n\t\t&AttachStateTransformer{State: b.State},\n\n\t\t// Create orphan output nodes\n\t\t&OrphanOutputTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n\t\t\tState:    b.State,\n\t\t\tPlanning: true,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Attach the configuration to any resources\n\t\t&AttachResourceConfigTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n\n\t\t// add providers\n\t\ttransformProviders(b.ConcreteProvider, b.Config, b.ExternalProviderConfigs),\n\n\t\t// Remove modules no longer present in the config\n\t\t&RemovedModuleTransformer{Config: b.Config, State: b.State},\n\n\t\t// Must attach schemas before ReferenceTransformer so that we can\n\t\t// analyze the configuration to find references.\n\t\t&AttachSchemaTransformer{Plugins: b.Plugins, Config: b.Config},\n\n\t\t// Create expansion nodes for all of the module calls. This must\n\t\t// come after all other transformers that create nodes representing\n\t\t// objects that can belong to modules.\n\t\t&ModuleExpansionTransformer{Concrete: b.ConcreteModule, Config: b.Config},\n\n\t\t// Plug in any external references.\n\t\t&ExternalReferenceTransformer{\n\t\t\tExternalReferences: b.ExternalReferences,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t&ReferenceTransformer{},\n\n\t\t&AttachDependenciesTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Make sure data sources are aware of any depends_on from the\n\t\t// configuration\n\t\t&attachDataResourceDependsOnTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// DestroyEdgeTransformer is only required during a plan so that the\n\t\t// TargetsTransformer can determine which nodes to keep in the graph.\n\t\t&DestroyEdgeTransformer{\n\t\t\tOperation: b.Operation,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t&pruneUnusedNodesTransformer{\n\t\t\tskip: b.Operation != walkPlanDestroy,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Target\n\t\t&TargetsTransformer{Targets: b.Targets},\n\n\t\t// Detect when create_before_destroy must be forced on for a particular\n\t\t// node due to dependency edges, to avoid graph cycles during apply.\n\t\t&ForcedCBDTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Close any ephemeral resource instances.\n\t\t&ephemeralResourceCloseTransformer{skip: b.Operation == walkValidate},\n\n\t\t// Close opened plugin connections\n\t\t&CloseProviderTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Close the root module\n\t\t&CloseRootModuleTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Perform the transitive reduction to make our graph a bit\n\t\t// more understandable if possible (it usually is possible).\n\t\t&TransitiveReductionTransformer{},\n\t}\n\n\treturn steps\n}\n\nfunc (b *PlanGraphBuilder) initPlan() {\n\tb.ConcreteProvider = func(a *NodeAbstractProvider) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn &NodeApplyableProvider{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractProvider: a,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tb.ConcreteResource = func(a *NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn &nodeExpandPlannableResource{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: a,\n\t\t\tskipRefresh:          b.skipRefresh,\n\t\t\tskipPlanChanges:      b.skipPlanChanges,\n\t\t\tpreDestroyRefresh:    b.preDestroyRefresh,\n\t\t\tforceReplace:         b.ForceReplace,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tb.ConcreteResourceOrphan = func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn &NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n\t\t\tskipRefresh:                  b.skipRefresh,\n\t\t\tskipPlanChanges:              b.skipPlanChanges,\n\t\t\tforgetResources:              b.forgetResources,\n\t\t\tforgetModules:                b.forgetModules,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tb.ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed = func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance, key states.DeposedKey) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn &NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n\t\t\tDeposedKey:                   key,\n\n\t\t\tskipRefresh:     b.skipRefresh,\n\t\t\tskipPlanChanges: b.skipPlanChanges,\n\t\t\tforgetResources: b.forgetResources,\n\t\t\tforgetModules:   b.forgetModules,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (b *PlanGraphBuilder) initDestroy() {\n\tb.initPlan()\n\n\tb.ConcreteResourceInstance = func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn &NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n\t\t\tskipRefresh:                  b.skipRefresh,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (b *PlanGraphBuilder) initValidate() {\n\t// Set the provider to the normal provider. This will ask for input.\n\tb.ConcreteProvider = func(a *NodeAbstractProvider) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn &NodeApplyableProvider{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractProvider: a,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tb.ConcreteResource = func(a *NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn &NodeValidatableResource{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: a,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tb.ConcreteModule = func(n *nodeExpandModule) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn &nodeValidateModule{\n\t\t\tnodeExpandModule: *n,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (b *PlanGraphBuilder) initImport() {\n\tb.ConcreteProvider = func(a *NodeAbstractProvider) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn &NodeApplyableProvider{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractProvider: a,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tb.ConcreteResource = func(a *NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn &nodeExpandPlannableResource{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: a,\n\n\t\t\t// For now we always skip planning changes for import, since we are\n\t\t\t// not going to combine importing with other changes. This is\n\t\t\t// temporary to try and maintain existing import behaviors, but\n\t\t\t// planning will need to be allowed for more complex configurations.\n\t\t\tskipPlanChanges: true,\n\n\t\t\t// We also skip refresh for now, since the plan output is written\n\t\t\t// as the new state, and users are not expecting the import process\n\t\t\t// to update any other instances in state.\n\t\t\tskipRefresh: true,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/graph_builder_plan.go"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/internal/terraform/graph_builder_plan.go"
53. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "\n# Get node_resource_import.go and node_resource_plan.go using curl\n# These were already in the response data from earlier - let me use the Bash tool \n# to make multiple calls to get the full files\n\necho \"Getting remaining files...\"\n\n# These files are already retrieved but just need to be properly saved\n# Let me verify what we have saved so far\nls /workspace/internal/terraform/*.go | wc -l\necho \"Files saved so far\"\nls /workspace/internal/terraform/*.go | xargs -n1 basename | sort\n"
}
Output
"Getting remaining files...\n15\nFiles saved so far\ngraph_builder_apply.go\ngraph_builder_plan.go\nnode_data_destroy.go\nnode_resource_abstract.go\nnode_resource_abstract_instance.go\nnode_resource_abstract_instance_test.go\nnode_resource_apply.go\nnode_resource_apply_instance.go\nnode_resource_destroy.go\nnode_resource_forget.go\nnode_resource_plan_instance.go\ntransform_deferred.go\ntransform_diff.go\ntransform_orphan_count.go\ntransform_state.go"
