"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\\n2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\\n3: \\n4: package terraform\\n5: \\n6: import (\\n7: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n8: \\t\\\"log\\\"\\n9: \\n10: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\\\"\\n11: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\\\"\\n12: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\\\"\\n13: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\\\"\\n14: )\\n15: \\n16: type graphNodeImportState struct {\\n17: \\tAddr             addrs.AbsResourceInstance // Addr is the resource address to import into\\n18: \\tID               string                    // ID is the ID to import as\\n19: \\tProviderAddr     addrs.AbsProviderConfig   // Provider address given by the user, or implied by the resource type\\n20: \\tResolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig   // provider node address after resolution\\n21: \\n22: \\tstates []providers.ImportedResource\\n23: }\\n24: \\n25: var (\\n26: \\t_ GraphNodeModulePath        = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\\n27: \\t_ GraphNodeExecutable        = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\\n28: \\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer  = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\\n29: \\t_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\\n30: )\\n31: \\n32: func (n *graphNodeImportState) Name() string {\\n33: \\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s (import id %q)\\\", n.Addr, n.ID)\\n34: }\\n35: \\n36: // GraphNodeProviderConsumer\\n37: func (n *graphNodeImportState) ProvidedBy() (addrs.ProviderConfig, bool) {\\n38: \\t// We assume that n.ProviderAddr has been properly populated here.\\n39: \\t// It's the responsibility of the code creating a graphNodeImportState\\n40: \\t// to populate this, possibly by calling DefaultProviderConfig() on the\\n41: \\t// resource address to infer an implied provider from the resource type\\n42: \\t// name.\\n43: \\treturn n.ProviderAddr, false\\n44: }\\n45: \\n46: // GraphNodeProviderConsumer\\n47: func (n *graphNodeImportState) Provider() addrs.Provider {\\n48: \\t// We assume that n.ProviderAddr has been properly populated here.\\n49: \\t// It's the responsibility of the code creating a graphNodeImportState\\n50: \\t// to populate this, possibly by calling DefaultProviderConfig() on the\\n51: \\t// resource address to infer an implied provider from the resource type\\n52: \\t// name.\\n53: \\treturn n.ProviderAddr.Provider\\n54: }\\n55: \\n56: // GraphNodeProviderConsumer\\n57: func (n *graphNodeImportState) SetProvider(addr addrs.AbsProviderConfig) {\\n58: \\tn.ResolvedProvider = addr\\n59: }\\n60: \\n61: // GraphNodeModuleInstance\\n62: func (n *graphNodeImportState) Path() addrs.ModuleInstance {\\n63: \\treturn n.Addr.Module\\n64: }\\n65: \\n66: // GraphNodeModulePath\\n67: func (n *graphNodeImportState) ModulePath() addrs.Module {\\n68: \\treturn n.Addr.Module.Module()\\n69: }\\n70: \\n71: // GraphNodeExecutable impl.\\n72: func (n *graphNodeImportState) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n73: \\t// Reset our states\\n74: \\tn.states = nil\\n75: \\n76: \\tprovider, _, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n77: \\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n78: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n79: \\t\\treturn diags\\n80: \\t}\\n81: \\n82: \\t// import state\\n83: \\tabsAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(ctx.Path())\\n84: \\thookResourceID := HookResourceIdentity{\\n85: \\t\\tAddr:         absAddr,\\n86: \\t\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider,\\n87: \\t}\\n88: \\n89: \\t// Call pre-import hook\\n90: \\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n91: \\t\\treturn h.PreImportState(hookResourceID, n.ID)\\n92: \\t}))\\n93: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n94: \\t\\treturn diags\\n95: \\t}\\n96: \\n97: \\tresp := provider.ImportResourceState(providers.ImportResourceStateRequest{\\n98: \\t\\tTypeName: n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\\n99: \\t\\tID:       n.ID,\\n100: \\t\\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\\n101: \\t\\t\\tDeferralAllowed: false,\\n102: \\t\\t},\\n103: \\t})\\n104: \\tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics)\\n105: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n106: \\t\\treturn diags\\n107: \\t}\\n108: \\n109: \\timported := resp.ImportedResources\\n110: \\tfor _, obj := range imported {\\n111: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] graphNodeImportState: import %s %q produced instance object of type %s\\\", absAddr.String(), n.ID, obj.TypeName)\\n112: \\t}\\n113: \\tn.states = imported\\n114: \\n115: \\t// Call post-import hook\\n116: \\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n117: \\t\\treturn h.PostImportState(hookResourceID, imported)\\n118: \\t}))\\n119: \\treturn diags\\n120: }\\n121: \\n122: // GraphNodeDynamicExpandable impl.\\n123: //\\n124: // We use DynamicExpand as a way to generate the subgraph of refreshes\\n125: // and state inserts we need to do for our import state. Since they're new\\n126: // resources they don't depend on anything else and refreshes are isolated\\n127: // so this is nearly a perfect use case for dynamic expand.\\n128: func (n *graphNodeImportState) DynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n129: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n130: \\n131: \\tg := &Graph{Path: ctx.Path()}\\n132: \\n133: \\t// nameCounter is used to de-dup names in the state.\\n134: \\tnameCounter := make(map[string]int)\\n135: \\n136: \\t// Compile the list of addresses that we'll be inserting into the state.\\n137: \\t// We do this ahead of time so we can verify that we aren't importing\\n138: \\t// something that already exists.\\n139: \\taddrs := make([]addrs.AbsResourceInstance, len(n.states))\\n140: \\tfor i, state := range n.states {\\n141: \\t\\taddr := n.Addr\\n142: \\t\\tif t := state.TypeName; t != \\\"\\\" {\\n143: \\t\\t\\taddr.Resource.Resource.Type = t\\n144: \\t\\t}\\n145: \\n146: \\t\\t// Determine if we need to suffix the name to de-dup\\n147: \\t\\tkey := addr.String()\\n148: \\t\\tcount, ok := nameCounter[key]\\n149: \\t\\tif ok {\\n150: \\t\\t\\tcount++\\n151: \\t\\t\\taddr.Resource.Resource.Name += fmt.Sprintf(\\\"-%d\\\", count)\\n152: \\t\\t}\\n153: \\t\\tnameCounter[key] = count\\n154: \\n155: \\t\\t// Add it to our list\\n156: \\t\\taddrs[i] = addr\\n157: \\t}\\n158: \\n159: \\t// Verify that all the addresses are clear\\n160: \\tstate := ctx.State()\\n161: \\tfor _, addr := range addrs {\\n162: \\t\\texisting := state.ResourceInstance(addr)\\n163: \\t\\tif existing != nil {\\n164: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n165: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n166: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Resource already managed by Terraform\\\",\\n167: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"Terraform is already managing a remote object for %s. To import to this address you must first remove the existing object from the state.\\\", addr),\\n168: \\t\\t\\t))\\n169: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n170: \\t\\t}\\n171: \\t}\\n172: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n173: \\t\\t// Bail out early, then.\\n174: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n175: \\t}\\n176: \\n177: \\t// For each of the states, we add a node to handle the refresh/add to state.\\n178: \\t// \\\"n.states\\\" is populated by our own Execute with the result of\\n179: \\t// ImportState. Since DynamicExpand is always called after Execute, this is\\n180: \\t// safe.\\n181: \\tfor i, state := range n.states {\\n182: \\t\\tg.Add(&graphNodeImportStateSub{\\n183: \\t\\t\\tTargetAddr:       addrs[i],\\n184: \\t\\t\\tState:            state,\\n185: \\t\\t\\tResolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\\n186: \\t\\t})\\n187: \\t}\\n188: \\n189: \\taddRootNodeToGraph(g)\\n190: \\n191: \\t// Done!\\n192: \\treturn g, diags\\n193: }\\n194: \\n195: // graphNodeImportStateSub is the sub-node of graphNodeImportState\\n196: // and is part of the subgraph. This node is responsible for refreshing\\n197: // and adding a resource to the state once it is imported.\\n198: type graphNodeImportStateSub struct {\\n199: \\tTargetAddr       addrs.AbsResourceInstance\\n200: \\tState            providers.ImportedResource\\n201: \\tResolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig\\n202: }\\n203: \\n204: var (\\n205: \\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance = (*graphNodeImportStateSub)(nil)\\n206: \\t_ GraphNodeExecutable     = (*graphNodeImportStateSub)(nil)\\n207: )\\n208: \\n209: func (n *graphNodeImportStateSub) Name() string {\\n210: \\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"import %s result\\\", n.TargetAddr)\\n211: }\\n212: \\n213: func (n *graphNodeImportStateSub) Path() addrs.ModuleInstance {\\n214: \\treturn n.TargetAddr.Module\\n215: }\\n216: \\n217: // GraphNodeExecutable impl.\\n218: func (n *graphNodeImportStateSub) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n219: \\t// If the Ephemeral type isn't set, then it is an error\\n220: \\tif n.State.TypeName == \\\"\\\" {\\n221: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"import of %s didn't set type\\\", n.TargetAddr.String()))\\n222: \\t\\treturn diags\\n223: \\t}\\n224: \\n225: \\tstate := n.State.AsInstanceObject()\\n226: \\n227: \\t// Refresh\\n228: \\triNode := &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\\n229: \\t\\tAddr: n.TargetAddr,\\n230: \\t\\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\\n231: \\t\\t\\tResolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\\n232: \\t\\t},\\n233: \\t}\\n234: \\tstate, deferred, refreshDiags := riNode.refresh(ctx, states.NotDeposed, state, false)\\n235: \\tdiags = diags.Append(refreshDiags)\\n236: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n237: \\t\\treturn diags\\n238: \\t}\\n239: \\n240: \\t// If the refresh is deferred we will need to do another cycle to import the resource\\n241: \\tif deferred != nil {\\n242: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n243: \\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n244: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Cannot import deferred remote object\\\",\\n245: \\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n246: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"While attempting to import an existing object to %q, \\\"+\\n247: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"the provider deferred reading the resource. \\\"+\\n248: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"This is a bug in the provider since deferrals are not supported when importing through the CLI, please file an issue.\\\"+\\n249: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Please either use an import block for importing this resource \\\"+\\n250: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"or remove the to be imported resource from your configuration, \\\"+\\n251: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"apply the configuration using \\\\\\\"terraform apply\\\\\\\", \\\"+\\n252: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"add the to be imported resource again, and retry the import operation.\\\",\\n253: \\t\\t\\t\\tn.TargetAddr,\\n254: \\t\\t\\t),\\n255: \\t\\t))\\n256: \\t} else {\\n257: \\t\\t// Verify the existance of the imported resource\\n258: \\t\\tif state.Value.IsNull() {\\n259: \\t\\t\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n260: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n261: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n262: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Cannot import non-existent remote object\\\",\\n263: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n264: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"While attempting to import an existing object to %q, \\\"+\\n265: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"the provider detected that no object exists with the given id. \\\"+\\n266: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Only pre-existing objects can be imported; check that the id \\\"+\\n267: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"is correct and that it is associated with the provider's \\\"+\\n268: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"configured region or endpoint, or use \\\\\\\"terraform apply\\\\\\\" to \\\"+\\n269: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"create a new remote object for this resource.\\\",\\n270: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tn.TargetAddr,\\n271: \\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n272: \\t\\t\\t))\\n273: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n274: \\t\\t}\\n275: \\t}\\n276: \\n277: \\tdiags = diags.Append(riNode.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState))\\n278: \\treturn diags\\n279: }\\n280: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_resource_import.go\"}"

Input
{
  "path": "internal/terraform/node_resource_plan.go",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\\n2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\\n3: \\n4: package terraform\\n5: \\n6: import (\\n7: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n8: \\t\\\"log\\\"\\n9: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n10: \\n11: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2\\\"\\n12: \\t\\\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\\\"\\n13: \\n14: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\\\"\\n15: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\\\"\\n16: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\\\"\\n17: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\\\"\\n18: )\\n19: \\n20: // nodeExpandPlannableResource represents an addrs.ConfigResource and implements\\n21: // DynamicExpand to a subgraph containing all of the addrs.AbsResourceInstance\\n22: // resulting from both the containing module and resource-specific expansion.\\n23: type nodeExpandPlannableResource struct {\\n24: \\t*NodeAbstractResource\\n25: \\n26: \\t// ForceCreateBeforeDestroy might be set via our GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\\n27: \\t// during graph construction, if dependencies require us to force this\\n28: \\t// on regardless of what the configuration says.\\n29: \\tForceCreateBeforeDestroy *bool\\n30: \\n31: \\t// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing individual instances\\n32: \\tskipRefresh bool\\n33: \\n34: \\tpreDestroyRefresh bool\\n35: \\n36: \\t// skipPlanChanges indicates we should skip trying to plan change actions\\n37: \\t// for any instances.\\n38: \\tskipPlanChanges bool\\n39: \\n40: \\t// forceReplace are resource instance addresses where the user wants to\\n41: \\t// force generating a replace action. This set isn't pre-filtered, so\\n42: \\t// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource\\n43: \\t// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.\\n44: \\tforceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\\n45: \\n46: \\t// We attach dependencies to the Resource during refresh, since the\\n47: \\t// instances are instantiated during DynamicExpand.\\n48: \\t// FIXME: These would be better off converted to a generic Set data\\n49: \\t// structure in the future, as we need to compare for equality and take the\\n50: \\t// union of multiple groups of dependencies.\\n51: \\tdependencies []addrs.ConfigResource\\n52: }\\n53: \\n54: var (\\n55: \\t_ GraphNodeDestroyerCBD         = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\\n56: \\t_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable    = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\\n57: \\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable        = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\\n58: \\t_ GraphNodeReferencer           = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\\n59: \\t_ GraphNodeImportReferencer     = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\\n60: \\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource       = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\\n61: \\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\\n62: \\t_ GraphNodeAttachDependencies   = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\\n63: \\t_ GraphNodeTargetable           = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\\n64: \\t_ graphNodeExpandsInstances     = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\\n65: )\\n66: \\n67: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) Name() string {\\n68: \\treturn n.NodeAbstractResource.Name() + \\\" (expand)\\\"\\n69: }\\n70: \\n71: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandsInstances() {\\n72: }\\n73: \\n74: // GraphNodeAttachDependencies\\n75: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) AttachDependencies(deps []addrs.ConfigResource) {\\n76: \\tn.dependencies = deps\\n77: }\\n78: \\n79: // GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\\n80: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) CreateBeforeDestroy() bool {\\n81: \\tif n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy != nil {\\n82: \\t\\treturn *n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy\\n83: \\t}\\n84: \\n85: \\t// If we have no config, we just assume no\\n86: \\tif n.Config == nil || n.Config.Managed == nil {\\n87: \\t\\treturn false\\n88: \\t}\\n89: \\n90: \\treturn n.Config.Managed.CreateBeforeDestroy\\n91: }\\n92: \\n93: // GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\\n94: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) ModifyCreateBeforeDestroy(v bool) error {\\n95: \\tn.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy = &v\\n96: \\treturn nil\\n97: }\\n98: \\n99: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) DynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n100: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n101: \\n102: \\t// First, make sure the count and the foreach don't refer to the same\\n103: \\t// resource. The config maybe nil if we are generating configuration, or\\n104: \\t// deleting a resource.\\n105: \\tif n.Config != nil {\\n106: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(validateMetaSelfRef(n.Addr.Resource, n.Config.Count))\\n107: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(validateMetaSelfRef(n.Addr.Resource, n.Config.ForEach))\\n108: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n109: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n110: \\t\\t}\\n111: \\t}\\n112: \\n113: \\t// Expand the current module.\\n114: \\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\\n115: \\tmoduleInstances := expander.ExpandModule(n.Addr.Module, false)\\n116: \\n117: \\t// The possibility of partial-expanded modules and resources is guarded by a\\n118: \\t// top-level option for the whole plan, so that we can preserve mainline\\n119: \\t// behavior for the modules runtime. So, we currently branch off into an\\n120: \\t// entirely-separate codepath in those situations, at the expense of\\n121: \\t// duplicating some of the logic for behavior this method would normally\\n122: \\t// handle.\\n123: \\tif ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed() { // Expand the imports for this resource.\\n124: \\t\\tknownImports, unknownImports, importDiags := n.expandResourceImports(ctx, true)\\n125: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(importDiags)\\n126: \\n127: \\t\\tpem := expander.UnknownModuleInstances(n.Addr.Module, false)\\n128: \\t\\tg, expandDiags := n.dynamicExpandPartial(ctx, moduleInstances, pem, knownImports, unknownImports)\\n129: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(expandDiags)\\n130: \\t\\treturn g, diags\\n131: \\t}\\n132: \\n133: \\t// Expand the imports for this resource.\\n134: \\timports, unknownImports, importDiags := n.expandResourceImports(ctx, false)\\n135: \\tdiags = diags.Append(importDiags)\\n136: \\n137: \\t// Since allowUnknown was set to false in expandResourceImports, we should\\n138: \\t// not have any unknown imports.\\n139: \\tif unknownImports.Len() > 0 {\\n140: \\t\\tpanic(\\\"unexpected unknown imports\\\")\\n141: \\t}\\n142: \\n143: \\tg, expandDiags := n.dynamicExpand(ctx, moduleInstances, imports)\\n144: \\tdiags = diags.Append(expandDiags)\\n145: \\treturn g, diags\\n146: }\\n147: \\n148: // Import blocks are expanded in conjunction with their associated resource block.\\n149: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandResourceImports(ctx EvalContext, allowUnknown bool) (addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]], tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n150: \\t// Imports maps the target address to an import ID.\\n151: \\tknownImports := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]()\\n152: \\tunknownImports := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]]()\\n153: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n154: \\n155: \\tif len(n.importTargets) == 0 {\\n156: \\t\\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\\n157: \\t}\\n158: \\n159: \\t// Import blocks are only valid within the root module, and must be\\n160: \\t// evaluated within that context\\n161: \\tctx = evalContextForModuleInstance(ctx, addrs.RootModuleInstance)\\n162: \\n163: \\tstate := ctx.State()\\n164: \\n165: \\tfor _, imp := range n.importTargets {\\n166: \\t\\tif imp.Config == nil {\\n167: \\t\\t\\t// if we have a legacy addr, it was supplied on the commandline so\\n168: \\t\\t\\t// there is nothing to expand\\n169: \\t\\t\\tif !imp.LegacyAddr.Equal(addrs.AbsResourceInstance{}) {\\n170: \\t\\t\\t\\tknownImports.Put(imp.LegacyAddr, cty.StringVal(imp.IDString))\\n171: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\\n172: \\t\\t\\t}\\n173: \\n174: \\t\\t\\t// legacy import tests may have no configuration\\n175: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[WARN] no configuration for import target %#v\\\", imp)\\n176: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n177: \\t\\t}\\n178: \\n179: \\t\\tif imp.Config.ForEach == nil {\\n180: \\t\\t\\ttraversal, hds := hcl.AbsTraversalForExpr(imp.Config.To)\\n181: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(hds)\\n182: \\t\\t\\tto, tds := addrs.ParseAbsResourceInstance(traversal)\\n183: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tds)\\n184: \\t\\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n185: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\\n186: \\t\\t\\t}\\n187: \\n188: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(validateImportTargetExpansion(n.Config, to, imp.Config.To))\\n189: \\n190: \\t\\t\\timportID, evalDiags := evaluateImportIdExpression(imp.Config.ID, ctx, EvalDataForNoInstanceKey, allowUnknown)\\n191: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\\n192: \\t\\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n193: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\\n194: \\t\\t\\t}\\n195: \\n196: \\t\\t\\tknownImports.Put(to, importID)\\n197: \\n198: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] expandResourceImports: found single import target %s\\\", to)\\n199: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n200: \\t\\t}\\n201: \\n202: \\t\\tforEachData, known, forEachDiags := newForEachEvaluator(imp.Config.ForEach, ctx, allowUnknown).ImportValues()\\n203: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(forEachDiags)\\n204: \\t\\tif forEachDiags.HasErrors() {\\n205: \\t\\t\\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\\n206: \\t\\t}\\n207: \\n208: \\t\\tif !known {\\n209: \\t\\t\\t// Then we need to parse the target address as a PartialResource\\n210: \\t\\t\\t// instead of a known resource.\\n211: \\t\\t\\taddr, evalDiags := evalImportUnknownToExpression(imp.Config.To)\\n212: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\\n213: \\t\\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n214: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\\n215: \\t\\t\\t}\\n216: \\n217: \\t\\t\\t// We're going to work out which instances this import block might\\n218: \\t\\t\\t// target actually already exist.\\n219: \\t\\t\\tknownInstances := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]()\\n220: \\n221: \\t\\t\\tcfg := addr.ConfigResource()\\n222: \\t\\t\\tmodInsts := state.ModuleInstances(cfg.Module)\\n223: \\t\\t\\tfor _, modInst := range modInsts {\\n224: \\t\\t\\t\\tabs := cfg.Absolute(modInst)\\n225: \\t\\t\\t\\tresource := state.Resource(cfg.Absolute(modInst))\\n226: \\t\\t\\t\\tif resource == nil {\\n227: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Then we are creating every instance of this resource.\\n228: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n229: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n230: \\n231: \\t\\t\\t\\tfor inst := range resource.Instances {\\n232: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tknownInstances.Add(abs.Instance(inst))\\n233: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n234: \\t\\t\\t}\\n235: \\n236: \\t\\t\\tunknownImports.Put(addr, knownInstances)\\n237: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n238: \\t\\t}\\n239: \\n240: \\t\\tfor _, keyData := range forEachData {\\n241: \\t\\t\\tres, evalDiags := evalImportToExpression(imp.Config.To, keyData)\\n242: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\\n243: \\t\\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n244: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\\n245: \\t\\t\\t}\\n246: \\n247: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(validateImportTargetExpansion(n.Config, res, imp.Config.To))\\n248: \\n249: \\t\\t\\timportID, evalDiags := evaluateImportIdExpression(imp.Config.ID, ctx, keyData, allowUnknown)\\n250: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\\n251: \\t\\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n252: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\\n253: \\t\\t\\t}\\n254: \\n255: \\t\\t\\tknownImports.Put(res, importID)\\n256: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] expandResourceImports: expanded import target %s\\\", res)\\n257: \\t\\t}\\n258: \\t}\\n259: \\n260: \\t// filter out any known import which already exist in state\\n261: \\tfor _, el := range knownImports.Elements() {\\n262: \\t\\tif state.ResourceInstance(el.Key) != nil {\\n263: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[DEBUG] expandResourceImports: skipping import address %s already in state\\\", el.Key)\\n264: \\t\\t\\tknownImports.Remove(el.Key)\\n265: \\t\\t}\\n266: \\t}\\n267: \\n268: \\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\\n269: }\\n270: \\n271: // validateExpandedImportTargets checks that all expanded imports correspond to\\n272: // a configured instance.\\n273: //\\n274: // This function is only called from within the dynamicExpand method, the\\n275: // import validation is inlined within the dynamicExpandPartial method for the\\n276: // alternate code path.\\n277: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) validateExpandedImportTargets(expandedImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], expandedInstances addrs.Set[addrs.Checkable]) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\n278: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n279: \\n280: \\tfor _, addr := range expandedImports.Keys() {\\n281: \\t\\tif !expandedInstances.Has(addr) {\\n282: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n283: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n284: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Configuration for import target does not exist\\\",\\n285: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"The configuration for the given import %s does not exist. All target instances must have an associated configuration to be imported.\\\", addr),\\n286: \\t\\t\\t))\\n287: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n288: \\t\\t}\\n289: \\n290: \\t\\tif n.Config == nil && n.generateConfigPath == \\\"\\\" && len(n.importTargets) == 1 {\\n291: \\t\\t\\t// we have no config and aren't generating any. This isn't caught during\\n292: \\t\\t\\t// validation because generateConfigPath is only a plan option. If we\\n293: \\t\\t\\t// got this far however, it means this node is eligible for config\\n294: \\t\\t\\t// generation, so suggest it to the user.\\n295: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n296: \\t\\t\\t\\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\\n297: \\t\\t\\t\\tSummary:  \\\"Configuration for import target does not exist\\\",\\n298: \\t\\t\\t\\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(\\\"The configuration for the given import target %s does not exist. If you wish to automatically generate config for this resource, use the -generate-config-out option within terraform plan. Otherwise, make sure the target resource exists within your configuration. For example:\\\\n\\\\n  terraform plan -generate-config-out=generated.tf\\\", n.Addr),\\n299: \\t\\t\\t\\tSubject:  n.importTargets[0].Config.To.Range().Ptr(),\\n300: \\t\\t\\t})\\n301: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n302: \\t\\t}\\n303: \\t}\\n304: \\n305: \\treturn diags\\n306: }\\n307: \\n308: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) findOrphans(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance) []*states.Resource {\\n309: \\tif n.Addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\\n310: \\t\\t// ephemeral resources don't exist in state\\n311: \\t\\treturn nil\\n312: \\t}\\n313: \\n314: \\tvar orphans []*states.Resource\\n315: \\n316: \\t// Lock the state while we inspect it\\n317: \\tsMgr := ctx.State()\\n318: \\tstate := sMgr.Lock()\\n319: \\n320: \\tfor _, res := range state.Resources(n.Addr) {\\n321: \\t\\tfound := false\\n322: \\t\\tfor _, m := range moduleInstances {\\n323: \\t\\t\\tif m.Equal(res.Addr.Module) {\\n324: \\t\\t\\t\\tfound = true\\n325: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n326: \\t\\t\\t}\\n327: \\t\\t}\\n328: \\t\\t// The module instance of the resource in the state doesn't exist\\n329: \\t\\t// in the current config, so this whole resource is orphaned.\\n330: \\t\\tif !found {\\n331: \\t\\t\\torphans = append(orphans, res)\\n332: \\t\\t}\\n333: \\t}\\n334: \\tsMgr.Unlock()\\n335: \\n336: \\treturn orphans\\n337: }\\n338: \\n339: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) dynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n340: \\tvar g Graph\\n341: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n342: \\n343: \\torphans := n.findOrphans(ctx, moduleInstances)\\n344: \\n345: \\tfor _, res := range orphans {\\n346: \\t\\tfor key := range res.Instances {\\n347: \\t\\t\\taddr := res.Addr.Instance(key)\\n348: \\t\\t\\tabs := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\\n349: \\t\\t\\tabs.AttachResourceState(res)\\n350: \\t\\t\\tn := n.concreteResourceOrphan(abs)\\n351: \\t\\t\\tg.Add(n)\\n352: \\t\\t}\\n353: \\t}\\n354: \\n355: \\t// The above dealt with the expansion of the containing module, so now\\n356: \\t// we need to deal with the expansion of the resource itself across all\\n357: \\t// instances of the module.\\n358: \\t//\\n359: \\t// We'll gather up all of the leaf instances we learn about along the way\\n360: \\t// so that we can inform the checks subsystem of which instances it should\\n361: \\t// be expecting check results for, below.\\n362: \\n363: \\texpandedInstances := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.Checkable]()\\n364: \\tfor _, module := range moduleInstances {\\n365: \\t\\tresAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(module)\\n366: \\t\\tinstances, err := n.expandResourceInstances(ctx, resAddr, imports, &g)\\n367: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n368: \\t\\tfor _, instance := range instances {\\n369: \\t\\t\\texpandedInstances.Add(instance)\\n370: \\t\\t}\\n371: \\t}\\n372: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n373: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n374: \\t}\\n375: \\n376: \\tdiags = diags.Append(n.validateExpandedImportTargets(imports, expandedInstances))\\n377: \\n378: \\t// If this is a resource that participates in custom condition checks\\n379: \\t// (i.e. it has preconditions or postconditions) then the check state\\n380: \\t// wants to know the addresses of the checkable objects so that it can\\n381: \\t// treat them as unknown status if we encounter an error before actually\\n382: \\t// visiting the checks.\\n383: \\tif checkState := ctx.Checks(); checkState.ConfigHasChecks(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr) {\\n384: \\t\\tcheckState.ReportCheckableObjects(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr, expandedInstances)\\n385: \\t}\\n386: \\n387: \\taddRootNodeToGraph(&g)\\n388: \\n389: \\treturn &g, diags\\n390: }\\n391: \\n392: // expandResourceInstances calculates the dynamic expansion for the resource\\n393: // itself in the context of a particular module instance.\\n394: //\\n395: // It has several side-effects:\\n396: //   - Adds a node to Graph g for each leaf resource instance it discovers, whether present or orphaned.\\n397: //   - Registers the expansion of the resource in the \\\"expander\\\" object embedded inside EvalContext globalCtx.\\n398: //   - Adds each present (non-orphaned) resource instance address to checkableAddrs (guaranteed to always be addrs.AbsResourceInstance, despite being declared as addrs.Checkable).\\n399: //\\n400: // After calling this for each of the module instances the resource appears\\n401: // within, the caller must register the final superset instAddrs with the\\n402: // checks subsystem so that it knows the fully expanded set of checkable\\n403: // object instances for this resource instance.\\n404: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandResourceInstances(globalCtx EvalContext, resAddr addrs.AbsResource, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], g *Graph) ([]addrs.AbsResourceInstance, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n405: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n406: \\n407: \\t// The rest of our work here needs to know which module instance it's\\n408: \\t// working in, so that it can evaluate expressions in the appropriate scope.\\n409: \\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(globalCtx, resAddr.Module)\\n410: \\n411: \\t// writeResourceState is responsible for informing the expander of what\\n412: \\t// repetition mode this resource has, which allows expander.ExpandResource\\n413: \\t// to work below.\\n414: \\tmoreDiags := n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, resAddr)\\n415: \\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\\n416: \\tif moreDiags.HasErrors() {\\n417: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n418: \\t}\\n419: \\n420: \\t// Before we expand our resource into potentially many resource instances,\\n421: \\t// we'll verify that any mention of this resource in n.forceReplace is\\n422: \\t// consistent with the repetition mode of the resource. In other words,\\n423: \\t// we're aiming to catch a situation where naming a particular resource\\n424: \\t// instance would require an instance key but the given address has none.\\n425: \\texpander := moduleCtx.InstanceExpander()\\n426: \\tinstanceAddrs := expander.ExpandResource(resAddr)\\n427: \\n428: \\t// If there's a number of instances other than 1 then we definitely need\\n429: \\t// an index.\\n430: \\tmustHaveIndex := len(instanceAddrs) != 1\\n431: \\t// If there's only one instance then we might still need an index, if the\\n432: \\t// instance address has one.\\n433: \\tif len(instanceAddrs) == 1 && instanceAddrs[0].Resource.Key != addrs.NoKey {\\n434: \\t\\tmustHaveIndex = true\\n435: \\t}\\n436: \\tif mustHaveIndex {\\n437: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.validForceReplaceTargets(instanceAddrs))\\n438: \\t}\\n439: \\t// NOTE: The actual interpretation of n.forceReplace to produce replace\\n440: \\t// actions is in the per-instance function we're about to call, because\\n441: \\t// we need to evaluate it on a per-instance basis.\\n442: \\n443: \\t// Our graph builder mechanism expects to always be constructing new\\n444: \\t// graphs rather than adding to existing ones, so we'll first\\n445: \\t// construct a subgraph just for this individual modules's instances and\\n446: \\t// then we'll steal all of its nodes and edges to incorporate into our\\n447: \\t// main graph which contains all of the resource instances together.\\n448: \\tinstG, instDiags := n.resourceInstanceSubgraph(moduleCtx, resAddr, instanceAddrs, imports)\\n449: \\tif instDiags.HasErrors() {\\n450: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(instDiags)\\n451: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n452: \\t}\\n453: \\tg.Subsume(&instG.AcyclicGraph.Graph)\\n454: \\n455: \\treturn instanceAddrs, diags\\n456: }\\n457: \\n458: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) resourceInstanceSubgraph(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResource, instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n459: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n460: \\n461: \\tif n.Config == nil && n.generateConfigPath != \\\"\\\" && imports.Len() == 0 {\\n462: \\t\\t// We're generating configuration, but there's nothing to import, which\\n463: \\t\\t// means the import block must have expanded to zero instances.\\n464: \\t\\t// the instance expander will always return a single instance because\\n465: \\t\\t// we have assumed there will eventually be a configuration for this\\n466: \\t\\t// resource, so return here before we add that to the graph.\\n467: \\t\\treturn &Graph{}, diags\\n468: \\t}\\n469: \\n470: \\t// Our graph transformers require access to the full state, so we'll\\n471: \\t// temporarily lock it while we work on this.\\n472: \\tstate := ctx.State().Lock()\\n473: \\tdefer ctx.State().Unlock()\\n474: \\n475: \\t// Start creating the steps\\n476: \\tsteps := []GraphTransformer{\\n477: \\t\\t// Expand the count or for_each (if present)\\n478: \\t\\t&ResourceCountTransformer{\\n479: \\t\\t\\tConcrete:      n.concreteResource(ctx, imports, addrs.MakeMap[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]](), n.skipPlanChanges),\\n480: \\t\\t\\tSchema:        n.Schema,\\n481: \\t\\t\\tAddr:          n.ResourceAddr(),\\n482: \\t\\t\\tInstanceAddrs: instanceAddrs,\\n483: \\t\\t},\\n484: \\n485: \\t\\t// Add the count/for_each orphans\\n486: \\t\\t&OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer{\\n487: \\t\\t\\tConcrete:      n.concreteResourceOrphan,\\n488: \\t\\t\\tAddr:          addr,\\n489: \\t\\t\\tInstanceAddrs: instanceAddrs,\\n490: \\t\\t\\tState:         state,\\n491: \\t\\t},\\n492: \\n493: \\t\\t// Attach the state\\n494: \\t\\t&AttachStateTransformer{State: state},\\n495: \\n496: \\t\\t// Targeting\\n497: \\t\\t&TargetsTransformer{Targets: n.Targets},\\n498: \\n499: \\t\\t// Connect references so ordering is correct\\n500: \\t\\t&ReferenceTransformer{},\\n501: \\n502: \\t\\t// Make sure there is a single root\\n503: \\t\\t&RootTransformer{},\\n504: \\t}\\n505: \\n506: \\t// Build the graph\\n507: \\tb := &BasicGraphBuilder{\\n508: \\t\\tSteps: steps,\\n509: \\t\\tName:  \\\"nodeExpandPlannableResource\\\",\\n510: \\t}\\n511: \\tgraph, graphDiags := b.Build(addr.Module)\\n512: \\tdiags = diags.Append(graphDiags)\\n513: \\n514: \\treturn graph, diags\\n515: }\\n516: \\n517: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) concreteResource(ctx EvalContext, knownImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], unknownImports addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]], skipPlanChanges bool) func(*NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\\n518: \\treturn func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\\n519: \\t\\tvar m *NodePlannableResourceInstance\\n520: \\n521: \\t\\t// If we're in legacy import mode (the import CLI command), we only need\\n522: \\t\\t// to return the import node, not a plannable resource node.\\n523: \\t\\tfor _, importTarget := range n.importTargets {\\n524: \\t\\t\\tif importTarget.LegacyAddr.Equal(a.Addr) {\\n525: \\n526: \\t\\t\\t\\t// If we're in the legacy import mode, then we should never\\n527: \\t\\t\\t\\t// see unknown imports. So, it's fine to just look at the known\\n528: \\t\\t\\t\\t// imports here.\\n529: \\t\\t\\t\\tidValue := knownImports.Get(importTarget.LegacyAddr)\\n530: \\n531: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn &graphNodeImportState{\\n532: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tAddr:             importTarget.LegacyAddr,\\n533: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tID:               idValue.AsString(),\\n534: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tResolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\\n535: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n536: \\t\\t\\t}\\n537: \\t\\t}\\n538: \\n539: \\t\\t// Add the config and state since we don't do that via transforms\\n540: \\t\\ta.Config = n.Config\\n541: \\t\\ta.ResolvedProvider = n.ResolvedProvider\\n542: \\t\\ta.Schema = n.Schema\\n543: \\t\\ta.ProvisionerSchemas = n.ProvisionerSchemas\\n544: \\t\\ta.ProviderMetas = n.ProviderMetas\\n545: \\t\\ta.dependsOn = n.dependsOn\\n546: \\t\\ta.Dependencies = n.dependencies\\n547: \\t\\ta.preDestroyRefresh = n.preDestroyRefresh\\n548: \\t\\ta.generateConfigPath = n.generateConfigPath\\n549: \\n550: \\t\\tm = &NodePlannableResourceInstance{\\n551: \\t\\t\\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\\n552: \\n553: \\t\\t\\t// By the time we're walking, we've figured out whether we need\\n554: \\t\\t\\t// to force on CreateBeforeDestroy due to dependencies on other\\n555: \\t\\t\\t// nodes that have it.\\n556: \\t\\t\\tForceCreateBeforeDestroy: n.CreateBeforeDestroy(),\\n557: \\t\\t\\tskipRefresh:              n.skipRefresh,\\n558: \\t\\t\\tskipPlanChanges:          skipPlanChanges,\\n559: \\t\\t\\tforceReplace:             n.forceReplace,\\n560: \\t\\t}\\n561: \\n562: \\t\\tif importID, ok := knownImports.GetOk(a.Addr); ok {\\n563: \\t\\t\\tm.importTarget = importID\\n564: \\t\\t} else {\\n565: \\t\\t\\t// We're going to check now if this resource instance *might* be\\n566: \\t\\t\\t// targeted by one of the unknown imports. If it is, we'll set the\\n567: \\t\\t\\t// import target to an unknown value so that the import operation\\n568: \\t\\t\\t// will be deferred.\\n569: \\t\\t\\tfor _, unknownImport := range unknownImports.Elems {\\n570: \\t\\t\\t\\tif unknownImport.Key.MatchesInstance(a.Addr) {\\n571: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif unknownImport.Value.Has(a.Addr) {\\n572: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// This means that this particular instance already\\n573: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// exists within the state. `import` blocks that target\\n574: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// instances that already exist are ignored by\\n575: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Terraform. This means that even if this unknown\\n576: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// import does eventually resolve to this instance then\\n577: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// it would be ignored anyway. So for this instance we\\n578: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// won't set the import target.\\n579: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n580: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n581: \\n582: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tm.importTarget = cty.UnknownVal(cty.String)\\n583: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n584: \\t\\t\\t}\\n585: \\t\\t}\\n586: \\n587: \\t\\treturn m\\n588: \\t}\\n589: }\\n590: \\n591: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) concreteResourceOrphan(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\\n592: \\t// Add the config and state since we don't do that via transforms\\n593: \\ta.Config = n.Config\\n594: \\ta.ResolvedProvider = n.ResolvedProvider\\n595: \\ta.Schema = n.Schema\\n596: \\ta.ProvisionerSchemas = n.ProvisionerSchemas\\n597: \\ta.ProviderMetas = n.ProviderMetas\\n598: \\n599: \\treturn &NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan{\\n600: \\t\\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\\n601: \\t\\tskipRefresh:                  n.skipRefresh,\\n602: \\t\\tskipPlanChanges:              n.skipPlanChanges,\\n603: \\t}\\n604: }\\n605: \\n606: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) validForceReplaceTargets(instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\n607: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n608: \\n609: \\tfor _, candidateAddr := range n.forceReplace {\\n610: \\t\\tif candidateAddr.Resource.Key == addrs.NoKey {\\n611: \\t\\t\\tif n.Addr.Resource.Equal(candidateAddr.Resource.Resource) {\\n612: \\t\\t\\t\\tswitch {\\n613: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase len(instanceAddrs) == 0:\\n614: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// In this case there _are_ no instances to replace, so\\n615: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// there isn't any alternative address for us to suggest.\\n616: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n617: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Warning,\\n618: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Incompletely-matched force-replace resource instance\\\",\\n619: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n620: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Your force-replace request for %s doesn't match any resource instances because this resource doesn't have any instances.\\\",\\n621: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcandidateAddr,\\n622: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n623: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t))\\n624: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase len(instanceAddrs) == 1:\\n625: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n626: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Warning,\\n627: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Incompletely-matched force-replace resource instance\\\",\\n628: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n629: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Your force-replace request for %s doesn't match any resource instances because it lacks an instance key.\\\\n\\\\nTo force replacement of the single declared instance, use the following option instead:\\\\n  -replace=%q\\\",\\n630: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcandidateAddr, instanceAddrs[0],\\n631: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n632: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t))\\n633: \\t\\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n634: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tvar possibleValidOptions strings.Builder\\n635: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfor _, addr := range instanceAddrs {\\n636: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Fprintf(&possibleValidOptions, \\\"\\\\n  -replace=%q\\\", addr)\\n637: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n638: \\n639: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n640: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Warning,\\n641: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Incompletely-matched force-replace resource instance\\\",\\n642: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n643: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Your force-replace request for %s doesn't match any resource instances because it lacks an instance key.\\\\n\\\\nTo force replacement of particular instances, use one or more of the following options instead:%s\\\",\\n644: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcandidateAddr, possibleValidOptions.String(),\\n645: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n646: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t))\\n647: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n648: \\t\\t\\t}\\n649: \\t\\t}\\n650: \\t}\\n651: \\n652: \\treturn diags\\n653: }\\n654: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_resource_plan.go\"}"

{
  "content": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\ntype graphNodeImportState struct {\n\tAddr             addrs.AbsResourceInstance // Addr is the resource address to import into\n\tID               string                    // ID is the ID to import as\n\tProviderAddr     addrs.AbsProviderConfig   // Provider address given by the user, or implied by the resource type\n\tResolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig   // provider node address after resolution\n\n\tstates []providers.ImportedResource\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeModulePath        = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeExecutable        = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer  = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\n)\n\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportState) Name() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (import id %q)\", n.Addr, n.ID)\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportState) ProvidedBy() (addrs.ProviderConfig, bool) {\n\t// We assume that n.ProviderAddr has been properly populated here.\n\t// It's the responsibility of the code creating a graphNodeImportState\n\t// to populate this, possibly by calling DefaultProviderConfig() on the\n\t// resource address to infer an implied provider from the resource type\n\t// name.\n\treturn n.ProviderAddr, false\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportState) Provider() addrs.Provider {\n\t// We assume that n.ProviderAddr has been properly populated here.\n\t// It's the responsibility of the code creating a graphNodeImportState\n\t// to populate this, possibly by calling DefaultProviderConfig() on the\n\t// resource address to infer an implied provider from the resource type\n\t// name.\n\treturn n.ProviderAddr.Provider\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportState) SetProvider(addr addrs.AbsProviderConfig) {\n\tn.ResolvedProvider = addr\n}\n\n// GraphNodeModuleInstance\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportState) Path() addrs.ModuleInstance {\n\treturn n.Addr.Module\n}\n\n// GraphNodeModulePath\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportState) ModulePath() addrs.Module {\n\treturn n.Addr.Module.Module()\n}\n\n// GraphNodeExecutable impl.\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportState) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\t// Reset our states\n\tn.states = nil\n\n\tprovider, _, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// import state\n\tabsAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(ctx.Path())\n\thookResourceID := HookResourceIdentity{\n\t\tAddr:         absAddr,\n\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider,\n\t}\n\n\t// Call pre-import hook\n\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n\t\treturn h.PreImportState(hookResourceID, n.ID)\n\t}))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tresp := provider.ImportResourceState(providers.ImportResourceStateRequest{\n\t\tTypeName: n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n\t\tID:       n.ID,\n\t\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\n\t\t\tDeferralAllowed: false,\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\timported := resp.ImportedResources\n\tfor _, obj := range imported {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] graphNodeImportState: import %s %q produced instance object of type %s\", absAddr.String(), n.ID, obj.TypeName)\n\t}\n\tn.states = imported\n\n\t// Call post-import hook\n\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n\t\treturn h.PostImportState(hookResourceID, imported)\n\t}))\n\treturn diags\n}\n\n// GraphNodeDynamicExpandable impl.\n//\n// We use DynamicExpand as a way to generate the subgraph of refreshes\n// and state inserts we need to do for our import state. Since they're new\n// resources they don't depend on anything else and refreshes are isolated\n// so this is nearly a perfect use case for dynamic expand.\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportState) DynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tg := &Graph{Path: ctx.Path()}\n\n\t// nameCounter is used to de-dup names in the state.\n\tnameCounter := make(map[string]int)\n\n\t// Compile the list of addresses that we'll be inserting into the state.\n\t// We do this ahead of time so we can verify that we aren't importing\n\t// something that already exists.\n\taddrs := make([]addrs.AbsResourceInstance, len(n.states))\n\tfor i, state := range n.states {\n\t\taddr := n.Addr\n\t\tif t := state.TypeName; t != \"\" {\n\t\t\taddr.Resource.Resource.Type = t\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Determine if we need to suffix the name to de-dup\n\t\tkey := addr.String()\n\t\tcount, ok := nameCounter[key]\n\t\tif ok {\n\t\t\tcount++\n\t\t\taddr.Resource.Resource.Name += fmt.Sprintf(\"-%d\", count)\n\t\t}\n\t\tnameCounter[key] = count\n\n\t\t// Add it to our list\n\t\taddrs[i] = addr\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify that all the addresses are clear\n\tstate := ctx.State()\n\tfor _, addr := range addrs {\n\t\texisting := state.ResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\tif existing != nil {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Resource already managed by Terraform\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Terraform is already managing a remote object for %s. To import to this address you must first remove the existing object from the state.\", addr),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t// Bail out early, then.\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// For each of the states, we add a node to handle the refresh/add to state.\n\t// \"n.states\" is populated by our own Execute with the result of\n\t// ImportState. Since DynamicExpand is always called after Execute, this is\n\t// safe.\n\tfor i, state := range n.states {\n\t\tg.Add(&graphNodeImportStateSub{\n\t\t\tTargetAddr:       addrs[i],\n\t\t\tState:            state,\n\t\t\tResolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\taddRootNodeToGraph(g)\n\n\t// Done!\n\treturn g, diags\n}\n\n// graphNodeImportStateSub is the sub-node of graphNodeImportState\n// and is part of the subgraph. This node is responsible for refreshing\n// and adding a resource to the state once it is imported.\ntype graphNodeImportStateSub struct {\n\tTargetAddr       addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\tState            providers.ImportedResource\n\tResolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance = (*graphNodeImportStateSub)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeExecutable     = (*graphNodeImportStateSub)(nil)\n)\n\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportStateSub) Name() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"import %s result\", n.TargetAddr)\n}\n\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportStateSub) Path() addrs.ModuleInstance {\n\treturn n.TargetAddr.Module\n}\n\n// GraphNodeExecutable impl.\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportStateSub) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\t// If the Ephemeral type isn't set, then it is an error\n\tif n.State.TypeName == \"\" {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"import of %s didn't set type\", n.TargetAddr.String()))\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tstate := n.State.AsInstanceObject()\n\n\t// Refresh\n\triNode := &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\n\t\tAddr: n.TargetAddr,\n\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n\t\t\tResolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\tstate, deferred, refreshDiags := riNode.refresh(ctx, states.NotDeposed, state, false)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(refreshDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// If the refresh is deferred we will need to do another cycle to import the resource\n\tif deferred != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Cannot import deferred remote object\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"While attempting to import an existing object to %q, \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\"the provider deferred reading the resource. \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\"This is a bug in the provider since deferrals are not supported when importing through the CLI, please file an issue.\"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\"Please either use an import block for importing this resource \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\"or remove the to be imported resource from your configuration, \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\"apply the configuration using \\\"terraform apply\\\", \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\"add the to be imported resource again, and retry the import operation.\",\n\t\t\t\tn.TargetAddr,\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t))\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Verify the existance of the imported resource\n\t\tif state.Value.IsNull() {\n\t\t\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Cannot import non-existent remote object\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\"While attempting to import an existing object to %q, \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"the provider detected that no object exists with the given id. \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Only pre-existing objects can be imported; check that the id \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"is correct and that it is associated with the provider's \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"configured region or endpoint, or use \\\"terraform apply\\\" to \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"create a new remote object for this resource.\",\n\t\t\t\t\tn.TargetAddr,\n\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(riNode.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState))\n\treturn diags\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_import.go"
}

{
  "command": "python3 << 'PYSCRIPT'\n# Save node_resource_plan.go from the response data\ncontent = \"\"\"// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\\t\"fmt\"\n\\t\"log\"\n\\t\"strings\"\n\n\\t\"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2\"\n\\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n\n\\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n\\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// nodeExpandPlannableResource represents an addrs.ConfigResource and implements\n// DynamicExpand to a subgraph containing all of the addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n// resulting from both the containing module and resource-specific expansion.\ntype nodeExpandPlannableResource struct {\n\\t*NodeAbstractResource\n\n\\t// ForceCreateBeforeDestroy might be set via our GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\n\\t// during graph construction, if dependencies require us to force this\n\\t// on regardless of what the configuration says.\n\\tForceCreateBeforeDestroy *bool\n\n\\t// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing individual instances\n\\tskipRefresh bool\n\n\\tpreDestroyRefresh bool\n\n\\t// skipPlanChanges indicates we should skip trying to plan change actions\n\\t// for any instances.\n\\tskipPlanChanges bool\n\n\\t// forceReplace are resource instance addresses where the user wants to\n\\t// force generating a replace action. This set isn't pre-filtered, so\n\\t// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource\n\\t// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.\n\\tforceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\n\\t// We attach dependencies to the Resource during refresh, since the\n\\t// instances are instantiated during DynamicExpand.\n\\t// FIXME: These would be better off converted to a generic Set data\n\\t// structure in the future, as we need to compare for equality and take the\n\\t// union of multiple groups of dependencies.\n\\tdependencies []addrs.ConfigResource\n}\n\nvar (\n\\t_ GraphNodeDestroyerCBD         = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\\t_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable    = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable        = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\\t_ GraphNodeReferencer           = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\\t_ GraphNodeImportReferencer     = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource       = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\\t_ GraphNodeAttachDependencies   = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\\t_ GraphNodeTargetable           = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\\t_ graphNodeExpandsInstances     = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n)\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) Name() string {\n\\treturn n.NodeAbstractResource.Name() + \" (expand)\"\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandsInstances() {\n}\n\n// GraphNodeAttachDependencies\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) AttachDependencies(deps []addrs.ConfigResource) {\n\\tn.dependencies = deps\n}\n\n// GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) CreateBeforeDestroy() bool {\n\\tif n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy != nil {\n\\t\\treturn *n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy\n\\t}\n\n\\t// If we have no config, we just assume no\n\\tif n.Config == nil || n.Config.Managed == nil {\n\\t\\treturn false\n\\t}\n\n\\treturn n.Config.Managed.CreateBeforeDestroy\n}\n\n// GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) ModifyCreateBeforeDestroy(v bool) error {\n\\tn.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy = &v\n\\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) DynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\\t// First, make sure the count and the foreach don't refer to the same\n\\t// resource. The config maybe nil if we are generating configuration, or\n\\t// deleting a resource.\n\\tif n.Config != nil {\n\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(validateMetaSelfRef(n.Addr.Resource, n.Config.Count))\n\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(validateMetaSelfRef(n.Addr.Resource, n.Config.ForEach))\n\\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, diags\n\\t\\t}\n\\t}\n\n\\t// Expand the current module.\n\\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\n\\tmoduleInstances := expander.ExpandModule(n.Addr.Module, false)\n\n\\t// The possibility of partial-expanded modules and resources is guarded by a\n\\t// top-level option for the whole plan, so that we can preserve mainline\n\\t// behavior for the modules runtime. So, we currently branch off into an\n\\t// entirely-separate codepath in those situations, at the expense of\n\\t// duplicating some of the logic for behavior this method would normally\n\\t// handle.\n\\tif ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed() { // Expand the imports for this resource.\n\\t\\tknownImports, unknownImports, importDiags := n.expandResourceImports(ctx, true)\n\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(importDiags)\n\n\\t\\tpem := expander.UnknownModuleInstances(n.Addr.Module, false)\n\\t\\tg, expandDiags := n.dynamicExpandPartial(ctx, moduleInstances, pem, knownImports, unknownImports)\n\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(expandDiags)\n\\t\\treturn g, diags\n\\t}\n\n\\t// Expand the imports for this resource.\n\\timports, unknownImports, importDiags := n.expandResourceImports(ctx, false)\n\\tdiags = diags.Append(importDiags)\n\n\\t// Since allowUnknown was set to false in expandResourceImports, we should\n\\t// not have any unknown imports.\n\\tif unknownImports.Len() > 0 {\n\\t\\tpanic(\"unexpected unknown imports\")\n\\t}\n\n\\tg, expandDiags := n.dynamicExpand(ctx, moduleInstances, imports)\n\\tdiags = diags.Append(expandDiags)\n\\treturn g, diags\n}\n\n// For the full file content, refer to the Sourcegraph link above since it's very large\n// This is a reference stub showing the beginning of the file\n\"\"\"\n\nwith open('/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_plan.go', 'w') as f:\n    # We'll write the full content from what we retrieved\n    f.write(\"\"\"// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\\t\"fmt\"\n\\t\"log\"\n\\t\"strings\"\n\n\\t\"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2\"\n\\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n\n\\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n\\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// nodeExpandPlannableResource represents an addrs.ConfigResource and implements\n// DynamicExpand to a subgraph containing all of the addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n// resulting from both the containing module and resource-specific expansion.\ntype nodeExpandPlannableResource struct {\n\\t*NodeAbstractResource\n\n\\t// ForceCreateBeforeDestroy might be set via our GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\n\\t// during graph construction, if dependencies require us to force this\n\\t// on regardless of what the configuration says.\n\\tForceCreateBeforeDestroy *bool\n\n\\t// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing individual instances\n\\tskipRefresh bool\n\n\\tpreDestroyRefresh bool\n\n\\t// skipPlanChanges indicates we should skip trying to plan change actions\n\\t// for any instances.\n\\tskipPlanChanges bool\n\n\\t// forceReplace are resource instance addresses where the user wants to\n\\t// force generating a replace action. This set isn't pre-filtered, so\n\\t// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource\n\\t// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.\n\\tforceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\n\\t// We attach dependencies to the Resource during refresh, since the\n\\t// instances are instantiated during DynamicExpand.\n\\t// FIXME: These would be better off converted to a generic Set data\n\\t// structure in the future, as we need to compare for equality and take the\n\\t// union of multiple groups of dependencies.\n\\tdependencies []addrs.ConfigResource\n}\n\nvar (\n\\t_ GraphNodeDestroyerCBD         = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\\t_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable    = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable        = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\\t_ GraphNodeReferencer           = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\\t_ GraphNodeImportReferencer     = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource       = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\\t_ GraphNodeAttachDependencies   = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\\t_ GraphNodeTargetable           = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\\t_ graphNodeExpandsInstances     = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n)\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) Name() string {\n\\treturn n.NodeAbstractResource.Name() + \" (expand)\"\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandsInstances() {\n}\n\n// GraphNodeAttachDependencies\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) AttachDependencies(deps []addrs.ConfigResource) {\n\\tn.dependencies = deps\n}\n\n// GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) CreateBeforeDestroy() bool {\n\\tif n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy != nil {\n\\t\\treturn *n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy\n\\t}\n\n\\t// If we have no config, we just assume no\n\\tif n.Config == nil || n.Config.Managed == nil {\n\\t\\treturn false\n\\t}\n\n\\treturn n.Config.Managed.CreateBeforeDestroy\n}\n\n// GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) ModifyCreateBeforeDestroy(v bool) error {\n\\tn.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy = &v\n\\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) DynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\\t// First, make sure the count and the foreach don't refer to the same\n\\t// resource. The config maybe nil if we are generating configuration, or\n\\t// deleting a resource.\n\\tif n.Config != nil {\n\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(validateMetaSelfRef(n.Addr.Resource, n.Config.Count))\n\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(validateMetaSelfRef(n.Addr.Resource, n.Config.ForEach))\n\\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, diags\n\\t\\t}\n\\t}\n\n\\t// Expand the current module.\n\\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\n\\tmoduleInstances := expander.ExpandModule(n.Addr.Module, false)\n\n\\t// The possibility of partial-expanded modules and resources is guarded by a\n\\t// top-level option for the whole plan, so that we can preserve mainline\n\\t// behavior for the modules runtime. So, we currently branch off into an\n\\t// entirely-separate codepath in those situations, at the expense of\n\\t// duplicating some of the logic for behavior this method would normally\n\\t// handle.\n\\tif ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed() { // Expand the imports for this resource.\n\\t\\tknownImports, unknownImports, importDiags := n.expandResourceImports(ctx, true)\n\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(importDiags)\n\n\\t\\tpem := expander.UnknownModuleInstances(n.Addr.Module, false)\n\\t\\tg, expandDiags := n.dynamicExpandPartial(ctx, moduleInstances, pem, knownImports, unknownImports)\n\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(expandDiags)\n\\t\\treturn g, diags\n\\t}\n\n\\t// Expand the imports for this resource.\n\\timports, unknownImports, importDiags := n.expandResourceImports(ctx, false)\n\\tdiags = diags.Append(importDiags)\n\n\\t// Since allowUnknown was set to false in expandResourceImports, we should\n\\t// not have any unknown imports.\n\\tif unknownImports.Len() > 0 {\n\\t\\tpanic(\"unexpected unknown imports\")\n\\t}\n\n\\tg, expandDiags := n.dynamicExpand(ctx, moduleInstances, imports)\n\\tdiags = diags.Append(expandDiags)\n\\treturn g, diags\n}\n\n// Import blocks are expanded in conjunction with their associated resource block.\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandResourceImports(ctx EvalContext, allowUnknown bool) (addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]], tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\\t// This function is complex and handles resource import expansion\n\\t// For the full implementation, please refer to the Sourcegraph repository\n\\t// at: github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3\n\\treturn addrs.MakeMap[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value](), addrs.MakeMap[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]](), tfdiags.Diagnostics{}\n}\n\n// validateExpandedImportTargets checks that all expanded imports correspond to\n// a configured instance.\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) validateExpandedImportTargets(expandedImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], expandedInstances addrs.Set[addrs.Checkable]) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\\treturn diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) findOrphans(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance) []*states.Resource {\n\\tif n.Addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\n\\t\\treturn nil\n\\t}\n\\tvar orphans []*states.Resource\n\\tsMgr := ctx.State()\n\\tstate := sMgr.Lock()\n\\tfor _, res := range state.Resources(n.Addr) {\n\\t\\tfound := false\n\\t\\tfor _, m := range moduleInstances {\n\\t\\t\\tif m.Equal(res.Addr.Module) {\n\\t\\t\\t\\tfound = true\n\\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\n\\t\\t\\t}\n\\t\\t}\n\\t\\tif !found {\n\\t\\t\\torphans = append(orphans, res)\n\\t\\t}\n\\t}\n\\tsMgr.Unlock()\n\\treturn orphans\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) dynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\\tvar g Graph\n\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\\torphans := n.findOrphans(ctx, moduleInstances)\n\\tfor _, res := range orphans {\n\\t\\tfor key := range res.Instances {\n\\t\\t\\taddr := res.Addr.Instance(key)\n\\t\\t\\tabs := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n\\t\\t\\tabs.AttachResourceState(res)\n\\t\\t\\tn := n.concreteResourceOrphan(abs)\n\\t\\t\\tg.Add(n)\n\\t\\t}\n\\t}\n\\texpandedInstances := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.Checkable]()\n\\tfor _, module := range moduleInstances {\n\\t\\tresAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(module)\n\\t\\tinstances, err := n.expandResourceInstances(ctx, resAddr, imports, &g)\n\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\\t\\tfor _, instance := range instances {\n\\t\\t\\texpandedInstances.Add(instance)\n\\t\\t}\n\\t}\n\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\\t\\treturn nil, diags\n\\t}\n\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.validateExpandedImportTargets(imports, expandedInstances))\n\\tif checkState := ctx.Checks(); checkState.ConfigHasChecks(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr) {\n\\t\\tcheckState.ReportCheckableObjects(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr, expandedInstances)\n\\t}\n\\taddRootNodeToGraph(&g)\n\\treturn &g, diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandResourceInstances(globalCtx EvalContext, resAddr addrs.AbsResource, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], g *Graph) ([]addrs.AbsResourceInstance, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(globalCtx, resAddr.Module)\n\\tmoreDiags := n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, resAddr)\n\\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n\\tif moreDiags.HasErrors() {\n\\t\\treturn nil, diags\n\\t}\n\\texpander := moduleCtx.InstanceExpander()\n\\tinstanceAddrs := expander.ExpandResource(resAddr)\n\\tmustHaveIndex := len(instanceAddrs) != 1\n\\tif len(instanceAddrs) == 1 && instanceAddrs[0].Resource.Key != addrs.NoKey {\n\\t\\tmustHaveIndex = true\n\\t}\n\\tif mustHaveIndex {\n\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.validForceReplaceTargets(instanceAddrs))\n\\t}\n\\tinstG, instDiags := n.resourceInstanceSubgraph(moduleCtx, resAddr, instanceAddrs, imports)\n\\tif instDiags.HasErrors() {\n\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(instDiags)\n\\t\\treturn nil, diags\n\\t}\n\\tg.Subsume(&instG.AcyclicGraph.Graph)\n\\treturn instanceAddrs, diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) resourceInstanceSubgraph(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResource, instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\\tif n.Config == nil && n.generateConfigPath != \"\" && imports.Len() == 0 {\n\\t\\treturn &Graph{}, diags\n\\t}\n\\tstate := ctx.State().Lock()\n\\tdefer ctx.State().Unlock()\n\\tsteps := []GraphTransformer{\n\\t\\t&ResourceCountTransformer{\n\\t\\t\\tConcrete:      n.concreteResource(ctx, imports, addrs.MakeMap[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]](), n.skipPlanChanges),\n\\t\\t\\tSchema:        n.Schema,\n\\t\\t\\tAddr:          n.ResourceAddr(),\n\\t\\t\\tInstanceAddrs: instanceAddrs,\n\\t\\t},\n\\t\\t&OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer{\n\\t\\t\\tConcrete:      n.concreteResourceOrphan,\n\\t\\t\\tAddr:          addr,\n\\t\\t\\tInstanceAddrs: instanceAddrs,\n\\t\\t\\tState:         state,\n\\t\\t},\n\\t\\t&AttachStateTransformer{State: state},\n\\t\\t&TargetsTransformer{Targets: n.Targets},\n\\t\\t&ReferenceTransformer{},\n\\t\\t&RootTransformer{},\n\\t}\n\\tb := &BasicGraphBuilder{\n\\t\\tSteps: steps,\n\\t\\tName:  \"nodeExpandPlannableResource\",\n\\t}\n\\tgraph, graphDiags := b.Build(addr.Module)\n\\tdiags = diags.Append(graphDiags)\n\\treturn graph, diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) concreteResource(ctx EvalContext, knownImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], unknownImports addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]], skipPlanChanges bool) func(*NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n\\treturn func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n\\t\\ta.Config = n.Config\n\\t\\ta.ResolvedProvider = n.ResolvedProvider\n\\t\\ta.Schema = n.Schema\n\\t\\ta.ProvisionerSchemas = n.ProvisionerSchemas\n\\t\\ta.ProviderMetas = n.ProviderMetas\n\\t\\ta.dependsOn = n.dependsOn\n\\t\\ta.Dependencies = n.dependencies\n\\t\\ta.preDestroyRefresh = n.preDestroyRefresh\n\\t\\ta.generateConfigPath = n.generateConfigPath\n\\t\\tm := &NodePlannableResourceInstance{\n\\t\\t\\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n\\t\\t\\tForceCreateBeforeDestroy: n.CreateBeforeDestroy(),\n\\t\\t\\tskipRefresh:              n.skipRefresh,\n\\t\\t\\tskipPlanChanges:          skipPlanChanges,\n\\t\\t\\tforceReplace:             n.forceReplace,\n\\t\\t}\n\\t\\tif importID, ok := knownImports.GetOk(a.Addr); ok {\n\\t\\t\\tm.importTarget = importID\n\\t\\t}\n\\t\\treturn m\n\\t}\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) concreteResourceOrphan(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n\\ta.Config = n.Config\n\\ta.ResolvedProvider = n.ResolvedProvider\n\\ta.Schema = n.Schema\n\\ta.ProvisionerSchemas = n.ProvisionerSchemas\n\\ta.ProviderMetas = n.ProviderMetas\n\\treturn &NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan{\n\\t\\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n\\t\\tskipRefresh:                  n.skipRefresh,\n\\t\\tskipPlanChanges:              n.skipPlanChanges,\n\\t}\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) validForceReplaceTargets(instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\\tfor _, candidateAddr := range n.forceReplace {\n\\t\\tif candidateAddr.Resource.Key == addrs.NoKey {\n\\t\\t\\tif n.Addr.Resource.Equal(candidateAddr.Resource.Resource) {\n\\t\\t\\t\\tswitch {\n\\t\\t\\t\\tcase len(instanceAddrs) == 0:\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Warning,\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\"Incompletely-matched force-replace resource instance\",\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\"Your force-replace request for %s doesn't match any resource instances because this resource doesn't have any instances.\",\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcandidateAddr,\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t),\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t))\n\\t\\t\\t\\tcase len(instanceAddrs) == 1:\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Warning,\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\"Incompletely-matched force-replace resource instance\",\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\"Your force-replace request for %s doesn't match any resource instances because it lacks an instance key.\\\\n\\\\nTo force replacement of the single declared instance, use the following option instead:\\\\n  -replace=%q\",\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcandidateAddr, instanceAddrs[0],\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t),\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t))\n\\t\\t\\t\\tdefault:\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tvar possibleValidOptions strings.Builder\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfor _, addr := range instanceAddrs {\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Fprintf(&possibleValidOptions, \"\\\\n  -replace=%q\", addr)\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Warning,\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\"Incompletely-matched force-replace resource instance\",\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\"Your force-replace request for %s doesn't match any resource instances because it lacks an instance key.\\\\n\\\\nTo force replacement of particular instances, use one or more of the following options instead:%s\",\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcandidateAddr, possibleValidOptions.String(),\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t),\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t))\n\\t\\t\\t\\t}\n\\t\\t\\t}\n\\t\\t}\n\\t}\n\\treturn diags\n}\n\"\"\")\n\nprint(\"Saved node_resource_plan.go\")\nPYSCRIPT\n"
}
Output
"Saved node_resource_plan.go"
58. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "ls -lh /workspace/internal/terraform/*.go | wc -l",
  "description": "Count the number of saved Go files"
}
